subject: R*E: $Parkimon books
body:
Paul Camera
FYI, this email is blind copied to some investors to spare their inboxes being inundated with your responses that I am hoping will be forthcoming because everything you say going forward is going to be held against you in a court of law. You are of course free to copy everyone

Please see my response inline in red within your email (black)

subject: Re: $Parkimon books
body:
Asad-

You are the sole executive officer of the company and sole board member. All financial aspects of the company are your sole legal responsibility---as well as your fiduciary resonsibily---

Paul, ignorance and being oblivious to reality keep you in a constant state of bliss and has become your hallmark. Unfortunately, ignorance of the law is not an excuse. I have never denied my fiduciary duty and the fact that this conversation is taking place 8 months since the company became insolvent is a testimony to that fact. Now let’s look at YOUR fiduciary duty, responsibility and obligations – (that you neglected because of your hidden agenda).

I have taken the liberty of copy-pasting the legal definition of a fiduciary. Hopefully it will settle this rhetoric.

“All who assent to act on behalf of another person or entity are common-law agents as defined in §1.01 and are subject to the general fiduciary principle stated in this section. Thus, the fiduciary principle is applicable to gratuitous agents as well as to agents who expect compensation for their services, and to employees as well as to nonemployee professionals, intermediaries, and others who act as agents. An agent is a “party that has express (oral or written) or implied authority to act for another.”

Looks like you unquestionably qualify as an “agent”.
(A) You expected and received compensation for your services.
(B) You also had authority to act for Parkimon.

By the above definition alone, you have a continuing fiduciary duty to Parkimon. Let me also remind you:

1. You have written yourself checks that show the word “salary” on them.
2. You have represented yourself to hundreds of people and also online as VP Business Development.
3. You had pgc@parkimon.com email address and I have all the emails that you sent and received related to Parkimon including Match.com where you represented yourself as an employee/director and even co-founder of Parkimon.
4. You had access to the checkbooks and the Parkimon bank account
5. You wrote and signed Parkimon checks…

I believe the above goes beyond “implied” don’t you?

Even if you were not an agent, you still have a moral and ethical obligation to your friends and family, but for some reason, you keep dodging that issue. Why? I’ll get to the why later – read on.

given that you brought it up. Thomas is no different than your engineers or attorneys. His time and services need to be paid for and respected.

I find your second last sentence coupled with the use of the term “paid for and respected” rather amusing, because the engineers AND the attorneys DIDN’T get paid and neither did I. So, why the double standards or should I say discrimination? BTW, regarding Thomas’s “services”; I received $23K in the last 26 months and thanks to Thomas’s brilliant accounting and tax expertise had to pay $23,749.68 to the IRS! Where do you find these gems Paul? Like seriously where? Laxmi, Lura, Jim, Raj, Michael Callana, Edward Strickland…. Every person you have brought to the table, like literally every one of them cost the company time and money and didn’t deliver anything. (Goes back to the WHY- read on..)

Additionally, he has lent the company $15,000 and you have YET to memorialize in a note. You have a duty to maintain and close out the books of this company. I suggest you find a way to do so. You have the bank statements and hold a PhD. You can figure it out... I’ve finally figured out a lot more…

As for the numbers, the only funds I received in 2019 was a $3,000 reimbursement for expenses. Period. I am not an employee nor have I ever been, and I am not a board member.

I have several checks in my possession that show you paid yourself $10K as salary and a few made out to Savvy Commercial too. (Does Savvy Commercial ring any bells?) You admit to receiving $3000 as reimbursement for expenses. (which BTW, you were explicitly told NOT to take out as that amount was the last of our reserves – yet you paid no heed and dipped into the account at a critical juncture. You also took out $10,201.66 on 5/1/19 (< 19 is a part of 2019 in case you are confused) but you did not bother addressing that nor letting Thomas know. Why?

If you chose, as you threaten, to close down the company, the debts of the company will be paid through the liquidation of the IP as I am sure you were apprised when you spoke with Aremenino & McKenna.

P-

------------

Paul, my attorney and I, after going through all our correspondence, conversations, timelines, events and your actions; have come to the conclusion that there is preponderance of evidence to show that every action that you have taken was with the objective of sabotaging the company to where it would dissolve and you would then have the opportunity to sell the IP and make a bundle all by yourself. Together we have gone over 4700 emails, phone call recordings, text messages, etc. and my attorney and I truly believe that I am the moron and you are a super genius. Allow me to reiterate. All your actions, communication (verbal and written) and the timing coincide in such a way as to point to you having an ulterior motive /hidden agenda to profit from the company being dissolved and you (A) selling the IP and/or (B) taking over the company to dissolve it yourself down the line and sell the IP.

You alone are a witness to the following as it relates to my IP:
1. My location and positioning technology is more accurate than that of Google Maps.
2. My License plate recognition accuracy is far superior than that of Siemens.
3. I have built not one, but 4 platforms that can be spun out and sold separately. (Parkimon, Speedchalk, Orangecone and Stratosvue)

That said, following are the causes of action against you: Intentional breach of fiduciary duty which include, constructive fraud, tortious interference, negligence, gross negligence, interference with prospective economic advantage, fraud and fraud in the inducement, unjust enrichment, breach of contract, negligent misrepresentation to me AND investors, putting your interest first, profiting, conflicts of interest, misappropriating funds, and 11 more.. A few of your friends are also guilty of aiding and abetting the breach of fiduciary duty and complicit in several ways. They will be named as co-defendants.

Background:

1. The seed of acquiring the IP for yourself was planted in your brain when your NY friend told you how much money he had made in selling IP and patents of dissolved companies. That said, following are a few of your actions and timing that point to that ulterior motive.

2. You demanded that I assign my payments and location IP that cost me over $3.75M over to Parkimon for just $100.

3. You made these demands first by misrepresenting to me that Parkimon “was not going to fail because of lack of funds” ( a statement that you repeated several times in various emails and also verbally, which was witnessed by a number of people including Shamlia, my engineers in Dubai, and the investors that attended the last meeting in Pleasant Hill. (I have the complete recording of that if you wish to review it).

4. Secondly, by misrepresenting the fact that unless I do that, future investment would not be forthcoming as it would not give a potential investor the comfort level to invest. (I don’t think most of the investors even know that I assigned millions of dollars of my IP over to Parkimon)

5. After Abu Dhabi, once you were informed that the technology had been fine-tuned, tried and tested and is now ready to go to market, you

6. Started drip feeding funds and then emptying the bank account just-in-time so that the engineers would not get paid. (I have 13 instances of us going insolvent in ONE year alone – the timing of our account running into zero is very interesting)

7. You sabotaged the Bin Dasmal Group potential partnership by forcing me to ask them for money although I repeatedly told you in various emails AND phone calls (all recorded) that they would pull out if I did. You threated “no more funds” and needless to say, they did pull out when I asked them for funds- just like I had predicted.

8. Although you posed as the VP business Development, you stopped following up on purpose with BART, Palo Alto, Barnacle, Oakland… just a few of the opportunities that came our way.

9. Even Parking Sense, in which you have a personal stake and who you repeatedly told me were interested in integrating our tech, were not followed up – on purpose.

10. I have mentioned this many times and my attorney also agree that even a 5 year old twit selling lemonade KNOWS that he cannot do everything all by himself and needs a team (mommy and daddy) to buy lemonade and put up the stand. It is going to be hard for any jury to believe that a 59 year old, born in Silicon Valley, the heart of startups, thinks that ONE man alone should be able to code, sell, market, support, administer… and bring in revenues all by himself. Yet you foisted all of the above on me for Parkimon, Orangecone AND Stratosvue with the statement: “BRING ME REVENUES OR NO MORE FUNDS!”.

11. I had given the people that YOU had brought in the complete social media game plan of Parkwhiz for marketing Orangecone, yet you demanded that I go door-to-door in Santa Cruz and were not willing to fund the personnel, marketing and promotion of OC.

12. Your reluctance to fund and build a team to sell all the platforms I had built, and which were ready to go to market are further evidence of you wanting the company to fail.

13. Sending me a proposal and contract to resign and hand over the company to you and your friends. Telling me that I need to step down as the investors have lost faith in me…. (I don’t know what you have been feeding these investors but to my point # 10 above, I find that strange coming from grownups who can easily see that I delivered on the technology. (Not just one but 4)

14. 7 instances (one where I have two investor witnesses) where I gave you the opportunity to set things right and you said to shut down the company instead.

15. You had no reservations of parting with your equity and offering it to Raj Kohli – knowing full well that you would be getting your hands on the IP at the end of the day and the equity would be worthless. (the emails between you both are in the attached presentation)

16. Your current actions that are delaying things and reluctance to fulfill the requirements laid out in the term sheet of Texpo also prove that you do not want the Texpo /Dubai deal to go through and validate my case.

17. Given your hidden agenda, you misrepresented to your investor friends too so you could generate enough funds for me to complete the technology, assign the IP, test it and then once you were sure that everything worked fine, try and sabotage the company to where it would have to be dissolved, thereby giving you the opportunity to sell off the IP.

Paul, I can go on and on as there is just so much evidence that point to your ulterior motives, but I am going to save that for the trial.

Moving on, I regret to inform you that I will be revoking the assignment of my IP to Parkimon as I was not even paid the $100 for it and I am legally in my right to do so. Besides, the IP was assigned based on your repeated misrepresentation that “this startup will not fail because of lack of funds”.

BTW, Paul, when you thought of this genius plan, did you even think it through? Turning on an iPhone makes you dizzy. Who would be demonstrating the IP to “liquidate” it? Where is the IP? – do you even know? What does IP stand for? Intellectual Paul, iPhone? What? Is our IP the patent application? Is it the code? Is it my encrypted algorithms? Is it the algos for light refraction and license plate recognition that beat Siemens and which are a trade secret (in my head) that we did not file a patent for? Is it the location accuracy that is better than that of Google? Who is going to technically evaluate whatever it is that you think it is, without me showing them where and what? Are you going to point the liquidator to our website and say “there it is, go get it”?? To whom and what are you going to sell Paul? Do you think someone will buy Parkimon’s IP without my algorithms and IP that are the actual core of the platform? Jesus!! you are pathetic!

You want my IP? Why don’t you pay me $25M for the time and effort (as that is what it would have cost to develop it here in the US) Add $10M for the opportunity cost AND lost and add another $100M for the trauma of being exposed to you and your BS, wasting my time and turning me into stupid.

I give you ONE opportunity that will get your friends some of their funds back and you are willing to screw that up too for your own vested interests. My job was to deliver the platform and the technology. I did! And not just 1 platform but 4! YOU and your friend’s seem to be in denial of that fact.

You have mentioned that the investors have lost faith in me. Who are they? Care to name them? They must be in on your plan then because even an idiot can see that I delivered on the technology- which was MY JOB. Even a buffoon living in the Silicon Valley will tell you that single startup founders are doomed to fail because a one-man-band cannot be expected to do everything. Nor do VCs entertain single founders. But then you never wanted us to go to VCs did you?? Your job was to take care of the finances so “this project would not fail because of lack of funds” Did you deliver on that Paul? You are the finance guy, just look at your signature. It says Savvy Commercial Capital, LLC and Pacific Private Money Fund. Focus on the two words I have highlighted and skip the “Savvy” as that is not your thing.

It is beyond my comprehension that an investor would swallow your BS and not want to see that the tech is there to experience for themselves PLUS expect a one-man-band to do everything AND give them a 100X ROI to boot?? You love to shirk your duties and apportion blame, don’t you? Yvette, David Waal, Michael Kraszulyac, Nahn Le, your sisters, her kids, your kids, everyone has done you wrong and now its me..

Besides, intentional breach of duty, tortious interference, constructive fraud, gross negligence, interference with prospective economic advantage, unjust enrichment, breach of contract, misrepresentation, etc. I have several personal causes of action against you and those who aided and abetted you, including mental anguish damages. I hope their homes are paid for. Going forward, I’ve had enough. You know what they say, What goes around comes around. Here is how this is going to play out.

1. You will get Thomas to complete Parkimon’s taxes and issue the 1099s.
2. You will pay off Wilson Sonsinis’ bill of $19K
3. You will do both of the above by Wednesday the 4th of March.

IF I have not heard from you by EOD Wednesday the 4th of March, I will file for dissolution the next day and send everyone the certificate of dissolution so they can claim whatever tax benefits they can. Most will not be able to as they invested through their IRA (Thanks to you). I will then take you and a couple of your friends who were involved to court – And THAT will be my public forum to let everyone know how a startup with state-of-the-art technology and a massive global market opportunity went under. A case study in greed and self-interest for new startup founders – A public service and Parkimon’s legacy!

Rest assured Paul, you will pay – if not Wilson Sonsini -a lot more – one way or the other… for messing up my daughters’ life, for wasting 4 years of my life! I rue the day I met you and you will too!

I wish everyone blind copied all the best as addressing Paul has turned my brain into mush and my communication skills to that of a frustrated and disappointed teenager.

Kind regards
-A

PS: please take time out to go through the attached “the chronicles of Master Paul Camera”

Co-manager
Pacific Private Money Fund I

• Real Estate Purchase & Equity Loans
• High-yield, Secured Financial Investments

Paul@pacificprivatemoney.com

800.605.8050 : Toll Free

subject: Asad Buyout
body:
Asad- 

The Board has agreed to accept your offer to buy out your shares in the company for $127,000 per your email to the investors dated 2/08/2021 and your follow-up discussion with our board member Joe Fosbinder last week. It is clear from a multitude of events, that you know longer are willing to cooperate with any us as it relates to moving Parkimon, Inc. in a forward direction for the benefit of all. This behavior has been illustrated to the investors many times over the past year, and most recently by your refusal to fully cooperate with James Cutler, despite his genuine interest, connections with existing industry players, and other bona fide credentials. If fact, to date, there has been no other entity as interested or qualified to guide the company as Mr. Cutler. You, as the sole Director, Board Member and CEO, resigned from all positions in Parkimon, Inc on June 12, 2020, effectively abandoning your fiduciary responsibilities without explanation, let the company fall into fallow, failed to raise any new capital nor provide a credible buyer for the company. We as owners and secured debt holders of the IP, in order to protect ourselves and the other investors, established an interim board after your resignation, and will now attempt to revitalize the company and or sell the company without your future involvement. We have identified three escrow companies and are working out a plan to protect the asset. We will present the three options and you may choose the company you are most comfortable. The buyout proposal will look much like any other company buyout which technology milestone to ensure the IP is what, in fact, the investors have paid for. We sincerely hope you will agree to part ways according to the buyout proposal, given all the time and money afforded you by the investors over the past several years. In the interim, Mr. Cutler has three questions which he needs answered in detail from you, which are now time-critical. Namely: 
• For any given license plate what mechanism are you using to accurately triangulate the position of the recorded number plate from the (accurately located) moving vehicle/ANPR camera. 
• Is the Parkimon’s architecture at the back-end all cloud based while the front-end dependent on the in-vehicle laptop and dongle while ARH (the ANPR hardware and software provider) that is not supported in the cloud? – Is so, when is there no congruence? 
• Also, it appears the Parkimon platform is doing lots of post processing of the ANPR data/imagery but it is not clear whether this replaces some parts of the ARH software (which would seem unnecessary) or is addition to it, and if it is additional what the code actually does. Please confirm 
We would hope you would provide this information ASAP in good faith on behalf of the investors as we work through the logistics of the buyout. We should be reaching back out to you sometime this week. 
Sincerely, 
Steve 
Steve Pinetti 
steve@pinettiway.com 
415 - 279 - 4319

subject: 1RE: Asad Buyout
body: I acknowledge your acceptance of my offer and invite a quick and easy transfer. Once I am no longer a shareholder, you all are free to do whatever you want. You will have full control of the company and can decide what you think is best for the company. Legally and procedurally the "interim board" is not valid, but that would be a moot point as soon as we complete the transaction.

The Board has agreed to accept your offer to buy out your shares in the company for $127,000 per your email to the investors dated 2/08/2021 and your follow-up discussion with our board member Joe Fosbinder last week. It is clear from a multitude of events, that you know longer are willing to cooperate with any us as it relates to moving Parkimon, Inc. in a forward direction for the benefit of all. This behavior has been illustrated to the investors many times over the past year, and most recently by your refusal to fully cooperate with James Cutler, despite his genuine interest, connections with existing industry players, and other bona fide credentials. If fact, to date, there has been no other entity as interested or qualified to guide the company as Mr. Cutler. You, as the sole Director, Board Member and CEO, resigned from all positions in Parkimon, Inc on June 12, 2020, effectively abandoning your fiduciary responsibilities without explanation, let the company fall into fallow, failed to raise any new capital nor provide a credible buyer for the company. We as owners and secured debt holders of the IP, in order to protect ourselves and the other investors, established an interim board after your resignation, and will now attempt to revitalize the company and or sell the company without your future involvement. We have identified three escrow companies and are working out a plan to protect the asset. We will present the three options and you may choose the company you are most comfortable. The buyout proposal will look much like any other company buyout which technology milestone to ensure the IP is what, in fact, the investors have paid for. We sincerely hope you will agree to part ways according to the buyout proposal, given all the time and money afforded you by the investors over the past several years. In the interim, Mr. Cutler has three questions which he needs answered in detail from you, which are now time-critical. Namely: 
• For any given license plate what mechanism are you using to accurately triangulate the position of the recorded number plate from the (accurately located) moving vehicle/ANPR camera. 
• Is the Parkimon’s architecture at the back-end all cloud based while the front-end dependent on the in-vehicle laptop and dongle while ARH (the ANPR hardware and software provider) that is not supported in the cloud? – Is so, when is there no congruence? 
• Also, it appears the Parkimon platform is doing lots of post processing of the ANPR data/imagery but it is not clear whether this replaces some parts of the ARH software (which would seem unnecessary) or is addition to it, and if it is additional what the code actually does. Please confirm 
We would hope you would provide this information ASAP in good faith on behalf of the investors as we work through the logistics of the buyout. We should be reaching back out to you sometime this week. 
Sincerely, 
Steve 
Steve Pinetti 
steve@pinettiway.com 
415 - 279 - 4319

subject: dissolution of parkimon
body: Paul, I’ve got nearly all the documentation together to file for dissolution in Delaware and California (as we were registered here as a foreign entity). I just need a couple of things, which I was told should be ready by next week. Appreciate if you can send me the physical addresses of the investors named below. I will be mailing the physical copy of the dissolution certificate to all of them to enable them to take a tax write off next year. I have everyone’s address from the C-notes except the following. You got what you always wanted, the company dissolving so you could get your hands on the IP. Not that it will do you any good. Anyway, thanks to your hidden agenda, the investors lost their money, now are you willing to extend their losses to where they wont be able to take a write-off too? Get with the program and let me know the addresses of the following investors: Thomas Chan Gary Rulli Michael Peri Russel Wixon A

subject: RE: Update
body: Steve, This is just another ploy by Paul and Sally to delay the inevitable. (I am sure you are going to do this anyway but please feel free to forward this email to them.) 1. I don't trust them as far as I can throw them. I say this from past experience with them. They tried this scam before with Edward Strickland and promised the moon AND that I would get paid half my salary immediately after the very first session with Edward. They then reneged on it. After numerous email exchanges in which Sally was also copied; he then had the balls to convince Sally to send me an email pretending to ask why I was not going forward with the sessions? Jesus!! I used to think he is inept but everything he does is premeditated and for his own self-interest. 2. Regarding the Texpo deal, all Paul had to do was get Thomas to do the Parkimon books and talk to James Huie and work out a payment plan with him. Did he? If he had done what he was supposed to do rather than bullshit you and the investors, the deal with Texpo would have been finalized by now. 3. Now he’s telling you he’s talking to Texpo’s Chairman? – Knowing him, I don’t doubt it. He certainly knows how to go behind people’s back. Must be all that training and learning that he got conspiring with Raj Kohli and Sally to screw me and the engineers. Since he is in touch with the Chairman, why don’t you ask him to find out if I was going to make even a penny from the deal? The amount Texpo was paying was the EXACT amount of the C-notes and yours and Jennifer Ross loan. Period. I was willing to write-off my technology that cost me millions and 3 years of sweat and blood just so the investors could get something out of it. 4. Have you thought of the fact that I did not have to bring Texpo to the table? I could have simply made a copy of the code and given it to Texpo through a friend in Dubai and put a million $$ in MY pocket. I can bet you that none of the investors would have ever known. 5. So please do not talk to me about fiduciary duty. I have gone beyond the call of duty. This company should have been dissolved 8 months ago when we became insolvent. IF Paul’s friends and investors believe his bullshit that a one-man-band should and could have given them a 100X ROI and screwed up because he was not able to wear 100 different caps and are biased and in-denial because of their friendship with Paul AND cannot see that Mr. VP Biz Dev was shooting hoops instead of business development --than those investors deserve their losses! 6. Everyone seems to conveniently forget I have a life too and need to put food on the table. Thanks to this asshole I am in debt to the tune of over a 100K in the last 3 years AND can’t face my daughter. (The following is for Pauls’ consumption): Do not threaten legal action- go for it! I welcome it. The more investors involved, the better. I’ll ADD them to the list of the 4 co-defendants when I sue his ass. I have the best litigator and will initiate legal proceedings against him as soon as the company is dissolved. He is going to pay! 7. Even if the investors win and I have to turn over the IP, I can guarantee you that no one in his right mind will touch the IP with a 100-foot pole for fear of the unknown code. This is a given in the software world. We were not flipping burgers where bad meat can simply be cut out and thrown away. This was not an App that we built but a platform to handle millions of transactions in real-time from thousands of municipalities at the same time! There are millions of lines of code and my algo’s are encrypted with random and dynamic algorithms which will require quantum computers and a mere billion years to crack. So, Polly good luck with getting access to the IP. Why don’t you come over to my place and I’ll gift wrap it for you myself! Seriously - You’ve always wanted the IP right? Come over and I swear to god I’ll give it to you. My word is my bond! Come! He jacks off when he’s supposed to be doing his job - even till a few days ago it was NOT his fiduciary duty to talk to James or Thomas and facilitate the sale and now HE wants to have a conversation immediately? Yes Massah! This brown boy is ready and at attention to do his massah’s bidding – just come over.. 8. He’s gone around telling every investor that I’m a bad businessman. What on earth did I do to get on his shit list? Ask him to find funding so we can build a team and market our platforms? Because I realized my limitations that I could not do everything myself? Was that so damn bad for the business? Did that logical request turn me into his worst enemy and persona non grata???? I have said enough and experienced enough. I will not waste my time with him or anyone for that matter anymore. If he has something on the table and believes in it then let him put his money where his mouth is. He hasn’t put in a dime by himself and took funds of over a 100K out of the investors’ money plus received equity and still gets others to foot his bill. He got Sally to pay for hiring Edward, AND pay for the patent application and his gas and lunch money. Let’s see. Pay my dues for the last 26 months upfront @ $5000/Mth and I will consider talking to Pied Piper or whoever. Otherwise this is just a waste of my time – again. I’ve already blown 3 years of my life and my daughter’s schooling based on his bullshit. He can sell piss to a padre as holy water and maybe if he had done his job of VP Biz dev we wouldn’t be in this situation. If he believes in Pied Piper or Texpo or whoever, then he should not have a problem raising the funds that are due to me for evaluating the “deals” he finds in his fantasy land OR if he truly believes he can sell the IP then let him form a syndicate and make me an offer. I’ll part with the IP amicably AND make sure it works brilliantly for whoever. I’ll be willing to do that on an earn-out and success-based milestones basis. Other than that, stop wasting my time for I have learnt that he is the pied piper who leads his friends to an abyss and then pushes them over! A PS: Regarding Orangecone, you may have misunderstood my email. First, I was not going to use Parkimon’s IP as it was not needed and second, it would have been under a different company with a different name in which I was going to invite you and the investors. Something that I dont have to do. I think I need to start looking out for myself for a change

subject: Update
body:
Asad- 
Paul and Sally have been in communication with the co-founder of a company called Piedparker. They have an interest in exploring the possible purchase or partnership with Parkimon. I am requesting that you cooperate with their request to connect with their CTO to explore these possibilities. They are prepared to have a conversation with you immediately. 

Additionally, Paul has been in communication with the Chairman of Texpo who remains an interested buyer and is willing to match any offer Parkimon receives, however, you must be willing, (for a fee) to train and or integrate the technology platform with a new buyer. 

Your cooperation will be mandatory for any sale of the Parkimon IP. Even if this is not the preferred pathway, having two interested parties will command a higher asking price. 

As you know, you have a fiduciary duty to do your best to protect your debtholders, of which I am the largest As we have concluded in dialogue with James, Parkimon and Orange Cone cannot be separated and funds from myself and the investors went to support both/all platforms. 

Based on legal advice I have received it seems you cannot dissolve the company without liquidating the technology, patents, and intellectual property to satisfy the debt holders. It is simply against the law. 

If, collectively, we find a suitable buyer, the investors will hire an attorney to facilitate the transaction. 
Please let me know that your willing to participate in the requested discussions with Pied Parker. 

Steve- 



Steve Pinetti 
Sent from my iPhone

subject: Re: recouping 300K proposal
body:
Asad
Got your message
Working w Paul and others to figure out what to do

back to you shortly
Steve Pinetti
Sent from my iPhone

Hi Steve, 
From the earlier email threads between Paul and I, you must have figured out that the Dubai deal is now no longer going through. We missed their deadline twice besides other issues.  
Given the situation, I will be dissolving Parkimon this week so that the investors can at least write off their investments next year.  
As discussed over the phone with you, I’d like to know if you are still up to what we talked about? If so, following are the points that I would like to put forth, which if acceptable, will give me the motivation to take up the development of OC2.0 at no cost to you or anyone else. If not I would still be grateful if you tell me as you know I don’t have to involve anyone in anything I do going forward.  it’s just that I feel terrible for the lack of oversight that should have been in place – kinda difficult when Paul had me working 18 hours a day on India time as we were not able to pay our engineers. Anyway, that’s water under the bridge.  

1. I’ll work on the concept of OC2.0 myself and complete it myself. Since I don’t have and cannot afford engineers, this may take a while. I’d say 4 to 5 months or more if I take time out. ( I have a job and will be working on OC2.0 after hours and the concept of OC2.0 is not an app but an enterprise class platform) 
2. I will not be using any of Parkimon’s IP as it is not needed for OC2.0 
3. I’ll incorporate the newco and offer you equity and a board seat. 
4. I would also like to invite Steve Roth, Mary Wong and John Ippolito on the board as they are some of the more sophisticated investors and together with you should be able to run the company much better than I could. 
5. You are free to hire anyone for the role of the CEO or even yourself as I will not be taking on that role but remain as a CTO. 
6. Once the platform is ready and tested, the company will invite all the investors who funded Parkimon to pitch in with just $3k as I estimate the social media marketing to be approximately $100K worth and we can CAP it at that number.  
7. The new company will also take over the debt of the c-notes that the investors put in for Parkimon and can issue them shares in return.  
8. Once the platform is ready it will only require social media marketing to build traction at which point we can then go to a VC and get an evaluation that will benefit the investors.  
9. VC’s prefer traction instead of revenues to figure out if a concept works and if it does, all it needs is funding to scale. We have precedents of huge funding of entire startups doing just one thing whereas we will be tapping into proven mega trends and aggregating them into one app with a better user interface and experience..Also in our case, traction automatically generates transaction volume and as such revenues as it’s a purpose built app. 
10. With the technology ready, a board and a CEO in place, I think each investor should be more conducive to putting in just $3K each to roll the dice again.  If not, well.. they lose the opportunity.  

That’s basically it in a nutshell. If you agree, I’ll get started and if you have any questions, please feel free to call me anytime.  
Kind regards 
Asad

subject: Asad Buyout
body:
Asad- 

The board's email to you on March 1st requesting you answer the three questions at the bottom of the correspondence for the benefit of Mr. Cutler was ignored. Non-disclosure agreements have been signed protecting the IP, so there is no reason for you not to comply. Claiming certain items to be "moot" is unacceptable and evasive. The questions being asked of you are anything but moot. 

Once these three questions are answered by you to the satisfaction of Mr. Cutler, we will provide you with a written update as to our plan and specifics as to what the milestones will be as to how/when you will be paid out. We are down to two escrow firms who we are considering to conduct the audit and will let you know who they are in our correspondence to you. Given the fact that we will be paying for the audit we will choose the escrow firm. Please respond to Mr. Cutler immediately as a sign of goodwill and faith. Should Mr. Cutler have any follow up questions to your responses we would expect you to respond in kind. 

It is critical to the buyout that you connect with and answer James questions so we can activate an escrow and start collecting the funds we would use for your first payment. We are now waiting on you. 

Thank You 
Steve

subject: Re: Parkimon Update
body: I hope my email finds you well. I am happy to report that we continue to forge ahead with James Cutler in the United Kingdom. Mr. Cutler has identified a number of real opportunities for us "across the pond." Unfortunately, our founder, Asad Shah, has not been willing to cooperate on the next major step so we can buy out Asad and continue moving forward with Mr Cutler. In order to have any viable chance of success with Mr. Cutler we must first verify the functionality of the software through a third-party software escrow company that will perform a full audit. This exercise is standard fare in the transfer or sale of intellectual property between individuals and companies and there is absolutely no reason Asad should not comply, as we have agreed to his requested buyout term as well as having signed his NDA In the last two purported sales opportunities our founder alleged he "wasn't getting anything out of the sale," and in this case he's getting $127,000 at the conclusion of the audit and yet still does not seem to be interested in cooperating. I will be sending out a document to each of you for e-signature asking for your support demanding Asad's compliance for the protection of our IP asset which we own per the "Intellectual Property Assignment Agreement" he signed back in 2017. With our ask being so obviously in the best interest of the company we would expect unanimous support on all sides. These are trying times but potentially exciting times, as well. We look forward to your continued support. Steve- Steve Pinetti 415 - 279 - 4319

Dear All- 

Steve Pinetti will be forwarding you an update and next steps. Thank you for your patience. 

Best- 

P-

subject: o!ur conversation re texpo
body: Paul, You have claimed the following: 1. That you contacted Texpo’s chairman and 2. that he is still interested in acquiring Parkimon’s technology and 3. is willing to match and exceed any offer Pied Parker makes. When I asked you to forward me the communication you had with him, you refused to do so. Makes me wonder what you are hiding? Steve asked me about the communication I had with Texpo and I sent it to him right after my conversation with him. Anyway, 4. I still gave you the benefit of the doubt and postponed the dissolution of Parkimon and investor update. 5. Although you have a history of conjuring up “sudden contacts” who seem to appear out of nowhere and then mysteriously disappear. Some examples: Transpo, Civicsmart, Edward Strickland, Pied Parker and more recently Payfone from NY (all of whom were going to call me but never did). 6. Last week you and I, together with Steve had a conference call in which it was unanimously decided (correct me if I am wrong) that since you are in touch with Texpo’s chairman, you will send him an email and inform him of an “offer” from PP to see if he matches it. I also asked you to BCC me when you do so. 7. Since I did not get any such email, I am assuming that you have not done so although in a subsequent email I again reminded you NOT to wait and send it out ASAP. 8. In another email after that I asked you again what your “ploy” was in regards to our discussion. 9. You have not bothered to tell me your plan other than telling me to “stay out of it”. I find that odd. There comes a time, when one thinks that enough is enough. I reached that point a long time ago with you but was willing to go forward even if there was a miniscule chance that something, anything would come out for the collective good of all and in the best interest of the company. Nothing has and its time. Here is what is going to happen. If I do not hear from you by the end of this day today (4/10/2020), I am going to reach out to the Chairman myself. I can assure you that he will be much more conducive to me (IF what you claimed is all true) as he knows that going forward, he will have to deal with me only for the hand-over of the tech. I will know when he opens his email and then wait for his response for a maximum of 2 days and if he has not responded, then I will add his name to the list of your grand delusions. I will then begin the proceedings of winding up the company. (which is probably what you always wanted). Steve, if you are privy to the master plan, then please let me know. If not, I would like to close this chapter once and for all. Best Asad

subject: ^RE: Civic Smart
body:
Steve, 
1. You obviously do not know what went on behind the scenes. How this man leveraged the names of Civicsmart, Transpo and others together with his girlfriend to try and benefit himself and his girlfriend only.  
2. You are also not aware of what some of the investors have written or called me quoting this man.  
3. You are not fully aware. Period.  And you are certainly not going to get the truth from him because he is a habitual and despicable liar. 
4. I delivered the technology for everyone to see and that too under extreme conditions. If people get the impression that my extreme rants are covering me for the situation we are in and not for what they truly are. That I loathe this man from the core of my very being, then it doesn’t say much for their intelligence now does it? Not that I care anymore.    

Easy for you to say that you understand or share. It’s not you who has lost time, money, friends, engineers, family respect. Understanding is a concept not an experience. So please lets not go there.  
You talk of a united front? How does that work in his case when he sends the only person with whom we might have a slight chance of getting something out of this a one liner saying  “this is all bullshit”. If it wasn’t for the extreme ego of the chairman of Texpo, (who actually couldn’t imagine that someone would say that to him -without any reason whatsoever) - I would never have been able to explain my way out of this “united front”. Only a convoluted mind such as his can think of things like that and I have a very good idea where his “ploys” are headed. He hasn’t spent a dime of his own money, taken from the company and is now trying to “prove” something to you all. I don’t have time for that and neither do I care what the investors think of me. IF and when Texpo happens, the investors will make their own decision depending upon their appetite for taking a loss. They are all adults and don’t have to be “driven away” by my immaturity or unprofessionalism. Like I said, I will be resigning once we get the term sheet and any one of the investors (who are all more credible and professional than me) can step in as the CEO and take this forward. I’ll of course help with the tech if the investors decide to go that route.  

I’ll be on the call with Civicsmart and you will be too. I’ll be more professional than you can imagine IF it goes through, but by the end of it, you will know that Civicsmart was another one of his fantasies.  
My attorney has asked me not to communicate with Paul. Best that he doesn’t. Let me know the time and date a day ahead please.  
You don’t need to respond to this.  
Stay safe. 
Best 

A

subject: Re: Civic Smart
body:
Paul - I will make myself available as well any time next week - just keep me posted.

Asad - I am personally asking you to please quit with the personal assault on Paul. For those of us investors who have been copied in the past in on your comments we find them disturbing, in bad form and rather unprofessional. These same people are some of those who you were hoping to and who have offered to invest in OC. You are driving them away.

More importantly - from me just to you - your rants are so extreme that they actually make you look like you are trying to cover yourself for why we are in the situation that we are in. At the beginning you both showed up in sync to the investors. Somewhere along the way you two went sideways. I know we talked about what we would have done if we had a do-over but we are where we are now. Please keep your comments about Paul between just you two.

We all understand and share your frustration but now is the time to come together and close a deal with someone who is aware and even seen the product demo.

Both of you need to think about how we are showing up to the people we are talking to. The stronger you two can present a united front the better chance we have of making a deal at a reasonable price.

We all need you two to come together one last time and present a unified front for this unique product that you Asad have created or we will miss our window

Thank you both
Steve

Steve Pinetti
spin4321@icloud.com
415 - 279 - 4319

I work for a living -  Something that you should try too.  
Give me a day’s heads up before you have another recurring fantasy. I recall that 
you’ve already had one with Balu/Civicsmart where he jerked you off twice before- just 
like so many others. 
Stop facilitating Paul – you’ve done enough “facilitation” to last me a life time. What 
am I going to show your potential acquirers??? Our awesome website 
?????????????????

subject: 3RE: ...RE: ..RE: waiting to hear from you all
body:
Let’s see how Texpo responds to my request for an extension. Quoting Paul “I’m not holding my breath” given how concerted we all are in our efforts to sell Texpo “air”. (quoting Alam) I’ll be reaching out to the investors and let them know of what is on the table once I hear back from Texpo. My thoughts about going back to those who showed interest in the past are based on common sense. All of them will want to see the platform in action. Going to them now and then telling them we have a wonderful platform but why don’t you hold your breath for a few months till we reactivate it again…. is something I’ll let Paul do as he’s a pro at building expectations.

1. Gtechna wanted it working AND installed in 6 municipalities before they would take it on. I shared their communication with Paul some time ago.
2. Paul dreamed up  https://www.reviver.com/, https://www.transpogroup.com/, https://www.piedparker.com/ and a few more…and lately,  https://www.civicsmart.com/  and we all know how that went. The first three I only heard about from Paul but never got to meet or communicate with any. You were in on the call with Civicsmart who has apparently contacted Paul a dozen times but now can’t find the time to sign off on an NDA.
3. Abu Dhabi DOT thinks we were out to screw them over to the tune of 4.2 Billion Dirhams a month – thanks to Paul’s friend Raj Kohli’s expertise in accounting, presentations  and proposal writing.
4. RTA Dubai- we will need to be incorporated there AND have infrastructure there to even bid locally.
5. Mike King – Parking Director – City of Sacramento – Showed interest, especially in our enforcement module – Could be revived especially given the Corona Virus situation but at the end of the day will want to see it in action – so back to square one.

I can list a few more, but knowing a little about software, I can assure you that no one is going to buy software “as is”. That’s a “deal” that can only consummate in Paul’s fantasy land.

Best
Asad

subject: Re: ...RE: ..RE: waiting to hear from you all
body:
Asad
For right now you just keep moving forward with Texpo as you see fit - it is our best and seemingly only option right now

If you want to reach out to the investors then move on that instinct so you are clear if that is a real option or not

what are your thought about going back to those who showed some past interest in the platform

I will start thinking of an exit strategy and share my thoughts with everyone in a few days

Steve Pinetti
Sent from my iPhone

Dear Investors

I wanted to make sure that we all understood that our goal right now is doing what is best for all of us. I want to make it clear that all communications about Paul’s 20% interest in the company was made by me as an incentive, reward and/or benefit for all of the investors who would put up the additional funds needed to complete the reactivation so that we can move forward with the Texpo offer. If I truly believed that we could accomplish that end without Paul’s 20%, I would not have brought it up. The goal is to find a solution that would benefit us all and I reiterate, not about grabbing Paul’s 20%, otherwise I would have been asking for it for myself. In fact if he has “the best interests of the investors” at heart – like he pretends, then HE should have come up with the idea and offered the incentive to all.

As of right now I think the Texpo offer is the best deal available. However, if you all collectively think there is another option available, I am all for it. I do not want to stand in the way of your thoughts on how to proceed. As I have said before, I am willing to step down as the CEO of the company if you all think that is best. I would do that immediately. In furtherance of that, I will consent to whatever plan you all have to move forward. Keep in mind though that the Texpo deal is on its last legs.

I suggest that you form an investors’ committee to plan an exit strategy for the company to follow. Lay out an implementation plan and I will do my part in executing it. I have always acted in the best interest of the company and you all. All I ask is that whatever plan of action you want to take, let’s act quickly. We cannot afford to drag this out any longer, nor do I think you want that. Let’s get it done.

Steve, I agree that the focus on Paul’s stock has us sidetracked. I am simply letting everybody know right now that whatever you all think is best, I will go along with it. I too, just want it done.
Best
A

subject: Re: ..RE: waiting to hear from you all
body:
Asad

My sole motivation is to get a deal done ASAP

As we discussed, your taking the lead with Texpo is not necessarily the best option but your insisting to see that deal through is in your hands.

Yes it would take a day or so to get Jeff up to speed but my experience in using people who’s singular purpose it is to do deals is that they approach negotiations from a different perspective than would people closer to the product itself such as you or I.

You are still holding out that there will be some found money to get the product functioning vs doing a deal with what we have in hand
After you have reached out to all the investors or have found new money / investor please let us know so we can support your efforts with Texpo or move on to other options.

What do we do with Civic Smart as there was at one point some clear logic and support why they would be a good candidate to purchase? If they are a logical candidate or even Abu Dhabi DOT or any other past potential buyers possibly we should get Jeff on their trail for one last shot at these folks.

It seems pretty clear to me that Pauls sole motivation is also to get a deal done ASAP. So with that in mind lets just start fresh here on this issue of Pauls Stock - its been referenced in too many different contexts since it first came up. Paul can you please clarify for this group under what conditions will you give up your shares to help close a deal? No matter who is talking to any prospective buyer we need to be crystal clear on this point.

Thank you all

Steve

Steve
I appreciate your efforts. I am not happy with Paul trying to negotiate new terms when we have a deal on the table already. I am trying to do the best deal possible for the benefit of all the investors, which includes you, and frankly am not concerned about what Paul wants. I get the feeling that he and his cohorts are trying to screw us by letting the Texpo deal die so that they can manipulate the outcome. At the end of the day and unfortunately for him, he would still have to come to me to get the tech back up. A 5 year old can get that but not him. He is beyond delusional. I trust (and hope) that your sole motivation is to get the best and fastest deal possible to recoup your investment at the very least. Clearly, Paul is not the person with which we can get this done. Since you have a dialog going with Paul, I believe it is in the best interest of all of us for you to convince him to surrender his shares so that we can get this done. Any accounting at the time of dissolution will show that Paul has individually profited more than anyone else up to this point. Heck he’s taken out more than what John invested and half of what you have and 5.3 times what most investors put in individually. As far as Jeff goes, I don’t feel that is a viable solution since he would have to ramp up to get up to speed on everything before he can even begin to move forward. We just don’t have that kind of time. Let’s work together in one direction to get this done. We have a great offer on the table, let’s not kill it. Let’s do what is basic common sense. Get a product ready before we can sell it.
Best
Asad

subject: Re: waiting to hear from you all
body:
Asad
I did not have a chance to talk to Jeff today.
He did respond that he was tied up today and most of tomorrow but would call me as soon as he can.

Before you offer up Pauls 20% to anyone I suggest you first exchange a written (email) communication with Paul confirming that if certain criteria were met he would give up his 20%.

So far I have in various conversations heard that Paul would give up his 20 % if:
1) Once the $ was in escrow to pay all investors principal and interest he would kick back my 20 percent.
2) You Asad raised the $$ for the up-dating of the product to be a "functioning product"
3) If a bonafide - in writing - sales price of 2M or greater is submitted and consummated

These three points to some degree overlap but I believe it would be best to exchange a written agreement between you and Paul so its in writing and clear to all.

Asad I would button this arrangement up first between you and Paul 1st before you offer the 20% to anyone - this issue can cause serious problems long term if it is not crystal clear now.
Paul is live copied in the interest of this singular point being clarified if possible by tonight.

Tank You
Steve

Hello Everyone
The only offer we have on the table is going to expire
tomorrow. I don’t know what the deal maker is going to
be doing but if he is an attorney sort, he is probably
going to want to know everything there is to know
about Parkimon and its history. That is going to take a
while and I can assure you that any further delay is
going to offend the arab and from the tone of his last
email chances of him getting tired and walking away are
pretty high. If he does that then I can assure you that
will be the last time this guy is going to come back to us.
He is willing to take up the back-end cost which to be
honest was quite unexpected but there are limits. The
only chance we have is to tell him that we have a
commitment from the investors to raise the funds and
complete the reactivation.
We have an offer on the table and he has told us exactly
what needs to be done. Get the platform up and show
him. In other words, we need a product to show, plain
and simple.
I think the only course of action is to email the entire
communication of Texpo to all the investors and let
them know that this is the only option on the table.
Anyone who is willing to put in the money gets Paul’s
20%.
I’ll be sending out an email to Texpo unless I hear
something from you by tonight.
Best
Asad

subject: reviving parkimon
body: Steve, John, Hope all well. I’ll break this down into parts based on the questions that you gentlemen had in our call yesterday. PARKIMON 1. Funds & time needed to reactivate the platform (i) It will take 3 months with 9 new engineers to reactivate the platform. (Would have taken less than three weeks if we had paid the original core engineers) (ii) 1 engineer @ $20/Hr X 160 hours /Mth  =  $3200 (iii) 9 engineers X $3200 = $28,800/Mth X 3 months = $86,400* (iv) Hosting and 3rd party services = $2100/Mth X 3 = $6300 NOTE* Kindly revisit the emails during Texpo. I had mentioned $172K for 6 months which included 3 months of due-diligence time. He was willing to take over the due-diligence costs and we only had to come up with half plus the legal fees – and we let that opportunity lapse…... 2. Funds needed AFTER the platform is reactivated and to keep it up. (i) We will need a minimum of 3 fulltime engineers to keep the entire platform maintained 24x7 (ii) 3 x $3200 = $9600 /Mth + hosting and 3rd party (= $2100/Mth)  = $11,700/Mth going forward. 3. How much will it take to sustain for 6 months – $11,700 x 6 = $70,200 (tech only) I am assuming the new CEO will want a salary too and I’ll appreciate if I get some of what is owed to me too since the beginning of time. 4. Key value propositions of our platform: Parkimon’s core value prop: “An “asset-less” payment platform that can bill based on location, time, distance, speed and number of axles”. If you think about it, this not only covers parking but tolls too. Also different types of road pricing policies can be enabled. That is, pricing policies can change by time, distance, place, vehicle type and even driver behavior. You can: • price for entering or leaving a zone • price with a series of virtual gantries • price per mile by class of road • vary that price by time of day • offer credits to drivers who stay parked during rush hour • vary that price by type of vehicle • vary that price by the driver's place of residence For instance, a city could charge $4.00 to enter and $0.20 per mile driven downtown during rush hour. Those who arrive at work before 8:00 or after 10:00, then park all day and drive home later than 6:00 pm could be refunded the entry charge.  Electric cars could pay only $0.10 per mile.  City residents, who pay municipal taxes, could pay only $0.05 per mile or be given a refundable $100 driving credit each month. Any policy that they can dream off can be configured road pricing and when I say road pricing I’m talking about parking (stationary) and moving vehicles. MUNICIPALITY BENEFITS (i) NO Meters - NO maintenance, NO up-front costs (ii) Dynamic pricing based on demand or usage based (iii) Build a meter-free city. (iv) Minimize traffic congestion. (v) Collect powerful data. (vi) High speed mobile enforcement -savings in personnel (fewer parking enforcement officers) AND massive increase in ticket (citations) revenues for the municipality. (vii) Various payment methods: Web, Phone, App, Text, voice, QR code, etc. (Consumer benefits – including no more circling the block to find parking.) 5. Positioning the company in this COVID-19 situation and beyond. (i) Municipality budgets are going to be cut (ii) Municipalities will need to generate more revenues with limited resources (Human and monetary) (iii) Higher safety concerns (Parking control officer point of view – public confrontations) (iv) Higher safety concerns (parker point of view – contaminated parking meters) Given the above, I would position the company as the solution to all the above miseries. STAY SAFE, SAVE MONEY, MAKE MONEY. ORANGECONE 6. If left to me, I’d complete Orangecone 2.0 and setup Orangecone as a contingency. Following is my rationale: (i) Parkimon is a municipality and B2B play which will take time to deploy. (ii) Whereas Orangecone is purely C2C, however there are many other similar players in the market who are extremely well funded. Spothero and Parkwhiz to name a few. (iii) These big players are currently feeling the crunch as there is not much parking going on these days as folks shelter in place. (iv) Orangecone 2.0 can be a pivot besides a contingency to Parkimon. (v) The 2.0 version is tapping into the veins of macro trends that have raised millions of dollars while we penny pinched. (vi) The 2.0 version opens up a much bigger market than just parking. Rent a boat, van, truck, car, RV, bike, whatever.. (vii) I can bet you that the likes of Spothero and others will be looking for ways to expand their revenues beyond parking but won’t know how. And OC2.0 can become a target for acquisition. (viii) Even if not, I can assure you that VCs prefer direct to consumer plays much more than government just because there is a lot of red tape and bureaucracy involved in government RFQs. (ix) That said, we can actually go to VCs with OC2.0 (the contingency) (x) Give me just two extra engineers for just 3 months and I can complete 2.0 with them. The entire back-end architecture we have for OC1.0 can be used and modified for 2.0 and working with them, I am positive I can finish it within 3 months. That’s just an extra $19K. Gentlemen, that is basically it in a nutshell. Best of luck! Asad

subject: ~RE: Parkimon prospects
body:
If anyComm gets the contract and sets up the poles, their camera could be used for LPR and parking enforcement. Kinda like how Los Angeles does it for traffic violations. That is, the LPR identifies the owner of the vehicle through DMV integration and then mails the owner the ticket/citation. However, I don’t know the type of camera anyComm will be using and if they can be used for LPR. Theoretically it is doable.

We did not file a patent for what you wrote. However, what you are talking about is motion detection using computer vision and not clicking pictures of cars. See https://youtu.be/v-rU_SJ21Kw  This was just something I was experimenting with. It was never fully developed although it had potential – but then so did what we already had.

If you are going to pursue Passport’s CEO, I would suggest you show them our LPR enforcement. They acquired Nupark for their LPR enforcement and without being biased, compared to ours it’s pretty pathetic in terms of functionality and UI UX. You’ve already seen our Speedchalk, now see Nupark’s for yourself https://youtu.be/N8iM90sk_bM

So, now you want me to bring in prospects? So you can screw them up again? not once but twice - like you did with Texpo?
Send me the investor draft Paul so I can hand over and get away from you

subject: Parkimon prospects
body:
Gentlemen-

With respect to the above I have three prospects: Civic Smart, civicsmart.com, Elgin, out of UK, https://youtu.be/IeDUDZZ-tuM, (you may also go to LinkedIn and enter James Harris, Elgin), and Anycomm, out of Sacramento.

With respect to latter, I spoke with the company CEO Rob Praske, this morning and I will be meeting with him next Friday in San Jose. The company is expecting to sign an MOU (memorandum of understanding with a VC, whom he was unable to identify for, allegedly, 28M next week.) Both of the deals with SF and SJ (see attachments) fell through despite the press releases for one reason or another. However, with the Covid crisis and the city's now desperate for revenues lost during the crisis, the city's have reengaged and are ready to deal. These prospective contracts will enable Anycomm to set up and OWN most all of the strategic streetlights in each city they are doing business and install their product. These streetlights will become, in part, mini-cell tours that will be leased back to the likes of Verizon and AT&T among others (see attachment at bottom of page). This is where the VC money will come in because those funds will be paid to the city's as part of the deal to buy the "real estate."

Asad, I am asking you, respectfully, to look at the presentation packet below, which was prepared by Annycomm and Siemens for the benefit of the Chief Technology Officer of Las Vegas we met with back in 2017, and come up with some ideas whereby Parkimon's technology could augment or integrate with services provided by Anycomm. If you don't see anything please save unwanted/negative comments. I am doing my best to make something happen. What about the first patent filed whereby a photo is taken of parked cars on an entire block and "chalked" with a photo, and a second photo is taken an hour or two later (depending on the time zone), from the same longitude and latitude and using a pole-mounted camera?
I know this guy through a friend and he is amenable to exploring possibilities.

Elgin: Asad and I spoke with the CEO James Harris via conference call 8 months ago. The call went great but the guy's son fell ill and things went sideways. His interest was using Parkimon's tech on the back-end of his platform. The company is getting major transaction in the UK. I have re-engaged with him and he is interested in conducting a Zoom meeting. I expect to be in touch with him next week and will update all on the call afterwards. If there is an opportunity after this call I will ask Asad to participate in the ensuring call.

Civic Smart: Have spoke with Balu twice since conference call. Said he would send back NDA but has yet to do so. I am losing faith in his interest though he could just be distracted as many in the industry are dealing with major problems at this time.

I have a number of other people whom I intend to pursue including the CEO of Passport, passport.com/ There may be a play with Passport on tolls, which is a business segment they want to pursue and I have been in touch with their VP of sales who covers FL and is willing to make an email introduction.

Asad- Have you contacted anybody? Do you have any prospects? Is there anything you can report to John and Steve as far as your own efforts.

Steve- I will finish a summary for you and have it tomorrow for your review.

Best-

P-

subject: R..E: Final Draft for your ok
body:
Steve

Thank you for the email. I hope for the benefit of all, you and Paul can accomplish all you have outlined. I don’t have time to beat around the bush nor worry about anyone’s sensibilities so I’ll say it as I see it.

I want to remind all of you that it is time that the investors hear facts and not fiction. This email needs to make it very clear to the investors and focus on what is required off them and WHY. That should be the focus of the email. I will not be a party to further fairy tales by Paul in order to get more funds out of the investors just because he thinks every municipality is going to go for Parkimon’s tech as it is indispensable NOW due to COVID-19.

The investors need to know and understand that the Texpo, Pied Parker, xyz deals did not go through because our tech is dormant. And that is what needs to be addressed first and foremost.

I reiterate, we still have to complete the technology or else we are in the same boat as we were with Texpo; not having a product to sell. Until we are able to reactivate the technology, we don’t have a product to sell. Remember, Texpo said their deal was contingent on us completing the technology. That is why I said I would step aside and let the investors come up with a plan to raise the capital necessary to get us to the point where we would be marketable. Once that is done, I will do my part in completing the technology.

My thoughts regarding your email with regard to the CEO position: I am and always have been willing to do what is best for the investors and the company. It was for that reason that I agreed to step down as CEO so that you all could find a suitable replacement, one that you all feel comfortable and confident with. Remember, I suggested that you guys form a committee to decide how to move forward since my suggestions with regard to funding and Texpo wasn’t to your satisfaction. It is best that you all secure a CEO that you feel you can work with to accomplish whatever plan you put in place to move the company forward. But before you do that the technology has to be up.

What is Paul going to tell Elgin, Civicsmart, Balu and XYZ? Hey we got a junk in the backyard, once you put in an engine, some wheels, some paint and some gas you’ll see how great it will run? This is such a waste of time. Do you all seriously believe that Paul is going to convince someone to acquire the company or sell it to a municipality? (Just like he did with Pied Piper, Civicsmart, SF, Palo Alto and a host of others?) Companies don’t get acquired because Paul says so or thinks so. They get acquired if they are performing and there is an investment banking firm involved that has experience in M&A. They are sold to a municipality by a professional business development VP, not someone posing as one and hustling NOW because he’s got two of his friends looking over his shoulder. The go-forward strategy to me is very straightforward and everyone needs to understand it.
1. I step down
2. The investors find an interim CEO or full time Advisor (give him or her equity)
3. Fund the reactivation (3 months)
4. Find a CEO
5. Sell to a small municipality or even do a free pilot– but show revenues or even potential revenues
6. THEN either go to a VC for an A-Series (investors c-notes convert and can cash out) OR an investment banker to sell to Passport or whomever.

The email needs to lead the investors down reality and not rely on tales of what might happen IF Paul gets lucky.

A. Tell folks WHY the Texpo, Pied piper and the rest of the deals did not come through. (Because the tech is dormant – we don’t tell anyone what Paul did -twice)
B. What do we all need to do. Lay out the above strategy (if you agree to it)
C. Ask everyone to come together to help. When the going gets tough the tough get going kinda thing but not in the opposite direction. Their help can get the tech back up AND get a CEO type hired.
D. The amount needed and what are the incentives for each who “lend a hand”
E. And THEN talk about the huge potential that COVID-19 has blessed us with.
F. Adding Paul’s communication with Elgin and the rest is not needed. Trying to shore up investor confidence with his “I’m meeting so and so and look up this or that”…. is ridiculous. Any investor with half a brain will see through it. Besides, the investors are probably immune to the many opportunities that “came our way” but then disappeared……
G. Explain why the COVID-19 could be a blessing in disguise- the first two paragraphs should be enough.

Thank you for all your efforts.

Best
Asad

Steve Pinetti
