Steve- 

Suggesting we attach the link below. Even SF will now consider alternatives out of sheer desperation. Asad and I met with them 2 years ago and while the Head of Parking Enforcement loved our concept---his boss, the Head of the SFMTA, who is no longer there, scoffed at the idea of parking meters disappearing. How quickly things can change...This article is indicative of where almost all major cities find themselves,and even SF will have no choice but to look at any and all alternatives that can help increase revenues. One idea might be to replace each meter that becomes functionally obsolete"virtually" (given that they are the cities are not going to have the money to replace meters which cost about $20,000) while marrying our enforcement technology using a camera with Annycom's technology and/or "ownership" of the lightoposts. With the civil unrest going on now, the authorities are likely going to want to be able to identify the bad guys using "the eye in the sky" ----privacy issues be damned.

subject: Final Draft for your ok
body: I am driving all day from Phoenix to Salt Lake City. Will revisit your comments tonight otherwise will send as is. Colm especially interested in any edits you would recommend. Thanks Steve Pinetti steve@pinettiway.com 415 - 279 - 4319 Parkimon Investors Our Partnership – Parkimon Inc. - since inception has for the most part been run by Asad and Paul. The efforts on both their parts have been relentless and we have received great feedback and interest on the functionality of our software from a number of potential buyers / partners -- yet we have hit a crossroads. We are out of capital – our platform is dormant. Asad and Paul have asked for help. John Ipolitto, ( Investor) and myself ( Investor / Note holder) were asked by Asad and Paul to be available to comment when asked on their planned next steps for Parkimon until a formal CEO can be found to actually run the business. Neither John or myself are assuming any liability for Asad or Pauls’s actions past, present or future. Asad has agreed to relinquish control over management of the company to a proper CEO. He has also agreed to playing an active role in assist / support the transition of the software to a buyer / partner. Paul has agreed to step back in to raise additional capital from new investors and or potential partners so that we can hire a real CEO and reactivate all our digital assets and get back on the trail of selling Parkimon. Both Asad and Paul have also indicated they are willing to contribute from their ownership shares should that be necessary to achieve the new goals just mentioned. The 30% discount we offered to Texpo has expired. Paul’s Current Update: Obviously with Covid 19 affecting all of us worldwide many businesses having been turned upside down, politicians and civic leaders are looking for solutions to the enormous economic fallout confronting their jurisdictions. I'm sure you have been watching the news about the riots over the last few days in the U.S. which is only compounding the economic fallout, here. Clearly we are entering a period of time of profound change as virtually all aspects of our lives are being turned upside down---which is creating great fear, anxiety, and anger---but it will also create opportunity for many. I was on a call last week and listening to the head of parking mobility for the city of Seattle (WA) and he said that most cities are now in a position whereby most all are being forced to consider things (i.e. new technologies) that we wouldn't otherwise simply because they don't have the resources now, financial, and human (due to mass layoffs) to run their municipalities as they have been previously. With many in the public fearing coming into contact with potentially contaminated parking meters and kiosks, opportunities for companies like ours who have the technology to provide "touchless payment solutions" and an ability to run a parking edifice without any costly and functionally obsolescent hardware (see www.parkimon.com). Also, there is less exposure, insulated and minimal confrontation of officers with angry parkers as well as massive increase in citation revenue if they integrate with DMV and mail tickets instead of stop n go. I have three prospects: Civic Smart, civicsmart.com, Elgin, out of UK, https://youtu.be/IeDUDZZ-tuM, (you may also go to LinkedIn and enter James Harris, Elgin), and Anycomm, out of Sacramento. With respect to latter, I spoke with the company CEO Rob Praske, this morning and I will be meeting with him next Friday in San Jose. The company is expecting to sign an MOU (memorandum of understanding with a VC, whom he was unable to identify for, allegedly, 28M next week.) Both of the deals with SF and SJ (see attachments) fell through despite the press releases for one reason or another. However, with the Covid crisis and the city's now desperate for revenues lost during the crisis, he city's have reengaged and are ready to deal. These prospective contracts will enable Anycomm to set up and OWN most all of the strategic streetlights in each city they are doing business and install their product These streetlights will become, in part, mini-cell tours that will be leased back to the likes of Verizon and AT&T among others (see attachment at bottom of page). This is where the VC money will come in because those funds will be paid to the city's as part of the deal to buy the "real estate." Elgin: Asad and I spoke with the CEO James Harris via conference call 8 months ago. The call went great but the guy's son fell ill and things went sideways. His interest was using Parkimon's tech on the back-end of his platform. The company is getting major transaction in the UK. I have re-engaged with him and he is interested in conducting a Zoom meeting. I expect to be in touch with him next week and will update all on the call afterwards. If there is an opportunity after this call I will ask Asad to participate in the ensuring call. Civic Smart: Have spoke with Balu twice since conference call. Said he would send back NDA but has yet to do so. I am losing faith in his interest though he could just be distracted as many in the industry are dealing with major problems at this time. I have a number of other people whom I intend to pursue including the CEO of Passport, passport.com/ There may be a play with Passport on tolls, which is a business segment they want to pursue and I have been in touch with their VP of sales who covers FL and is willing to make an email introduction. Thats the news for now. Paul and Asad plan to have a proper CEO in place ASAP who will communicate with the entire investor group regularly in the future and to whom questions can be directed. Thank you Steve Pinetti

subject: RE: Responses to your questions regarding company finances (See responses in Red)
body:
Asad,

What is the agenda and objective of the requested meeting?

Your email on 6/12 indicated you would let us all know your thoughts on next steps by Sunday, which is today. Did you mean “all investors”? Is communication still going out tonight?

The divide between you and Paul is beyond my comprehension and frankly, my interest as an advisor to fix at this point. BUT, I will extend the courtesy of attending this meeting as promised, provided it has clear objectives and a path to constructive next steps. Who are the meeting attendees?

Thanks,
Mary

subject: RE: Responses to your questions regarding company finances (See responses in Red)
body: Please see my comments inline in blue.

subject: Responses to your questions regarding company finances (See responses in Red)
body: Paul, I have the following concerns: 1. You were informed on the conference call we had with Mary before the IPMI conference that the Parkimon account was in the negative and the company was insolvent and had to be winded down. 2. You agreed to deposit 35K immediately, that was to be used to cover bounced checks and critical operational expenses. Jennifer Ross wired $35K on loan to us, which you have a deposit receipt in accordance with this agreement. 3. I would also like to remind you of the concerns that we all had about the limited funds and mutually agreed to conserve them, to the extent that Mary even asked whether we really need to go to the IPMI conference. Duly noted. After spending scarce funds willy nilly, and without authorization, its rather late in the day for you to “duly note” 4. Although you acknowledged and agreed to everything during the conference call with Mary; you waited till I left San Francisco and then without my authorization made two withdrawals as under: 6/07/2019 check no. 1095 5000 / to Thomas Chan - Thomas Chan lent $5,000 and than another $15,000 to the company within the last month without a note or interest as a favor to us to cover the company's overdraft checks. He did it in good faith and expected to be paid back expeditiously. While he asked for $10,000 of the last deposit, I gave him $5,000 in good faith back. He has not been paid interest nor does he have a note and this situation needs to be handled immediately in order for him to continue his services. A. Thomas Chan was being paid interest as there are records of that. B. He has also been duly compensated for all the accounting work that he performs. I have his invoices and payment receipts. C. What part of #1,2 & 3 did you miss relative to your response above? D. First of all you had no authorization to withdraw funds to pay anyone. Secondly, while you think it is okay for you to take money out and pay Thomas and yourself knowing full well that the situation is critical, (and behind my back – I might add) you had no inhibitions in telling me to delay the salaries of the engineers for a few months (numerous times this past year) 6/07/2019 check no, 1092 3000/ to Paul Camera - As customary in the past I received $3,000 bi-annually to cover unreimbursed expenses including phone, gas, travel, meals with clients and for attending functions such as the IPMI conference, I have not been paid anything from the company for 9 months, despite funding it continuously and took $3,000 to cover those and upcoming expenses because it was important that I be at the IPMI conference. You were informed prior to the IPMI that your presence is not required and Parkimon would not foot your bill. Yet you take money out for “upcoming expenses” for the IPMI? Explain that? You told Mary and me on the conference call that your presence was important because of Kevin Dougherty – although I didn’t see why - since I was going to give him a demo. Anyway, Kevin, was not even there. Had you simply called him beforehand you would have known that he was not coming due to his wife expecting a baby and figured out your “importance”. I informed every one of the agenda for the IPMI, you still showed up and did exactly the opposite of what the agenda was. Your self-proclaimed importance at IPMI is debatable. You have received equity in the company for your funding efforts. You are not due anything. In retrospect I should have addressed the aforementioned items with you and Mary in our last discussion. Going forward all financial payments will be reviewed by Mary and the team and agreed to in writing prior to payment. 5. On 4/6/2019, you also withdrew cash of $5166, once again, without any authorization from me. I am a signer on the account, which you, yourself, granted me in the company's meeting minutes before you left for Dubai to pay various expenses while you were gone. That's why you gave Thomas Chan the checkbook. You had no problem with my singing checks and paying for various items for the last 8 months. That does not answer my question about withdrawing cash, nor does it answer my question about who authorized it and when and why was I not informed about it. The only items that you paid by check are yourself and Thomas. ALL our expenses are on auto-pay. The $5,166 was contractually owed by the company to The Pinetti Family Trust and was paid on time in accordance with that agreement. It was paid by cashiers check as the past payment bounced and we are now obligated to send cashiers checks. The repayment of the first $150,000 is due at the end of the June, 2019. Please explain to the rest when we meet as to why the “past payment” bounced. You can also inform them about the number of times in the last one year that we have been suspended from Upwork for bounced checks and missed payments. 6. On 05/01/2019, you also withdrew cash of 10,201.66, again, without any authorization. All items were legitimate company expenses that were directed from the last board meeting and agreed to pay in the meeting minutes. Thomas can provide a full reconciliation of those expenses, including the first cashiers check to the Pinetti Family Trust. Explain the $10,201.66 and limit your answer to who authorized the withdrawal? 7. On LinkedIn you are posing as “Co-Founder at Parkimon, Inc.” The purpose of that which you had previously agreed to was to give me more legitimacy in dealing with potential clients, however since you have changed your mind I have since changed it to Business Development. If you are not willing to re-initate a compensation agreement with me to raise further funds of behalf of the company, I will change it to a past relationship. I still see you as Co-Founder – Parkimon. Kindly fix it. I will determine your relationship and compensation with the company when we meet next. Please note that you are neither an officer, director, co-founder nor even an independent contractor of Parkimon. The checkbook was given to you in good faith for safekeeping. This is a specious statement. It was NOT given to me for safekeeping but it was given to our bookkeeper, Thomas Chan for safekeeping and for me to pay bills when necessary---which I have and in good faith.R “good faith” is not exactly how I see it. Going forward, please answer the following by June 15th 2019 A. Who authorized the withdrawals mentioned in #4, #5 and #6 above? See above. B. Why did you make two cash withdrawals instead of writing checks? (Cash is anonymous.) After the company bounced a check to Pinetti Family Trust---they asked, respectfully, to have cashiers checks sent going forward. Your statement and allegation is specious. The check was noted as being paid to the Pinetti Family Trust. No cash was taken. Please come prepared to reconcile the amounts as they do not match what was owed to Pinetti. C. Why are you posing as a co-founder of Parkimon and on who’s authority? See above. Mary, I will be available Thursday and Friday any time after 10AM. We can meet at VenturePad. Please let me know a time so I can book the conference room. I will be grateful if you can please request Colm to be present also. Thanks and kind regards Asad Sincerely Asad U. Shah – PhD.

subject: orangecone collateral and resignation letter
body:
Hi Steve,
Attached please find my resignation letter for Orangecone Inc.
My attorney requires me to hand over the source code of Orangecone to you if you wish to exercise your claim against the loan you made to Orangecone.
If so, then please let me know and I will send over the agreement.
Kind regards
Asad

535 Mission St. 14th Fl. San Franciso CA 94105

June 18, 2020
To:
Steve Pinetti
2647 Leavenworth St.
San Francisco. CA 94133
RE: Resignation letter

Dear Steve,
This letter serves as my resignation as CEO and Director of Orangecone Inc. effective immediately.
I was able to develop and deliver the Orangecone platform that was akin to Airbnb for private driveways, but we could not come up with the funds to market it and the company has been insolvent since an year now. With that said, I no longer feel as though I can do the best possible job for the company and must resign my position.
I will cherish your friendship and the technical accomplishments and innovations that are part of my history with Orangecone.
Going forward, I am confident that you will be able to fine an excellent replacement as CEO and revive the company and the technology will eventually see the light of day.
I wish you and the company the best in the future.
With gratitude and warm regards,
Sincerely

Asad U. Shah

subject: Parkimon
body:
Asad-

I spoke with Mary yesterday and she conveyed to me that she would not go forward with you unless I am involved.

For the benefit of the cause I am making this last-ditch effort to resurrect our relationship and move forward. If you decline, I need to let my investors know that I have been let go. I owe them that. Respectfully, if I am out of the picture the chance of raising money from any of these people will be zero.

My girlfriend Sally, is an angel investor in a company called Red Shift Networks www.redshiftnetworks.com. She has been involved with the company for 13 years. The company will be sold within the next 12 months. The CEO and co-founder is a man by the name of Amitava Mukherjee. You can look at his profile on LinkedIn. I have met with him myself. He is an incredible man with great gifts. As Sally pointed out to me this morning, Red Shift was in the very same situation Parkimon is now, a number of times, and each time Amitava was able to pull the company out of the abyss. Amitava, like you, is a technologist and Sally is willing to arrange a meeting with Amitava, if you are amenable, to allow the company to get some input from someone who has been there. Conceivably, he could become an advisor for Parkimon. He knows ALL the VCs and, would be able to connect you to those sources that would best serve the company's next steps. Please let me know if you are interested in meeting with him.

Also, I am forwarding an inquiry from Douglass Parking who sees potential order from us. You let me know what you want to do.

P-

subject: Re: Parkimon
body:
Paul,

Just to clarify, what I had said was that I would not be involved until/unless you and Asad close the gap on all fronts.

Mary

Sent from my Samsung Galaxy smartphone.

subject: Receipt of Collateral and Cancellation of Promissory Notes
body:
Asad - I am willing to sign the Acknowledgment of Receipt of Collateral and Cancellation of Promissory Notes you sent to me on June 18th, however, before signing the release I would like to first have a neutral third party review and evaluate the source code.

Back in November of 2018 Orange Cone had a viable platform that worked and was tested by Jim Lehay. I believe we actually had a functional App that was being used for demonstrations. I am anticipating that the software code will be reinstated to an account that we can test to ensure it is as operational or better than it was at that date, including the beta site where it can be tested by our consultant.

Once it is validated and determined to be operational I will release any future claims. Please forward me what you have in mind and provide instructions for our consultant to review.

Thank you.
Steve

subject: R..E: Receipt of Collateral and Cancellation of Promissory Notes
body: Steve, I will gladly satisfy your request, however, my attorney has advised that since the loan was for Parkimon and OC was developed with Parkimon investor funds, I will need written confirmation from you that all investors know that you are requesting this and approve. Also, as you are aware that I have resigned and am no longer associated with Parkimon. Releasing your claim on Parkimon of your debt is now between you and the current management of Parkimon. To your point highlighted in yellow below: The platform has been on the shelf since more than a year now and as I explained to you all several times (check your emails dated 5/4/2020, and investor updates) Tech that has been shelved has to be reactivated to be demonstrated. It cannot be “reinstated to an account” – Reactivation will take time and human and monetary resources. While it would take 3+ months for Parkimon, it will still take 2 to 3 weeks or more for OC and that too from someone who was working on it. For a 3rd party consultant it will take months to figure out the source code, as that is what you will be getting. You will need different types of skill sets for the front-end (web) and database. Since there are apps associated with the platform, you will also need iOS / iPhone experts and Android experts. You’ll need to get Apple and Google Store accounts restored and more to get the consumer Apps up. In short, a lot of handholding and if Paul hadn’t Effed the engineers by spending their salaries on himself, this could have been done in a couple of weeks or less. Here is a link to some 3rd parties that can help you. Please let me know who you select so I can be sure they have the necessary qualifications to get it up and evaluate it. Kind regards Asad

subject: Action by Written Consent of the Directors and Stockholders of Parkimon, Inc -to Steve Pinetti
body:
Dear Mr. Steve Pinetti,

As the outgoing Chief Executive Officer and member of the Board of Directors of Parkimon, Inc., I write to inform you that you have been elected to the board of directors of Parkimon Inc., pursuant to the attached Action by Written Consent by all of the members of the company’s prior Board of Directors, and by the holders of a majority of the outstanding shares of capital stock of the company. I wish you the best of luck in this endeavor and trust you will responsibly and effectively manage Parkimon, Inc.

Kind regards

Asad Shah

subject: .~..FW: transition of parkimon and orangecone ip /source code
body:
Gentlemen:
In furtherance to my email below. I am addressing the concern that Dr. Fosbinder expressed with regard to my position as a shareholder. Please understand that I do NOT have any authority in the day to day running of the company; that is entirely up to the board. For example, with Steve declining to take a seat on the board, there is now a vacant seat. The two existing Board Members can fill that position under full authority. You can pick whomever you want. The same goes with the hiring of the officers of the company. The board has full authority.

Please rest assured that I have no interest in interfering in how you run the company. As I have mentioned many times before. It would be great if the company is successful and the platform is used somewhere – anywhere.
Best
Asad

subject: transition of parkimon and orangecone ip /source code
body:
Hello John, Doctor Fosbinder,

As previously stated, I agree to perform any and all obligations under my IP assignment agreements entered into with the company. (Please see attached.) However, I am not obligated to provide any additional services to the company and as you know, under law, I cannot be compelled to provide any further services to the company.

That said, following are the to-dos that need to be performed by any one of you for the successful transitioning of the IP source-code and documentation.

1. The source code needs to be moved to a secure online repository. As such please sign up at https://github.com/ and choose the “Teams” package. It will cost $4 per user per month. That is all you need currently as you can give the login credentials to any engineer you hire to get the platform back up. At a high level, GitHub is a website and cloud-based service that helps developers store and manage their code as well as track and control changes to their code. It is used by millions of engineers and companies the world over and was recently acquired by Microsoft. Companies from 3M, American Airlines, NASA to government to banks to Facebook, all use it and it is the most secure cloud based host for software code that you will find on the internet.

2. Anyway, once you have signed up on Github and created an account, please send me the login credentials. I will then upload all the documents, videos and the source code for Parkimon and Orangecone to it and let you know when done. (takes a day or less) You can then change the password.

3. In parallel, you also need to create an account on https://www.godaddy.com/ and let me know the account holder’s email address. I don’t need login credentials for that as I can then transfer Parkimon.com and Orangecone.app to your account. Click on this link to see how easy it is.

4. Doctor Fosbinder had some concerns about me owning majority shares and disrupting things if an “opportunity” came your way. Firstly the fact that I own shares and would benefit if some good came out of it should give you a comfort level. Secondly, owning the majority of shares does not give me control over the company. It gives me a veto right only in certain circumstances subject to shareholder vote in Delaware. In fact, most things are controlled by the board. Only certain larger actions go for shareholder approval. That is corporate law and I did not make it.

5. If you are still reluctant, the company can buy back my shares for what it owes me in just the back pay. If you recall I had made that offer initially.

If you have any questions, please let me know as I would like to make the transition as easy as possible.

Kind regards
Asad

subject: FW: parkimon rfq from armanino - sp
body:
Hi Steve
Please see Michaels’s email below. I’ll appreciate if you can get Paul to cooperate and request Thomas to send me a copy of all the taxes he filed for Parkimon. He created a huge fuss last time when Texpo wanted the documents and if he continues down that path again, he’ll be responsible for the consequences of his actions. He needs to understand that whatever impediments he thinks he can erect, only fortify my complaints against him and add more meat to the evidence against him. I’m willing to put things behind and release him of what he owes to the company but only if he cooperates.
Kind regards
Asad

-

Michael Hogan 
ArmaninoLLP

subject: parkimon rfq from armanino
body: Hi Michael, Thank you for all your insights on M&A and coming on the Zoom meeting. Truly appreciate all you do. I’d be grateful if you can give us a quote for (1) Assignment for the Benefit of Creditors (ABC), (2) a NOL tax study of Parkimon so we can determine how much will be taken out of the offer and what will the investors finally get. (3) Approx. cost for an attorney and also if you could recommend one that you have worked with before on similar matters. Once we have these figures, we will know exactly how much to raise in total for the above and also the reactivation of the platform. Since you were on the call and also heard Avi, I’d be beholden if you could also suggest and guide us on the best course of action going forward for both parties. That is, whether an equity purchase, ABC or asset purchase? Which would be the most economical and fastest route? Once again, thank you so much for all you do. God bless- stay safe! Warm regards Asad

subject: your text message
body: Hi Steve, I saw your text and appreciate your faith in me. I don’t want to keep revisiting history but this project that should have given me financial freedom has put me in a massive debt. I’ve lost 65 engineers who I used to call friends. 38 of them were married with 70+ kids between them. (It’s India after all – with a billion + population) Paul would keep telling me that this project is never going to fail for lack of funds and the engineers were told to wait 3 months for their salaries and then never paid. It happened 13 times in a span of just two years! We only needed 11 to 12 engineers at any one time. We had a turnover of 65! It was me who had to face them and their wives when I went to India last year. Do you have any idea what it took to go door to door and visit each and every one of them who got screwed because of me? My youngest daughter got a 100% scholarship from MIT and I could not even afford to pay for her living expenses in Boston. Shattered her dreams. How many people get admission in MIT, let alone a full scholarship? It’s me who has to face her. I put my heart and soul into this project and got nothing but heartache. Counting everyone including the investors, over 200 lives have been affected by this project. Maybe I’m biased but if I get into it, I can assure you that Paul will find a way to screw me and the deal some more. You’ve seen it happen with Texpo, not once but twice. You haven’t seen how it happened with Steve Roth and me and what Sally and Paul did to him and me. There is just too much bad blood. I’m sorry. I’ll help technically when needed but will not lead this as like I said to you yesterday. I don’t know Avi. Besides, after taxes the investors are probably going to get a haircut and that too will be blamed on me. My only suggestion is to use a law firm or Armanino to take this forward. You can easily do it yourself as the interim board member. No approval from anyone will be needed. Or just tell Paul to do it as he too is a shareholder. Just assign the company over to Armanino as they are probably a lot cheaper than a law firm and they will take care of everything. Once again, I’m sorry. Wish you all the best Asad

subject: Letter from SVLG
body:
Asad
I just got an email from Micheal Stebbins from Silicon Valley Law Group (SVLG) that a communication from them was sent to you in error. Happy to copy you on their apology to me on this matter.

We were working on a letter to send you but it was put on hold 10 days ago in the hope that we could pull off some kind of deal with Avi.
In this process the SVLG on Monday said they would help us do the Avi deal and yesterday they have decided not to help as the deal is actually too small for them.- go figure.

Yesterday we got ahold of Mike Hogan who will take over doing the deal with Avi.
The ultimate goal is to make the deal with Avi work - that's what we all want.

Please disregard any communication you get from Silicon Valley Law Group.

Steve

subject: RE: Parkimon - Ideas and Open Items
body: I find it highly distressing that from just a week ago and prior to the investor meeting, I was hearing names of people with fat bank accounts and big money and even their friends willing to invest… and us reaching a target of $1.5M easily …and NOW we suddenly start talking of selling our assets i.e. Orangecone. What if OC was not there, what would we be selling then?? Only two reasons startups fail. Lack of money and lack of execution. I believe we will be able to execute in Abu Dhabi and with OC as soon as we launch.. However, I’m not very sure about surviving till that happens coz of lack of the funds part anymore. Gentlemen, I don’t beat around the bush as that is not my style. I can hire people to bullshit and also manage and clean up my back-office. I need both of you for raising funds and THAT was THE underlying premise for the equity I gave up. If there is a problem going forward, then let me know. FYI, I talked to Salem about putting money in. His father’s take on this is that he has never invested in stock markets and “intangible” things. All his investments have been either in real estate, horses or factories, manufacturing and “tangible” businesses. Look at the group’s website. He is old school that a ways. He is willing to put in the funds for whatever we will need in terms of gantries, vehicles, etc. and we may not need the big numbers to do the DOT job that I thought we did. However, we do need to survive till that happens. If you gents cannot pull your weight and bring in the funds then let me know – Selling OC is not an option. I’ll put it on the shelf for the time being if needed. The IP that you all are sitting on is worth millions and can generate millions more – do not take it for granted. Best A

subject: Parkimon - Ideas and Open Items
body: Good morning Paul, Another thought on capital raising: In addition to the steps you plan to take per our call yesterday (which I wholly endorse), perhaps you could tap our current "Paul-sourced" investors like Moq for an extra $10K. $10K is much more manageable than $25K, and if 10 folks put in an extra $10K, that covers us for one month, and will enable Asad & team to launch OC and (hopefully) begin generating revenue. Just a thought. Talk to you later today, Chief. Hi Asad, One housekeeping matter and one thing I would like to confirm via email: 1) C-Notes: Please send me the correct $15K C-Note when you have a chance, and make sure that WS has all the missing C-Notes in the week ahead. We owe this to our investors. 2) IP Ownership: Per our discussion this past week, I would like to confirm that all the IP the drives the OC App and the DOT platform belongs solely to Parkimon. It thus follows that money realized in the sale or spin-off of such assets would accrue solely to Parkimon (owners and investors). Further, any expansion of the OC App and DOT Smart City Mobility Platform (into other countries, for example) would also benefit solely Parkimon. In contrast, any future new applications related, say, to insurance or other distinct businesses, do/would not belong to Parkimon, but would be funded separately through another round of financing, and owned by new investors. It is very important that we clarify this in writing; if my understanding is incorrect, please let me know. I appreciate your confirmation. Thank you! Raj

subject: Re: Yvette's Camera's subpoena
body:
Asad-

Is this a new subpoena? In a recent email you said you had been "recently slapped with a subpoena" regarding Paul.

Unfortunately, neither I nor any of the investors are able to help in this matter. The only officer of Parkimon or board member of the company since 2015, was you, and it would have been your fiduciary duty to have retained this information.

Asad, you resigned from Parkimon and Orange Cone to a non-existent board with no documentation or records. Hence no one had the opportunity to accept your resignation, which is a concern we will address later. In the meantime we have been advised to not to take responsibility for this subpoena as the only person who could possibly be responsible is you.

Steve
Steve Pinetti

Anyway, that is not the issue.

Best

subject: ~-RE: Re: orangecone PowerPoint
body: Raj With all due respect to your incredible command of the English language and willingness to correct every nuance of my grammar and punctuation. I have started getting the uncomfortable feeling that you are not just overstepping but stepping on my toes. While I have a healthy respect for your eye for microscopic details and understand your need to be 100% correct when it comes to presentations and stuff, I don’t appreciate your need to correct every communication I send out. I asked you for general advice on responding to Colm’s email regarding Mary as they are your friends. You went beyond the call of duty and offered me exact words and out of respect to you, I used them. However, this has to stop. I am not your 3rd grade student, nor are you my teacher or secretary. You are an investor AND now a friend and I really really like you and your energy; and I would like to keep the status quo for a long time and thought I’d let you know how I feel so it does not get out of hand. I have my own style of writing. On most occasions, its blunt and straightforward but gets the point across and has served me well for over 57 years. I value our friendship and rest assured it will be jeopardized if you don’t tone it down. The back-end house cleaning and raising of funds is where I need you and Paul most. I hope you don’t take this email any other way, because quite simply, the underlying thought behind it is our bourgeoning friendship that I feel was being endangered. Kind regards Asad PS: I had already drafted an email for Bob, which by the way was quite a bit like what you had offered and a little bit more – so please chill. (I think I can wield a pen and paper – maybe not nearly as good as you but better than most)

subject: Fw: Re: orangecone PowerPoint
body:
Hi Asad, 
One last thing - once you prepare the email for Bob, please send it to me 
one more time so I can do a careful final QC check. You can laugh at my QC 
paranoia - I will laugh with you! - but keep in mind when I issue my audit 
reports, passing judgment on folks who are invariably higher up the Sony 
food chain, I need 100% accuracy and clarity. Not 99%, but 100%. 
Hopefully this will help you understand my unfortunate intenseness... I am 
always asking myself: Did I overlook something, did I make a bloody 
mistake? 
 Put the power of Raj to our collective good use... after all, it 
costs you nothing but a bit of time. Best, 
Raj

subject: Re: orangecone PowerPoint
body:
Good morning Asad,
In addition to the (wonderful, now improved wonderful!) PDF file, we should send Bob a little note, say three or four paragraphs, giving a very high level overview of what Parkimon is, and were we would like someone to take us with the OC App. Something like this:

Dear Bob,
As you know, we met with Tim Bender last week, and Tim sees what many of our current investors see, namely a solid business opportunity and, what's more, an opportunity to introduce disruptive technology & know how to make the world a better place. We have the technology, and to a certain extent, the know how, but need an experienced, proven leader to successfully launch the OC App to maximize its full potential.

Attached please find a background deck to give you an idea of what the OC App is, as well as the market potential. There is much more to share, of course, but this will give you a flavor as to where were are and where we plan to go. Would be great to meet with you in person when you return from Lake Tahoe. Sincerely,

Asad Shah, PhD

Hi Asad,
Will call you now. Strong presentation, but I have many corrections that will make the presentation near perfect!
Raj

subject: RE: Raj's Equity Ownership Agreement
body: Hi Raj, FYI, neither Paul nor I have vested yet. Paul’s been working 10 hours a day and stressing 24 hours, while I’ve been working 18 hours a day. Paul’s brought in over $800k and brought me to this stage where my technology is ready to be deployed. He did it where there was nothing but a little demo. As such I value his contribution akin to a co-founder. He earned his 20% when he raised half a million – yet he is still hustling. While I highly value your input in everything that you do, I don’t believe I agreed to the 8% (when I did) for your accounting expertise. I distinctly remember our conversations about you bringing in the big boys who can write fat checks. Raising funds is the top priority if this is company is to survive, not accounting – I can hire someone for that. While, you have introduced Tim Bender and I really appreciate that, he has not yet come through. Anyway, all this is moot. Everyone’s vesting starts from the day they get started and as such I can back date it from when you got personally involved in the day-to-day biz of Parkimon. I reiterate, no one is vested yet and it would be unfair to others if you were given any special treatment as no one got it. I would suggest that you sign off on the agreement ASAP and tell Polly to do the same too. (His vesting started when he started raising money and yours will start when you sign off on the agreement ) However, please note that the terms are for raising money and advice, not handling the back-office -as without the funds, there will be no back-office to handle Have a safe trip and don’t do anything I would do in Copa Cabana Asad

subject: Raj's Equity Ownership Agreement
body:
Good morning Asad,
You have graciously given me a six percent (6%) stake in Parkimon, and while this has been communicated to Wilson Sonsinni, I thought it would be in our mutual best interest to agree on terms. Given my work thus far (over 100 hours in the past year) and introductions to people like Colm, Mary, Tim Bender, my friend Greg, my thought is that three percent 3% vests immediately, and the remaining three percent vests over three years provided my ongoing involvement primarily in the accounting/finance area. Hence an additional one percent would vest on June 1, 2019, then another on June 1, 2020, and the final on June 1, 2021. If I cease my involvement with Parkimon at any time before June 1, 2021, I forfeit any unvested equity ownership. If you decide you know longer want me involved for any reason even though I continue to provide the same advise and service that I have thus far, then we agree that I would receive half (50%) of any unvested equity ownership. If this sounds reasonable, please confirm via email and we will call it a deal. Thank you again for giving the opportunity to work with you on this exciting venture. I know times are tough now, but in the end I am confident that the intelligence and beauty of the technology you and your team have built will prevail.
Warm regards,

Raj

subject: Paul's confirmation & Raj's Agreement
body:
Hi Paul,
As discussed on the phone this afternoon, and per our email exchanges below, you understand that - per Asad's and your own representations to me - my position is that I am entitled to a fully-vested six percent of Parkimon effective immediately.
However, as a compromise to you and Asad, especially given my desire to continue supporting Parkimon's business in the years ahead and put the current friction behind us, I am willing to compromise by having a fully-vested three percent (3%) now and have the remaining three percent (3%) vest over two years, as you suggested on our call. Additionally:

a)
If Asad refuses to fully vest me with 3% immediately, per our call this afternoon, you agree to give me 15% of your 20% fully vested interest in Parkimon effective immediately, which will ensure I have a fully vested 3% equity interest in Parkimon now as consideration for my (unpaid) work this past year.

b)
Over the two-year term that my remaining 3% is to vest, if Asad terminates me for any reason other than my failure to implement the necessary accounting, management reporting, auditing, and related governance tasks, you agree to transfer an additional 15% of your 20% fully-vested equity interest (i.e. an additional 3% of Parkimon) to me.
c)
If I simply "check out" or fail to perform my governance role, including implementing and managing the accounting, auditing, and related governance functions, then I agree to and will forfeit any unvested equity ownership.
Paul, if you feel this is fair, it would ease my mind considerably if you could take a moment to return this email to me with your confirmation that you concur with the above terms. Your email confirmation will obviously assuage my present concerns regarding what I believe was represented to me. It will also enable me to continue supporting you and Asad as I have this past year, to everyone's mutual benefit. Thank you!
Raj
p.s. I look forward to having you and Asad over to my house Friday any time between 10 a.m. and 4 p.m., and am sure it will put all of us - and the Parkimon investors - on the right path going forward. Best,
Raj
p.s. If there is anything in the above that concerns you, feel free to call me until 8:30 this evening or any time after 7 a.m. tomorrow.

subject: Re: Raj's Legal Position and Email Tomorrow
body: Sent from my iPhone

Hi Paul,
Thank you for this email and your texts. Based upon your below email, I will not communicate with Asad, and thank you for ensuring that my six percent equity interest has been secured. I will of course continue to support you and Asad as I have in the past months. I have not mentioned our recent communications to anyone, so we can put them behind us and move forward in a positive manner, as governance, accounting, etc., will be very important if/when revenue begins coming in. Regards,
Raj

subject: Re: advisory role request
body:
Hi Paul,
I send this to you from a plane. Paul, I completely trust you, and that is a large statement for me to make. Your email below makes me feel much better. Paul, I want to continue supporting you and Parkimon, and believe the best way for me to do so is to report directly to you. Asad can make requests to me to help him with presentations, pricing, really anything. However, I will not send him emails or call him from this point on without checking with you.
Another important thing: We cannot trust Asad going forward having access to Parkimon's bank accounts. Rather, you and Thomas should have signing authority, and Asad should get a credit card (jointly with his wife, as he has NO credit), which he could use for Parkimon related expenses. He could send his monthly statement to you, you would forward to me and I would audit it. This will be particularly important once revenue begins coming in.
Also, you would determine whether over the course of the next three years I earn my additional 3% in unvested equity. Asad would have no say in this matter, as I do not trust him.
I will sign off for now and not email or call you until Tuesday or Wednesday.
I know this must be stressful, but keep in mind YOU control his cash flow, and you MUST seize this moment to get what you have earned or I fear Asad will shaft you Best
Raj

subject: 1RE: Parkimon Re-organization Proposal
body: I’ll review and let you know.

subject: Parkimon Re-organization Proposal
body:
Asad,

Attached is a proposal for your review that will allow Parkimon to continue as a going concern while a new strategy and management expertise is put in place to maximum our success.

Please take some time to reflect on this as many hours have gone into finding a solution in this critical time that is upon us.

Best-

P-

subject: R..E: Parkimon Re-organization Proposal
body: Paul, Like your fearless leader with the orange hair, you seem to be living in a bubble - dissociated with reality, and it’s all about “YOU”. Allow me to explain “your” proposal from MY angle. (see if you can put yourself in my shoes and then forgive my unprofessionalism. -- I try hard to avoid when conversing with you.) 1. You got me to build a product. 2. I more than delivered, as you now have 3 products to sell. 3. However, you did not give me the tools (monetary and human resources) to sell the products but expected me to deliver revenues. – From where I sit, you did not deliver as per ..and I quote: “this company will not fail for lack of funds!!” 4. Since I did not have the resources, I could not sell. You have therefore concluded and now truly believe that I am inept and not capable of taking the company forward (in this you are correct) 5. As per your term #4, The board can at any time dilute me to less than 1% by issuing more stock. 6. “The BOD shall have overall voting power in lieu of majority stock voting control.” So I can be put out to pasture at the whim of the honorable Board any time? 7. As per your term #1, The BOD can sell the IP at whatever price and I won’t have any say in the matter. For instance, one fine day they can decide to just get every investor’s investment back ($1.6M) and leave me in the dust. 8. “As incentive, you will be paid a salary of $6,000 per month” So now you want me to continue slaving for you for peanuts while you neuter me some more and with no way to protect MY interests. What am I? your boy? 9. “a one-time reimbursement of $78,000 will be paid to you within ninety (90) days” I believe I mentioned to you several times that I have been taking loans to pay off my loans. I do not have 90 days, or even 30. If I get confirmed for a job, I will be able to go to a bank and get another loan- that I would like to avoid. So, 90 days is not an option. Paul, I can go on and on and on and conversing with you is getting tiresome. If I didn’t know you any better, I would have thought that you had planned this “takeover bid” all along. But chill, you are not capable of thinking that far ahead. Let me make this easy for you and let me give you a proposal that can actually get you what you want. Total control PLUS all the things you want to do as per your proposal: (The following terms are non-negotiable.) 1. I will not tell James to dissolve the company on Monday. Instead I will wait till Wednesday to hear your thoughts. 2. I will resign and hand over the company to you. 3. I will not come on the BOD (as I won’t have any say anyways, plus I don’t want any liability going forward with you running the show.) 4. You will wire $78K to Bela’s account within 48 hours of receiving my resignation. Not 7, 30, 60 or 90 days. 5. I will keep 49% of shares in the company with an anti-dilution clause for 70% of the 49% (to recoup the money I spent on my payments IP that has been assigned to Parkimon plus the opportunity cost and lost, the hours and time, the emotional and physical BS that I had to bear plus a whole lot more) 6. You can then hire me as an independent technical consultant on a monthly retainer of $20K (salary for engineers and moi) plus a “delivery” bonus in case of an acquisition, licensing or whatever to “incentivize” me. 7. As the consultant, I will help Strickland do what he does and help in the sales, fund raising, etc. Rest assured you will have my full support as I know Parkimon has excellent technology and can do very well under the right management, plus I want to recoup my time and efforts. 8. You will make WSGR’s dues as a priority and will pay half of their dues upon my resignation. There you have it, let me know if this works for you – as it should, and I’ll stay away from WSGR for another couple of days. Best A PS: BTW, I don’t know who drafted the c-note that you attached but the interest is above usury limits, meaning that the investors probably won’t be able to enforce such a high interest rate… PPS: Your clause -C: “20% of loan value in stock options iii) 2X return on investment upon acquisition or merger iv) in the event of continuing on, a initial valuation share discount of 30% plus interest accrued” Did you come up with this brilliant clause all by yourself or do you have an advisor? If the later, you need to get another advisor. If the former, then please stop thinking- It’s in your best interest. (With this clause, I doubt if any VC will touch you with a 100 foot pole!!)

Asad,

subject: f~RE: . ~Re: .RE: ..RE: Response
body:
“Bailing this thing out”?? Any idea, as to why you have to bail us out- did you pass that minor detail to the new investors of how YOU screwed up the company? And are you really bailing us out or is this a repeat of what you did before? Finding mom and pop unsophisticated investors pitching in 25K each at a time and who wanted revenues without pitching in for a marketing, promotion, selling team??

“fire sale my IP” So you are all set to take over MY IP that cost me over $3.75 AND a whole lot more because of you!. IP that is worth at least $7M+ based on 30,000 hours of engineering efforts and Bay area prices! So you think, I’m going to fire sale it for peanuts so you and your new “investors” can get their hands on it for next to nothing? Was that plan-B all along? Is that why you are so relaxed – taking it easy – doing everything at the last minute? Not caring IF I dissolve or not? Was the “PROPOSAL” drafted so I would NOT accept it and dissolve the company so you could buy the tech in a fire sale??

Sorry to disappoint you but the “fire sale” that you are so hoping for is NOT going to be auctioned for peanuts. I will make sure that I get every penny I put in for time and effort and a lot more. (Which will also work for the current investors who don’t take a right off at the dissolution). May take a long time but it is not going into your hands. I will make sure of that.

me stepping down?? You on the board of Parkimon and running Parkimon? I could die laughing. But before I dissolve the company I have to take care of a few other things to make sure that you get exactly what you deserve whenever the IP is auctioned.
Hang tight – your intentions and that of your fictitious investors who were driving the proposal are very clear to me.
Best of luck
A

subject: Re: . ~Re: .RE: ..RE: Response
body: Please send the cap table. If I can't review the governing documents and cap table there is no point in wasting any more time. You're either stepping down or your not Without your cooperation there's no point in me continuing to try to bail this thing out. Consequently, you just need to move forward with James Huie and fire sale your IP. P-

OK. Then I want you to copy ALL of them in the next email and send me the first proposal you sent me AS IS together with the c note and Strickland's prop, with words to the effect that makes it very clear to all of them and me that they were involved in the making of the terms and conditions of the proposal. In short they need to own the proposal you sent to me. Since you are in talks with them, it shouldn't take you beyond this Friday. (tomorrow - which is your deadline) Best A

subject: Re: ..RE: ..RE: Response
body:
Yes and yes.

The guy I am meeting with at two, Jim Brear, has just sold his company, Veriflow. You can look him up. He said he "might be" interested. He wants to see a copy of the cap table. Please send it. We're now dealing with smart money and there are going to be demands on that money---assuming we're able to get it.

P-
Can you please answer the question in my mail below? I repeat. Are these people involved in your negotiations with me or not. A yes or no will do.

subject: Re: ..RE: ..RE: Response
body: Please forward A copy of the Cap Table. I have a meeting at 2 o’clock today with a potential investor who just sold his company. The first thing he asked me about last night when I spoke with him in preparation for our meeting today, Not coincidentally, was to see a copy of the Cap Table I have a meeting with him at 2 o’clock today. Please forward as soon as possible. Thank you.
You say they are driving it not you. Are these the same people that worked on “the many hours” of the proposal you sent me?

subject: Re: ..RE: Response
body:
Asad-

I have 9-11 investors that have indicated they are willing to write checks from $10,000 to $25,000. However, none of them will write a check until they understand the value of the IP and/or memorialize change in management. Please understand, I am not driving this, they are. In other words, it's their call, not mine.

This $150,000 to 200,000 I expect to come in will right the ship---pay you, pay your engineers and pay 1/3 of what you are "owed." It is possible, that once the findings of Strickland's audit are published---assuming, of course, it is determined that the technology is viewed as being as valuable as you think it is--Strickland, Roth, and Amitava, Sally's friend and co-founder of Red Shift, will have investors, who may want to come, in which case we can pay out the remainder of your demand immediately. This is up to you...BTW Amitava knows ALL the carriers and all the VCs and I am going to ask him to be an adviser. I will send you his comments regarding your pitch deck behind this.

If, on the other hand, you are going to close the company now you will get nothing for "what you are owed" for some time, or at least until the IP can be auctioned and final accounting worked out - this is, of course, your choice..

Please send me the CAP table as it is basis for the valuation of how we proceed. I cannot be asked to step in and solve this money issue without the knowledge and transparency of this information.

P-
See my response inline in blue.

subject: Response
body: Let me make this easy for you and let me give you a proposal that can actually get you what you want. Total control PLUS all the things you want to do as per your proposal: (The following terms are non-negotiable.) 1. I will not tell James to dissolve the company on Monday. Instead I will wait till Wednesday to hear your thoughts. What part of Wednesday did you miss? Do you like to do things down to the wire? It is kinda late now, I have received a check list from James and will be proceeding with the filing with the State by FRIDAY 6TH OF SEPTEMBER. This date is two (2) days from today. Once I file with the State it will be done. DO YOU GET IT or should I repeat? 2. I will resign and hand over the company to you. You will be handing over the company to a board of directors of which I will be one. You have been asked to be on the board but it is your choice not to be. If you are going to be a consultant, we will need a contractual agreement to put in place for a minimum of one-year. That is fine with me. 3. I will not come on the BOD (as I won’t have any say anyways, plus I don’t want any liability going forward with you running the show.) Okay. 4. You will wire $78K to Bela’s account within 48 hours of receiving my resignation. Not 7, 30, 60 or 90 days. You will receive 1/3 of this amount upon execution of changes to the governing documents, 1/3 upon completion of the consultant's deep-dive interview and the balance will have first priority in payment based on equity funding, no - later than 90 days of our agreement. I see, you are negotiating what is owed to me? Who do you think you are? You think I am going to beg for the peanuts the company was NOT paying me? What part of NON NEGOTIATABLE did you not get? I am not asking you. I am telling you. That is what is owed to me!! Don’t tempt me Paul, I will circulate this entire communication to ALL the investors. I’ve gone over 4073 email communications between us. Trust me you don’t want to try my patience! 5. I will keep 49% of shares in the company with an anti-dilution clause for 70% of the 49% (to recoup the money I spent on my payments IP that has been assigned to Parkimon plus the opportunity cost and lost, the hours and time, the emotional and physical BS that I had to bear plus a whole lot more) This seems reasonable provided the 80-20 split less the minor grants to Shamlia, Thomas and Kim, is what exists today. You will need to provide a copy of the most current cap table which will be made part of this agreement to insure the aforementioned is true. God!! get a calculator out. On second thoughts forget it. I wont even bother explaining. 6. You can then hire me as an independent technical consultant on a monthly retainer of $20K (salary for engineers and moi) plus a “delivery” bonus in case of an acquisition, licensing or whatever to “incentivize” me. Agreed. Please detail your proposed incentive requirements. I will once, I hear back from you. Trust me they will be very reasonable. I am not in the habit of screwing my own company. 7. As the consultant, I will help Strickland do what he does and help in the sales, fund raising, etc. Rest assured you will have my full support as I know Parkimon has excellent technology and can do very well under the right management, plus I want to recoup my time and efforts. OK 8. You will make WSGR’s dues as a priority and will pay half of their dues upon my resignation. Agreed They are owed about 30K. I’ll ask James again. There you have it, let me know if this works for you – as it should, and I’ll stay away from WSGR for another couple of days. Best A PS: BTW, I don’t know who drafted the c-note that you attached but the interest is above usury limits, meaning that the investors probably won’t be able to enforce such a high interest rate… PPS: Your clause -C: “20% of loan value in stock options iii) 2X return on investment upon acquisition or merger iv) in the event of continuing on, a initial valuation share discount of 30% plus interest accrued” Did you come up with this brilliant clause all by yourself or do you have an advisor? If the later, you need to get another advisor. If the former, then please stop thinking- It’s in your best interest. (With this clause, I doubt if any VC will touch you with a 100 foot pole!!)

subject: Cannabis Opportunity & Parkimon Web-site
body:
Hello Paul & Asad,
Two things for you to consider:

1) Cannabis industry opportunity: If you guys determine that, in the immediate/short term, there is a better opportunity to get the payment processing application up and running in the cannabis trade, as one of your investors, I trust your judgment and want to be 'part of the ride.' I understand the dilemma, as folks who have contributed funds were presented with the parking-related solutions, which clearly represent a HUGE opportunity. However, as this cannabis opportunity appears to have fallen in your/our laps, and if you believe your precious time & resources should be shifted in that direction, this is a POSITIVE event, and should be communicated as such to investors. What I, as an investor, would NOT want to have happen, is for you to shift in this direction without being a part of it, or at least without being made whole on my $25K. Perhaps you give investors a choice:
a) We (Asad and Paul) have recently come across a compelling opportunity in the cannabis trade, where there appears to be an immediate need & strong interest for the payment processing solutions that Parkimon has developed. Accordingly, we wanted to inform investors that we will, in the weeks/months ahead, shift our focus to this opportunity, as we believe it provides a more immediate opportunity to implement Parkimon's technology and enhance the Parkimon's value for its investors. Note that we still plan to roll-out the Parkimon parking/payment solutions, but the timing may be delayed, as we need to focus our attention on the opportunity in cannabis. As an investor, if you are comfortable with this change, then no need to respond.
b) If you are not comfortable with this shift, then Parkimon will redeem your note at 100% of the C-note (contribution) value.

2) Regarding the Parkimon web-site, I suggest two things:
a) Add a Mission Statement at the very top of the web-page, perhaps something like this: "Mission Statement: Parkimon will revolutionize the parking industry through its proprietary payment processing and parking applications. Over the past six years, Parkimon's engineers have developed technology solutions that are ready to be implemented. We are currently in discussions with a number of municipalities and private parking owners to roll-out our solutions. Please view the below short videos below to learn more about these opportunities."
b) I suggest moving the three videos (Benefit for the Operator, Benefit for the Consumer, Benefit for the Enforcer) right below the Mission Statement i.e. to the top of the web page. Upon a more careful look at the web-site, there is a lot of rich information, but I think it can be presented in a more user friendly way. We should also mention our COMPETITIVE EDGE: Parkimon is the only parking solution that has patent pending payment process that enables consumers to pay operators by phone. To learn about additional solutions, please see XXX on this web page.
Well, that's my two cents, I will let the web consultants advise on the rest!

Raj

subject: the need for engineers
body: Hi Raj, Once again thank you for hosting us. You had a question regarding why we still have /and or need engineers although the platform is supposedly “finished”? Thought I’d answer in detail. Once you finish a software project, it’s never exactly “finished” per se. It goes through a number of releases and phases. Alpha, Beta, etc. development, testing, integration, deployment, implementation, operations and maintenance…You work on the bugs, optimize the features and functionality, manage the code, etc. Our platform is not just a simple App. Its’ a complete cloud based enterprise class platform that has to sync with not just the consumer Apps but enforcers, adjudication (courts), DMV, etc. Everything has to sync with each other perfectly. We have no way of knowing what a client will use for a browser or what operating system they will be using. No way to know which of the thousands of phone models or operating system versions the consumers will be using --nor have any control over them. There is literally a ton of code to manage across all the platforms we are deploying to. The web, the iPhone, the Android …literally thousands of variations. Now that the platform is developed, functional testing like integration testing, system testing and acceptance testing will need to done as well as non-functional testing which by itself is crazy coz it includes: security testing, reliability testing, usability testing, efficiency testing, baseline testing, compliance testing, load testing, performance testing, scalability testing, stress testing, recovery testing….. get the picture We have to manage all that code, fix the bugs in near real-time and optimize our systems to not only work fast on the street but off the street. If it wasn’t for the payments modules I had already developed in my Ebanqo platform since 2010, this project would have taken at least 2 years and a few million dollars to where we are at today. To give you an example. Facebook is a “finished” product right? Works every day for millions of people- right? Then why does FB employ over 9000 developers and what are they doing? Behind the scenes, they are fixing bugs, optimizing, managing code and all of the above. That’s what. You get “updates” and patches from Microsoft every other week or month right.. FYI, those are bug fixes and new code to optimize an existing build. If Microsoft needs to constantly fix it’s code then …well I hope you now understand why I still need my guys continuing to work on our platform. Now my 9 core developers are senior full stack developers with over 15 years of experience each. ONE full stack engineer in the Bay area can make over $180K! See https://www.indeed.com/salaries/Senior-Software-Engineer-Salaries,-San-Francisco-Bay-Area-CA For the price of LESS than ONE we have 9! Anyway, while we can start going out and selling our platform, I need to have them continue doing their thing on a daily basis as while I have tried to test as much as I can - I am just one person and we have not yet deployed the platform under production environments with a lot of users. Rest assured, it will work fine as I have deployed payment systems that were doing millions of transactions and scaled lightning fast but Parking is new to me too and in payments there were not that many parts that needed to sync in real-time. Also as soon as we get a client, we may require customization and support. As such I cannot let my guys go or tell them to wait without pay until I get a client. Another problem is that code is like a language. Folks may speak the same language but in “different accents” and understanding someone else’s style takes time. The time lost can also turn into an opportunity lost as a potential customer is not going to wait for us to “train” a newbie if we let the current ones go. I hope the above helped in understanding the continuous need for engineers. Kind regards Asad

subject: Due diligence checklist
body:
Asad,

We have formally contracted with Edward Strickland and attached is a checklist of documents he will need prior to commencing the interviews, for which he is ready to go. Pillsbury, Madison and Sutro will be handling the IP portion, but Edward will collect the background information and their requisite needs prior to their involvement in the interest of time and costs.

The anticipated outcome of this engagement with Edward will be twofold: 1) to document and determine a valuation for the company/IP and 2) will also produce a 50-70 page “book” which will be utilized as due diligence material for either VC funding, an acquisition or outright sale of the technology platform. This is a format Edward has used in other cases and it is apparently standard fare.

While the checklist list is substantial, it is important that there is a master document to assist us going forward. I will put you directly in contact with Edward when you have the documents together to set your interview schedule. Edward would like the opportunity to have an initial meet and greet with you and hear your Parkimon sales pitch, after which you can coordinate your schedules for the remaining interviews. The completion of this document is estimated to be mid-December, but we expect to ascertain enough information from the initial interviews in order to procure additional funding to restart the company as Steven has urged.

Please let me know when you feel these items can be pulled together. Also, if you can email me the NDA I will push it out to the parties to be ratified prior to you handing over any documents.

As it relates to your earlier correspondence regarding the C-note, James Hui suggested modifying your original note. Are you able to do that yourself so we can start pushing this out to investors who have expressed interest in participating so far or does that need to be completed by WSGR?

Thank you for your cooperation.

Regards,
Sally

subject: Updated version of the due diligence checklist, please use this one to print
body:
THE Gbiz Inc.,

AN INDUSTRY STANDARD

DUE DILIGENCE CHECKLIST

FOR THE

PARKIMON INC., c/o SALLY THORNBER

COMPANY ASSET EVALUATION

Corporate Documents

ü Articles of Incorporation.
ü The Company’s current Bylaws.
ü The Company’s operating agreements.
ü The Shareholder agreements.
ü The Original Minutes of Board of Directors and Shareholder meetings.
ü All shareholder actions and meeting minutes.
ü All board actions and meeting minutes.
ü All documents furnished to shareholders and directors.
ü Certificates from all states, countries, and jurisdictions where the company does business.
ü The Company’s current Certificate of Incorporation/Articles of Incorporation, including any amendments thereto.

Capital Stock, any previous and any current Securities issuance

ü Current shareholder and optionee lists, including issuance dates and original issuance price. These lists should include any party who owns securities of the Company or has any rights regarding securities of the Company.
ü A summary of the vesting schedules of any stock or options subject to vesting.
ü Agreements relating to the purchase, repurchase, sale or issuance of securities.
ü Agreements relating to voting of securities and restrictive share transfers.
ü Agreements relating to preemptive or other preferential rights to acquire securities, including rights of first refusal.
ü Agreements relating to registration rights.
ü Shareholder agreements not involving the Company, including any agreement by a shareholder relating to the sale, voting, or transfer of securities of the Company.
ü Evidence of qualification or exemption under applicable federal and state blue sky laws for issuance or transfer of the Company’s securities.
ü Copies of stock certificates, warrants and option agreements.
ü Complete Stockholder contact information.
ü Number of outstanding shares, dates of issuance, and percent ownership.
ü All outstanding preferred stock, including covenants.
ü All outstanding options, warrants or convertible securities.
ü Employee stock benefit programs; stock options, stock purchases or other promises.

Financial Information

ü Audited financial statements since the Company’s inception.
ü Income statements, balance sheets, cash flow statements.
ü Records of all changes in equity position.
ü Accounting methods and practices.
ü Company prepared monthly or quarterly statements.
ü A three-year budget and financial projections.
ü A complete and current business plan.
ü Accounts receivable aging.
ü Accounts payable aging.

Product or Services pricing plans and policies

ü Revenue and gross margins by product.
ü Revenue and gross margins by service.
ü Income details.
ü Expense details
ü Explanation of any material write-downs or write-offs.
ü A summary of all bad debt.
ü Details of any outstanding contingent liabilities.
ü Accountant report on the company's financial condition.

Tax Status

ü Federal and state income tax returns for the last three years.
ü Detail of any tax audits.

Contracts and Agreements

ü List of Bank lenders.
ü List of and non-Bank lenders.
ü Joint venture and partnership agreements.
ü License agreements.
ü Purchase agreements.
ü Liens, equipment leases, mortgages or any other outstanding loans.
ü Insurance contracts and agreements.
ü Contracts with suppliers, vendors, and customers.
ü Any additional agreements or contracts relevant to the business of the company.

Governmental Regulations

ü Copies of all permits and licenses.
ü Copies of reports made to government agencies.
ü Detail of any inquiries made by any local, state or federal agencies.

Products and Services

ü Detail of product offering including market share by product line.
ü Inventory analysis including turnover, obsolescence and valuation policies. Report analysis by product line including analysis of any issues.
ü List of all major suppliers including dollar amount purchased per year.

Sales and Marketing

ü List of competitors and detail of market share.
ü List of major clients.
ü Analysis of pricing strategy.
ü Current electronic marketing structure.
ü All and any other marketing materials.
ü Sales commission structure.
ü Sales projections by product line.
ü Any pertinent marketing studies conducted by outside parties.

Management and Personnel

ü Management organizational chart.
ü Bios of senior personnel.
ü Bios of the engineers.
ü The details of any labor disputes.
ü Employee compensation plans including pension, options, profit sharing, deferred compensation and retirement.
ü Management incentive plans including pension, option, profit sharing, deferred compensation, retirement and any non-cash compensation.
ü Employee Confidentiality Agreements.
ü Listing of any Consulting Agreements.
ü Number of employees, turnover, absentee problems and hiring projections. Employee HR, benefits, and insurance manuals.
ü List of Company's Directors.
ü Investigation report on all principals, managers, and directors.
ü Credit history report on all principals, managers, and directors.
ü Resume verification on all principals, managers, and directors.

Property and Equipment

ü An appraisal of all equipment and fixed assets.
ü List of all real property owned by the company
ü Copies of titles, mortgages, and deeds of trust.
ü Detail of any easements or other encumbrances.
ü Leases and sub-leases.
ü Company space expansion plans.
ü Patents, trademarks and other intangible assets.

Research and Development

ü Detail all research and development in progress.
ü Commercial analysis of R&D efforts.
ü Documentation policies including examples.

Company Media Information

ü Copies of all past and planned company press releases.
ü Existing articles relating to the company and its industry
ü Company newsletters and any investor relations material

Legal Information Required Before Meeting With The IP Attorney

LEGAL AND REGULATORY

ü All documents and correspondence relating to any pending litigation, threatened litigation, or disputes which could potentially lead to litigation involving the Company, its executive officers or its directors.
ü Any consent decrees, injunctions, judgments, other decrees or orders, settlement agreements or similar matters.
ü All correspondence between the Company and all and any Government Regulatory Administration in any country.

INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

ü Form of Proprietary Information and Invention Agreements signed by past or present employees and consultants. Any documentation relating to the transfer to the Company or any employee of any technology.
ü Copies of the Proprietary Information and Invention Agreements signed by key employees and consultants.
ü A list of any employees or consultants who have not signed Proprietary Information and Invention Agreements, including a list any periods of time where key employees or consultants performed services for the Company while not bound by such agreements.
ü Copies of any Proprietary Information and Invention Agreements that contain any exclusions from assignment to the Company. Any licenses or agreements of any kind with respect to the Company’s or others’ patent, copyright, trade secret or other proprietary rights, proprietary information or technology, other than licenses or agreements pertaining to “off-the-shelf” or standard products.
ü A list of patents, copyrights and trademarks, and any searches relevant to such items that have been done.

LITIGATION

ü Description of any current litigation including potential damages.
ü Description of any potential litigation including potential damages.
ü Settlement documentation.

MANAGEMENT, EMPLOYEES AND CONSULTANTS CONTRACTS

ü Any agreements, understandings or proposed transactions between the Company and any of its employees, officers, directors, affiliates, or any affiliate thereof, including without limitation, employment agreements, indemnification agreements and any loans or guarantees.
ü Employee benefit and profit-sharing plans, including stock option, stock purchase deferred compensation and bonus plans or arrangements and forms of stock option grants or stock purchase agreements under such plans.
ü A list of officers and directors. If any officers are not currently devoting 100% of their business time to the Company, please note them on this list.
ü A list and breakdown of all outstanding accrued salary, paid time off, and reimbursable expenses.
ü Copies of offer letters.
ü Consulting contracts.

DEBT FINANCING

ü All debt instruments and credit agreements entered into by the Company, including lease financing, which are currently in effect.
ü Any guarantees of third-party obligations.

OTHER AGREEMENTS

ü Real property leases, including any amendments and all exhibits to such documents.
ü Capital Lease Agreements.
ü Standard forms of agreements used by the Company.
ü Joint venture and partnership agreements.
ü Management, service and marketing agreements.
ü Confidentiality and nondisclosure agreements.
ü Agreements requiring consents or approvals in connection with the financing.
ü Agreements, letters of intent or term sheets regarding any proposed acquisition by the Company of any company, business or material amount of assets.
ü Any other agreements material to the business of the Company, or outside the ordinary course of business.

MISCELLANEOUS

ü Any reports and studies prepared by the Company or outside consultants on the Company or its subsidiaries’ or affiliates’ business or financial condition.
ü Most recent audited financial statements and latest unaudited monthly financial statements.
ü Brief description of any contract restricting the ability of the Company to compete in any line of business with any person or entity, or committing the Company or any subsidiary to continue in any line of business.
ü Most recent Business Plan, Offering Memorandum or similar document of the Company.
ü Any documents indicating environmental liabilities or potential environmental liabilities, including violations of any environmental laws, or agreements for indemnity or remediation of any adverse environmental conditions.
