subject: Re: An update from the first meeting with Asad
body: If I can’t trust you at the start, I doubt I can going forward. Typical Paul’s play as he has done this before. Control the funds and I will come running. Not anymore. I have had enough of him. He uses and abuses people and is a moocher. Hopefully you will realize that sooner than later. He lies and exaggerates. He has embezzled funds, is a financial, operational and legal liability. He has told you that Nina, his sister is not a legal liability? – well let’s put that to the test next week. You think his “network” of friends are going to stand by him through thick and thin and fork over money? Let’s put that to the test too. He has put several of his close investor friends and even his own mother in legal jeopardy because of his stupidity. Once 9 of them are served and the rest get to see and hear the evidence of how senseless I was to succumb to his blackmail, conspiracies and backstabbing, I am sure we will all know where he stands. His idiocy and ignorance is beyond my comprehension. Parkimon is going to be a classic case study of how stupid overcame smart and drove a possible unicorn into the ground. Someone please explain to him - what goes around comes around! Have a good life. A

FYI - I don’t see much reason to continue on without a full outside evaluation of the company and the technology. The opportunity is available to you should you choose to take this route. For me further funding is dependent on completing this investigation- others may feel differently.

Sally
Sent from my iPhone

subject: An update from the first meeting with Asad
body:
Sally,

I have sent the checklist to George Willman at Pillsbury Law. I have a better picture of the level of Parkimon's business development. From what I gathered from our first meeting, Asad has engineered an integrated platform using tools from various licensed software. I did not go into the details; however, I have understood the system's operations. Parkimon's platform has not generated any revenue to date.

From our conversation, he would like to raise 1.5 million dollars ($1,500,000) in a Series A Round and also raise a Convertible Note at four hundred thousand dollars ($400,000). He asked me would it be better to raise the four hundred thousand dollars ($400,000) first and then raise the $1.5 million ($1,500,000). I stated, in my opinion, I would raise the $1.5 million ($1,500,000) and have a note of four hundred thousand dollars ($400,000) within the $1.5 million ($1,500,000). He explained the money would be used to implement two pilots to gain user traction. I suggested it should be at a low monetary price to demonstrate the scale of growth of both the users and revenue.

With the possible success of the two pilots, he believes he could have the potential to gain 10 million dollars ($10,000,000) in a Series B round. The money would develop the full implementation of a winner amidst the market within its industry.

There are some issues regarding the structure of the business concerning its current debt to the existing noteholders. Asad is concerned about how to maneuver this assignment into paying off the current investors. I have asked George look into structuring the current debt into a new term agreement. We will wait and see what George has to say.

Also, from my first meeting with Asad, I see some potential in his development, and at the same time, I see some weaknesses as well as threats to his current business model, beyond the fact that limited funds are available for any further activities. There could be a meeting-of-the-minds to find a possible solution to the present business model intricacies and capitalize this to move forward.

I have corresponded to George to set up a meeting with him. I hope that he can help with a solution to the company's structure and determine if Parkimon's integrated system has any merit for an IP valuation.

Sincerely,

Edward

subject: ssFW: just you and i paul
body: Here is the “executive summary” of my presentation for the investors. My apologies beforehand for the rated -R content. Once you read to the end, you’ll understand why I lose it with him. If you get to read this email first and are confused as to why you got this, please refer to my last email.

subject: just you and i paul
body: Paul, Call me human but here is the situation and reality as it is relevant to the both of us. Right or wrong, it doesn’t make a difference. I want you to understand this last statement. Right or wrong – it doesn’t make a difference. It is how I see it and that is why it is relevant. BTW, I remember when you first initiated the conversation about equity. You were at my sliding door and then took your shirt off and sat down in our backyard and we started talking about equity. I distinctly remember that I offered 10% and also justified it with the time, money and IP that I had put in. We went back and forth and ended up with "let’s table it for later". We eventually agreed on the 20% over time. Anyway, that’s history. Going forward, I want you to understand and comprehend the relevancy of my stance and my decision as it relates to you and where I am coming from. I reiterate, right or wrong. Doesn’t make a difference. ON A PROFESSIONAL LEVEL (and I have hard evidence to support every point I have made below): 1. Every consultant and staff that you have brought in has cost the company money with ZERO RESULTS. You know all of their names. Not a single person has contributed anything in terms of results. I think you will agree to that. 2. Your intermittent fund raising skills have cost the company human and monetary losses to the tune of over $380K - not counting the millions in opportunity costs in terms of time wasted in development, sales and the time I spent on damage controlling your bullshit. 3. You have absolutely NO technical sales skills when it comes to follow-up or closing anything related to tech. So VP or whatever of sales and marketing is a laughable concept. 4. You are a legal liability – and you know TWO reasons why- I have many more instances (more on that later) 5. You have embezzled funds and put your personal needs over who is important and when and as such are a financial liability. (there are many instances of this) 6. You have demonstrated total disregard and your own personal greed over what is in the best interest of your investors - your own friends AND company. That shows your true character (I have two witnesses to the above besides a lot more.) The above points are on a professional level. Below are on a personal level. A. I rue the day I met you as you have wasted years of my life. Had I not met you, I would have eventually found VCs or some super angel who had enough sense to look for traction rather than revenues AND got together a team to help me. B. Out of the 64 engineers that we had to rotate because we never had enough funds to pay them on time, I have lost 23 who I used to call my good friends. I knew their wives and husbands the names of their kids. I used to be welcomed at their homes. Literally wiz kids who I had personally hired and known for years and years. You destroyed all those relationships. C. There have been a number of instances of pure selfishness and total lack of basic human empathy by you. One example. I told you once I have not seen my kids for a long time, and you said, and I quote: “that’s your choice”. NO Paul, that was never my fucking choice!! Had it been my choice, I would have had enough money to bring the girls over! A modicum of a decent salary would have helped! Another example. You have the balls to negotiate what is already owed to me?? To tell me that you’ll give me this and that if I resign?? Who the fuck do you think you are?? Did you seriously think that by dangling what is already owed to me, I would be willing to hand over MY IP, MY company to a stupid stupid bitch like you? D. But what really really upsets me and makes the bile rise in my throat every time I think of you is that Mariam, my 17 year old got a full 100% scholarship from MIT (I had told you about that) but just because I could not afford food and lodging in the Boston area, I have had to stick her in a shitty university in Istanbul. I would like you to visualize -if you can – the father -daughter conversation I had with her when I had to tell her that her genius, her passion, her efforts and dreams are all going away because her father cannot afford her food and fucking lodging in Boston!!. I want you to scroll to the bottom of this email and see the 17 year old girl who's life your actions affected and screwed with. Your fucking fund raising skills and requirements to fund further from JUST ME while you went to your fucking ball games!! Your need for revenues. Your bullshit, your conspiracies….. I hope her face is ingrained in your subconscious and every time you go to your son’s ball game, pick up that basketball, see your daughter or look at yourself in the mirror, Mariam’s face pops up and you feel as disgusted with yourself as I do when I see MY face AND yours! E. CALL ME HUMAN, BUT given the above, “I can’t stand the sight of you” would be an understatement of the century! Paul, I was going to dissolve the company and it is my fiduciary duty to let everyone know as to WHY I am dissolving the company. I went through over 4000 emails and conversations and had enough time to make a video presentation plus link all the documents and communication to that presentation. Rest assured, I have taken 100% of the blame for succumbing to your blackmail, back stabbing, conspiracies with Raj, threats, and a lot more. Concrete evidence validating every sentence - gleaned from over 4000 emails! If you know anything about me, I think you know how meticulous I am about building a case. Just re-read Raj and Michael Calana’s emails again when I shut those fucks down. Paul, many of the emails make you personally liable to the investors and some of them to me. But like I said, I have taken full blame for allowing things to happen on my watch. If and when I dissolve the company the Pandora’s box will be open for everyone to see what an ass I have been for letting you ride me like you did. So thank your stars that it hasn’t come to that -Yet. Going forward, here is my suggestion. You have 20% in the company and have also benefitted monetarily from your association with Parkimon. You need to protect your “interest” but do it from the sidelines please. Let Sally and Steve know in your own way. Justify it whichever way you want. But Paul, I cannot work with you involved upfront and in my face. I just cannot. I lose all perspective and all kinds of intelligence, spatial, cognitive, inter-personal, emotional and everything in between. You’ve turned me into a little kid throwing barbs and darts at you and I hate myself for that. Unlike what you think of me, I don’t have any ego issues Paul, I could have easily sent this email to Sally and Steve and told them I don’t want to work with you but I am giving you that option to stop interfering with your brilliant ideas and just raise funds and do that behind the scenes. Like I said, you have your interest to protect. Rest assured I will do what is in the best interest of the company and the investors but I can’t be doing it with you in my face. Like I said, call me human. Think hard, about what I have said. This can be a deal breaker but with you on-board or involved in the “team”, the company is not going anywhere and I will have wasted more time than I can afford. That is my take on the situation. Your call. I’m not the type to hold grudges but this is too fresh. I'll move on and hopefully things will change between you and I but for now, You leave me alone and I leave you alone! A Before I forget. Here is Mariam, my daughter – who’s life got screwed up the day I met you! REMEMBER HER FACE AND I HOPE IT HAUNTS YOU EVERY DAY!

subject: response to avi
body: Michael, Please see my comments inline in red.

subject: RE: Parkimon
body:
Here’s a potential draft email for everyone’s consideration – please let me know your thoughts.

Avi:

I have reviewed all the questions below, and while I understand they may have some impact on your thinking, I can say that in my experience I see many situations where over time people’s initial expectations are not met (or they did not understand how these types of commitments work), further funding is required and parties become fatigued. Since I was not involved in the Company until recently, I don’t have specific answers to all the questions, but I can say that having now discussed the matter with the group and believe there is a path forward.

All of the engaged parties believe that there is value in this technology and that it needs to be taken to the next step; further, we believe the future animosity can be resolved to support a good forward plan. As you may know, the platform has previously been demonstrated, but when the funding ran out, it needed to be hibernated, and the existing infrastructure terminated. As such, while I can understand your skepticism, there is technology here. Further, I believe we can proceed under the framework of terms you provided, but we need to understand some specifics, including:

- Investment: you propose to invest $300k into the Company, but I did not see the pre-money valuation for such an investment. Can you please clarify? (Lets just keep it very very simple so there is no confusion: “What percentage equity do you want for your investment and how do you justify that percentage (evaluate the company)?

- Funding: currently the Company is not operating so how do you envision the operations to continue and payments handled?

Asad has agreed to re-engage a technology team, but it would be helpful to know how you would like to coordinate with that team such that you are comfortable that the code is secured and ongoing development can continue? Please take this part out. I can’t make a decision until I get the terms from Avi

Marketing: since the Company currently has no marketing resources, how do you envision this being handled, who will manage this and how will payments handled?

Again, we believe there is great opportunity here for you, and would like to push this forward, so please advise on the next steps.

Regards,

Michael Hogan
ArmaninoLLP
408 240 4908 direct | 858 353 7365 cell

subject: Parkimon
body: Mr. Hogan, I am resending this as I realized after I sent that I did not attach the other two gentlemen to the prior email. As I will reiterate, I was very much excited about buying the platform when it was first presented but the delay from Parkimon side gave me time to re-think and there are many questions and worries now in my mind. There is animosity between the only two shareholders (Paul and Asad) WHY? What am I paying $1.68M for? What am I buying? A piece of paper, software code? That I cannot see, nor understand, that is not working now? What happens if the founder and engineer of this technology is run over by a bus? What do I do with the code that I cannot even understand? The platform has a lot of potential, YET the investors now want their money back and are not even willing to get it reactivated so a buyer can see it. WHY? They expect that someone will pay money for air? WHY? I don’t even know if the technology exists. I have not seen it and you are expecting me to buy something which I have not seen nor checked nor done any due diligence on it. WHY? Paul is threatening the founder and only person who knows anything about the technology with FBI and suing him. And all the investors are okay with that and expect me to buy a company that has legal problems as well! WHY? The investors want me to buy and to get their money out but are not willing to help themselves to come up with a product that can be seen and even put up the small amount to get the platform up and running. WHY? Why should I bail out these kinds of investors? Do I look like the sacrificial lamb to all the investors? Asad wants me to buy because he believes it will be a dream. It looks more like nightmare to me. Under these circumstances, I am not interested to go forward with the deal, however, I am willing to give it a chance provided my following terms are agreeable. TERMS: I will invest in the company for a total of $300K in two parts, I want equity for that investment NOT C-Notes. The first $150K will go to activate the technology and improve it as Asad suggested he could to make it more valuable. This benefits everybody. The second tranche of $150K will go to market the platform. As you know, I met with Asad to get an idea about the technology. He tried to convince me that the technology will work, but again, nothing is proven yet. He also told me that improvements can be made to the technology to make it even better. I don’t have the engineers to do this and take it to next level, so I will need Asad going forward and not just to reactivate the platform. I hope you will be able to convince him to do that. That leads to my next point. Asad and Paul will need to sit together across the table and resolve their differences and both will need to sign off on waivers. ( I will discuss with my attorney what those should be) as I am not willing to take any risk with legal problems hanging over the company, nor do I want to get caught in the middle of war between them that might freeze the company in its tracks. Also, as I said above, I need Asad to do the technology. I can hire engineers, but it would take time for them to learn the platform before they can do anything with it. Time is money. I am told that Steve Pinetti has a lien on the Orangecone technology. I need to be sure that he won’t exercise his lien, then I have just invested in nothing. The only chance that this will work is that the technology (the only asset) is in place so that it can put the company in a position to be sold or profitable. If you are able to get him to sign some sort of document to guarantee that he won’t take the technology before we have a chance to make the company profitable, then I will come in. You can let Steve know that he will be paid in full with interest once the company makes enough money through either profits or a sale. Once the company has reactivated the platform and we start marketing it, the day the company makes its first dollar, I will be willing to buy the C-Note PLUS interest (till that day) of any investor who wants out. If ALL of these above terms are met, I will invest, otherwise I will not. Mr. Hogan, if you can facilitate this deal as per above, I will be willing to pay your hourly fee also. I have copied this email to Paul and Asad the only shareholders I know off. You can reach out to Steve Pinetti yourself. As I like to keep my funds in circulation, this offer is valid till November 10th 2020. Sincerely Avi Cohen

subject: avi call
body:
Michael, Steve,

I spent some time after the call thinking about our conversation. Again, based on the email we received, I think Avi was venting about the whole situation, so it isn't necessary to answer each question. So whether he was venting or justifying himself for a bigger grab, all is irrelevant as he is the only potential buyer right now and can lay whatever terms he wants and that’s the unfortunate reality. Still, we can't disregard his feelings since he could be our only way out.

As far as the next step, I think we need to find out what he specifically wants. In his email he mentioned equity, but we don't know how much equity he wants. And as Steve pointed out, we don't know what his vision is for the company, so we will need to know that. You should send him an email asking about his specific terms, including the equity issue.

I feel that it is best that you respond to his email via email only instead of calling him so that his answers will all be in writing and there is a paper trail of his commitments. Once you get that, you and Steve could give him a follow-up call or whatever. What do you think?
Best
Asad

subject: Re: F^W: Parkimon..
body:
Asad
We did and are crafting a response to Avi that will be sent tomorrow.

Steve

subject: Re: Parkimon
body:
Michael
If you haven't sent the email to Avi yet, please copy Steve also as Avi said you have to reach out to him, plus Steve is a critical part of the deal. Also I would not suggest waiting too long to send it. You never know what else Avi would cook up given more time. Let's keep the momentum going.
Best
A
Sent Via Phone

subject: ~RE: parkimons response to raj kohli's proposed solution
body:
I do not agree to your proviso. For obvious reasons we would like to return your investment in full with interest ASAP. However, it is subject to the availability of funds. We will work on a repayment plan though. I believe that is reasonable and logical. I need you to acknowledge this after which please do not communicate with me.

A word of sincere advice Raj. Please refrain from any more “advice” or mention of this with any of Parkimon’s officers or investors. Your honor is safe with me and I will not speak of it, but if I hear anything about it from anyone, all bets are off and I will be duty bound to protect mine. I wish you well and just wish it had not come to this.

Asad U. Shah – PhD.
Founder & CEO
Tel : +1 415 515 6415
Email> aus@parkimon.com
LinkedIn > in/asadushah
Web > http://www.parkimon.com

body:
Hi Asad, 
One important proviso: I must be repaid by December 31, 2018, and Paul 
will determine the timing of my repayment, in order for our below to be 
effective. Good luck! 
Raj

Hello Asad, I agree to your offer to take back my "$40K investment with interest when Parkimon has the spare funds". Once I have my $40K returned, I agree to forfeit the 6% equity interest that you previously promised, and will - once my $40K is returned - have no association with Parkimon. Peace, my friend - I really wish you the best of luck moving forward. Regards, Raj

Steve Pinetti
Sent from my iPhone

I think you need to have a conversation with him or he is going down. I am willing to sacrifice ALL my shares as the reality is that we have NOTHING right now. This is our only chance to get the platform up and running. Once that happens, it will be saleable or acquirable or whatever and everyone gets something out of it and more instead of a shave or NOTHING. He has already been paid for his services. subject: Re: R~..E: Parkimon
body:
Ok
Everyone needs to chill here.

Sounds like James Cutler won’t be involved as Avi is going to handle staffing the needed people to make it all work - that's how I am interpreting his words.
Asad has the responsibility to get the platform up to 2.0 speed. Avi can have someone else validate the new and improved platform when the time comes.

Paul, you're right Avi is making demands and he will continue to do so - as he can - as would you or I - because he knows he has us by the balls.

So with no other buyers we need to play through this situation.

If you and Asad are willing to give up your ownership interests we can keep going. Company might own the IP but you two own the company.

Need both to say yes now to giving your ownership over to Avi so Michael can move forward.

Please just respond here and stop emailing — let's enjoy our Friday night.

Steve

Asad,

You have been told by my attorney to stop the harassment and slanderous remarks towards me. I will forward this email to further support my allegations of your misconduct, which is actionable.

I am including this response to my attorney for the record. Please stop this behavior.

Paul

subject: Re: Parkimon
body: Ok so then you are killing the deal with Avi if Michael passes that message along. That ok?? Also means we will need money (where would that come from ?) for Asad to refresh the platform to 2.0 status for James to evaluate and then what? Steve

Michael-

I'm not interested in selling my stock for $1. Avi has set 3 deadlines that have all come and gone and has not monied up. I would like for Asad to provide the software as promised to the Parkimon attorney, Micahel Stebbins, so that it can be evaluated by James Cutler's people who will do so at no cost. Once completed we can decide next steps.

Best-

P-

subject: Re: Parkimon
body:
Mr. Hogan,

At this point, the company has NO net worth. It has NO money and the only asset it had was technology that cannot be demonstrated. Pre-money valuation is ZERO. If company is wind up, the investors and shareholders get nothing. I am investing in fact that it MAY be worth something once the technology is reactivated, either by Dr. Asad or whatever other team I choose. In fact, I want to make sure this company succeeds to guarantee that my money not only is protected but will make money. I will therefore want 100% equity so I can headhunt the right people from CEO to marketing to finance and whoever else is required to incentivize them and run the company and make it success. I do not want any of the fighting shareholders involved in company in any way nor any legal problems from them. Again, if I put money in the company, I will make sure that the money isn't wasted. I will have competent people assisting me with the day to day operations.

To your second point. You need to ask your lawyer about that. A director is needed to open Parkimon account and make necessary agreements for transfer of shares and other things I mentioned before. Once that is done, I will deposit funds and take over and do what is needed.

For your last point. Up to this point the company has not generated anything of value. Since I am putting in money, I will not waste it by not operating the company inefficiently; I want to protect my investment. I will have a marketing plan after consulting and hiring professionals.

I have another project that I am interested in so you need to make decision by the 15th of November.

Sincerely
Avi Cohen

The investors want me to buy and to get their money out but are not willing to help themselves to come up with a product that can be seen and even put up the small amount to get the platform up and running. WHY? Why should I bail out these kinds of investors? Do I look like the sacrificial lamb to all the investors? Asad wants me to buy because he believes it will be a dream. It looks more like nightmare to me. Under these circumstances, I am not interested to go forward with the deal, however, I am willing to give it a chance provided my following terms are agreeable. TERMS: I will invest in the company for a total of $300K in two parts, I want equity for that investment NOT C-Notes. The first $150K will go to activate the technology and improve it as Asad suggested he could to make it more valuable. This benefits everybody. The second tranche of $150K will go to market the platform. As you know, I met with Asad to get an idea about the technology. He tried to convince me that the technology will work, but again, nothing is proven yet. He also told me that improvements can be made to the technology to make it even better. I don’t have the engineers to do this and take it to next level, so I will need Asad going forward and not just to reactivate the platform. I hope you will be able to convince him to do that. That leads to my next point. Asad and Paul will need to sit together across the table and resolve their differences and both will need to sign off on waivers. ( I will discuss with my attorney what those should be) as I am not willing to take any risk with legal problems hanging over the company, nor do I want to get caught in the middle of war between them that might freeze the company in its tracks. Also, as I said above, I need Asad to do the technology. I can hire engineers, but it would take time for them to learn the platform before they can do anything with it. Time is money. I am told that Steve Pinetti has a lien on the Orangecone technology. I need to be sure that he won’t exercise his lien, then I have just invested in nothing. The only chance that this will work is that the technology (the only asset) is in place so that it can put the company in a position to be sold or profitable. If you are able to get him to sign some sort of document to guarantee that he won’t take the technology before we have a chance to make the company profitable, then I will come in. You can let Steve know that he will be paid in full with interest once the company makes enough money through either profits or a sale. Once the company has reactivated the platform and we start marketing it, the day the company makes its first dollar, I will be willing to buy the C-Note PLUS interest (till that day) of any investor who wants out. If ALL of these above terms are met, I will invest, otherwise I will not. Mr. Hogan, if you can facilitate this deal as per above, I will be willing to pay your hourly fee also. I have copied this email to Paul and Asad the only shareholders I know off. You can reach out to Steve Pinetti yourself. As I like to keep my funds in circulation, this offer is valid till November 10th 2020. Sincerely Avi Cohen

subject: Re: parkimon and paul
body:
Calling Paul now
Steve Pinetti
steve@pinettiway.com
415 - 279 - 4319

Sound advice Steve: “Everyone needs to chill here.” Kind of hard to do after what Avi pulled out of his hat but we need to rationally consider what he is presenting to us.

I have put in 4 years of my life, heart and soul, late nights, and thousands of hours at the cost of relationships - family, friends, engineers and have nothing to show for it. Our bank records will show that I was not financially compensated during that time. Paul, you on the other hand have benefitted.

Thought I’d step back and analyze the situation objectively before the effects of the chilling wear off. Went through the emails of our exchange yesterday and I believe it is important to set Paul’s misconceptions straight - once and for all. Steve, I’ll need your help there to explain it again to him so that he recognizes we need to do what is best for all the people that are hoping for an exit plan where they can realize some return on their investments. Paul, I don’t think you would want to explain why we lost another opportunity that would benefit them. I know I don’t, especially when I am prepared to do everything I can to get this deal done. Won’t you do the same Paul?

Paul, we’ve had our share of animosity and bitterness and it’s time to end that and figure out how to do what’s in the best interest of our friends and investors. So, allow me to explain a few things:

1. You want me to provide the software to attorney Michael Stebbins, so that it can be evaluated by James Cutler's people? Who does he represent? I know the company has not hired him, so he doesn’t represent the company. Frankly, the company cannot afford to hire him. The company doesn’t have any money, remember? In fact, the company owes me money for all the work I have done. Again, it can be clearly shown that I am the only one who hasn’t gotten paid for the time and effort put in.

2. I don’t say this in any conceited or arrogant or egotistical way but the reality that you Paul need to understand is that as the majority shareholder, I am the only officer, director and architect of Parkimon. Parkimon is ME. As the majority shareholder I can take certain actions at any time – that is, appoint myself as Chairman, director and president at any time. Please feel free to ask your lawyer friends about this. That is a legal fact that you need to comprehend. Given that fact:

3. Only the president /director and officer of the company makes decisions on behalf of the company, that includes who you hire as an attorney. Once hired, the attorney's responsibility is to represent the company not a shareholder not the creditors not anybody but the company and at the direction AND discretion of the president of the company.

4. Michael Stebbins was never hired as Parkimon’s attorney.

5. Even if Michael is hired as the attorney for the company he has no right or reason to take custody of the code nor can he tell me I have to send it over to this James guy. On the contrary he must advise the company (me) on what is in the best interest of the company – having advised, it is up to me to make a final decision.

6. Now let us look at James Cutler or whoever YOU think I should give the code /IP to. Guess what Paul, NO court in the world can force me to give the code/IP to any third party UNLESS THAT PARTY HAS PAID FOR IT!!!

7. As just a shareholder and a minority one at that, you have no legal rights to direct where the code, should or can go. WE as shareholders can vote on what we should do with the code, and I am the majority shareholder. I will not make a decision that would jeopardize the company and the investors.

8. It makes no sense to turn the code over to James Cutler because there is no sale to James Cutler. James has no right to the code right now as he is only a third party who's interested and hasn’t even made an offer nor ever will unless the platform is reactivated, and he can see it. (which brings us right back to square one)

9. Once again: The court cannot force me to turn the code over to a third party unless the party acquires legal rights to it. How do they do that? THEY HAVE TO BUY IT. They can't just say I'm interested, give me the code and then they never give it back or do something to it. In such a scenario, I would have violated my fiduciary duty because handing the code over to someone without payment would be negligence on my part.

10. I hope you get it now. Please stop thinking about UK and other exotic locations that Parkimon can be deployed.

11. Let’s stay focused on doing what’s best for the company and the investors. I know that I can, in good conscience, say that I have done everything I can for the best of the company. Paul, you need to do the same. You should quit fighting against me and instead work with me to get this done.

12. You’ve had a good run Paul, taken funds out of the company, travelled to Dubai and other exotic cities at company expense, played VP of business development and much much more. It is about time that you start putting the company and investors first. (YOUR friends)

13. On 5/03/2020 you committed to giving your shares to anyone who would bring in the funds to reactivate the platform. Here is a link to that part https://youtu.be/rMmL0deuMu0 Time to keep your word and get this monkey off your back Paul. Do what’s right by your friends. For what’s it worth, I’m giving up ALL of my 80% too and not going to get anything (still can’t believe this Avi guy). Anyway, be happy that I get stiffed too.

In conclusion:

Let’s try and end this animosity between us and do what’s right. Let’s move on in life. Talk to Steve and lets get this deal consummated without any more hitches. Let me know by Tuesday latest. I hope you will do what’s right. If not, there are a lot of other people that are going to be affected by your decision, I feel we have no alternative but to let them know by Wednesday what we are going to do about Avi. How would they react if they found out that you were the reason ANOTHER deal died? Let’s get it done!

Best
Asad

subject: FW: parkimon and paul - second request
body: Paul, This is my second attempt to facilitate this deal in the best interest of the company and investors. We need to end this animosity between us and face reality. Here is what we need to face: Avi does not want to inherit us as shareholders as he does not want even a shadow of a legal risk hovering over the company. Put yourself in a buyer’s shoes. Given the situation between the two of us, I am sure you can understand as to why he doesn’t want us. Once the platform is up and running and into revenues (around 4~6 months), he is willing to pay off any of the investors and creditors who wish to exit together with their interest accrued till that time. We can have him put that in writing. Although you agreed to give up your 20% shares on 05/03/2020 in front of a number of witnesses, you are now going back on that and making your cooperation conditional to the following two things: (A) That the source code (IP) should be given to an acquaintance of yours in UK. (B) That Avi keeps to his original offer of paying $1.6M to all the investors. Here is why “A” is not an option and will not happen. (i) Source code once handed over to someone can be easily copied, reverse engineered, modified, and then used. As such it must be protected. (ii) Your acquaintance has not made any offer to purchase or acquire it. (iii) To be fair to him, he cannot and will not make any offer as the platform has been on the shelf for the last one year and cannot be demonstrated. (iv) You are adamant that I give the code to your guy in UK, Why? Do you have something going on the side with him? I don’t know, but your stance is worrisome. (v) It would be a breach of my fiduciary duty and cause harm to the investors and the company if I allow the source code and IP to end up in another country with an unknown person. (vi) Short of it is that it is not happening unless he OR you come up with the money and offer that is better than Avi’s and that deal is then consummated. Regarding point B, Kindly re-read Avi’s email. He has justified reducing his risk very well and come up with several logical and valid points. You can read all of them yourself but reiterating just one should be enough: “They expect that someone will pay money for air? WHY?” He has also said, he is willing to buy out the investors once the company is in revenues. That should take care of your first wish. The harsh reality is that unfortunately for us, we are not in a position to dictate terms and have our “wishes” fulfilled. He is the buyer, the only one and he can easily walk away. No one can force him to cater to your wishes. If this deal dies, it will be on record that this is the 3rd time you will have killed a potential deal because of your ego. I’ve put in 4 years of my life, heart and soul, late nights, and thousands of hours at the cost of relationships - family, friends, engineers…. Just to find out that I will have to give up everything and walk away with nothing. Its hard but reality is what is. Just the fact that the technology will see the light of day, some day- is good enough for me. So let’s get over ourselves, shake hands and do this for the sake of the company and investors. Best Asad

subject: Re: ~RE: Parkimon
body:
This seems to me nothing more than a person who knows there is value in the company, but like most savvy business people, looking to acquire all the companies within Parkimon for pennies on the dollar.

He writes, "What am I paying $1.68M for? What am I buying? A piece of paper, software code? That I cannot see, nor understand, that is not working now?"
If he truly doesn't understand the software & tries to pass it off as something that is not even working and is intangible, then I guess goodwill has little to no value in his mind.
He points out all these issues (most created because of the current standstill you two are engaged in) and/or questions pretty much everything and then offers to buy everything.
But, wants all kinds of restrictions, covenants and limitations - all in his favor, of course.

What this does show is Parkimon is very likely worth more than he is offering. However, it has also allowed warts to spread freely - as he so aptly pointed out a number of them.

Which comes to the meat of the matter. As Rodney King said, "Can't we all get along?" Asad & Paul, because I have only bits & pieces over what you two are locking horns over - it's nearly impossible to offer any sort of specific suggestions to resolve your feud. And, please forgive me if I am speaking not only out of turn, but also inaccurately.
As Mr. Cohen also points out, all the investors are not willing to add more money to Parkimon because the two partners have basically no more funding from us investors - due to their feud. I'm sure both have excellent reasons why they are doing what they are doing. However, every day that goes by - the company's total value declines because their technology becomes less valuable. They've already told their story and what the technology can do to quite a number of entities.

I know I likely speak for most/all investors when I request the following: Can you both please put your differences aside long enough to sell the company? Paul, I'm guessing here, but if you are threatening a lawsuit - which will take years to reach any sort of conclusion - you know you're hurting all of us investors? Even if you win - we all lose. I do believe in your integrity and I do respect that trait in you quite a bit. However, have you thought about this? If you don't make a decision quickly, you will lose me as a future investor and others. If you win a lawsuit, do you honestly think you'll collect anything from Asad? And, what's to keep any of us investors from suing you for not putting us investors interest first? Again, I'm sure you have a number of reasons why you are digging your heels in, but at what expense?

Asad, the same goes for you. And, I'm sorry if I'm speaking out of turn, but what is your responsibility in all of this? If the technology - which you created - is as valuable as it seems - what is keeping you from buying out Paul's equity in the company? Mr. Cohen might go in with you to lend you the money to buy Paul out. But, the longer this goes on, nobody wins. Nobody. Parkimon is and will continue suffering from atrophy until you two mend fences.

All of this stress can't be good on either of you. I'm certain both of you have made mistakes, said things you shouldn't have. And, in the end, you both likely feel you haven't done a goddamn thing wrong. I will absolutely guarantee you it's much closer to 50/50 in terms of who is responsible vs the two of you and your viewpoints of 0/100 & 100/0.

Paul, I've tried reaching out to you a few times, but have not received a reply. If you'd like to explain the situation in detail, I'm all ears. Asad, the same goes for you. If my comment about the daily decline of Parkimon's value is wrong, I'd like to hear why. You both have responsibilities to your investors. I don't agree with letting this standstill continue while all investors' see their original investment slowly disintegrate into nothing, which is exactly what is going to happen if you two can't or won't resolve this mess.

My cell# is (925) 212-5776.

Respectfully,

Russell Wixon & investor

looks like there is still hope. 
Kind regards 
Asad

subject: avinator.com or aviously.com :-)
body:
Hi Avi, 
Paul writes “I’ll wait him out”   Wait for what? me to die?  I had a talk with Steve, Michael Hogan and a few investors and they all think Paul has to go. What kind of guy contacts a potential buyer and tells him that the industry that the company is in is "all but dead"??  He is deliberately trying to sabotage the deal. As you know, I have bent over backwards trying to do what is best for the company, including reaching out to Paul to try to put our differences aside.  Yet he says I am not a team player?  His agenda has always been clear. To kill this deal so that the company dissolves and he can buy the IP and tech for peanuts and sell it.  
However, there is some truth in what he said about parking.. But guess what, the next version that I have envisioned is not just about parking but renting: Cars, bikes, motorcycles, boats, Scooters, RVs, Vans, Trucks …. Since the core technology is all about renting vehicles and driveways, I can easily ADD renting rooms (just like Airbnb) AND let car rentals companies like Avis, Hertz and Budget, etc. also put up their cars on the platform. Also, I can add the API’s of hotels and airlines in there too and it could be the ONE APP for all kinds of travel and mobility! A FULL FLEDGED TRAVEL COMPANY APP! You can call the App AVINATOR or AVIOUSLY   If we get even a little bit of traction, I can bet Airbnb will buy you out if only to shut you out   (BTW, Airbnb charges 15% to guests and something from the host too). You could charge half of that and still make a boat load of money. They are always acquiring companies. Check this link out: https://zippy.digital/here-are-all-the-companies-airbnb-has-acquired-to-help-it-grow-into-a-31-billion-business/   The scale of this is soooo big.  
Anyway, just a thought. I have so many ideas popping that I need to chill   
Jake and I are coming to LA for a business meeting and I hope to see you on Friday?  
Kind regards 
Asad

subject: Parkimon
body:
Avi- 

Right now, because of Covid, the parking industry, as an adjunct of retail, is all but dead. Most companies in the industry are barely hanging on. Fixed costs will drive most, other than the top tier companies, out of business. This could create an opportunity for Parkimon as there are ways to attach the company's capabilities to second tier players looking for a "magic bullet," particularly after Covid lifts. 

In the interim, there are other applications that the technology could be used for. The gentleman who can help is James Cutler of Locatum, locatum.uk.com. Mr. Cutler was referred to me by the CEO of Elgin, James Harris, who was very interested at integrating Parkimon's technology into the back-end of his platform, elgintech.com. (I am still in touch with Harris as he is interested in making a beach-head in the U.S with Elgin.) Harris and Cutler are friends and there may be a way---once Covid lifts---to revisit the once perceived integration play with his company. In the immediacy, though, Cutler has some ideas that might enable the company (Parkimon) to pivot in a different direction in the "geospatial space." Please take a long look at his website. 

The difficulty in all this is getting Asad to become a "team player." He is, in my view, incapable and unwilling to take instruction or even consider suggestion from anyone. His narcissism is what makes him a great engineer---but it also makes it impossible to deal with him from a business standpoint. So the question is: How do you allow him the space to do what he is good at and keep him out of everything else? You let me know if I can help, otherwise, my position will be to wait him out. The company is dormant but can be started up in a week or two by paying a few bills, but the company---not Asad---owns the rights to the technology. 

Best- 

P-

subject: FW: Parkimon -pauls mail to avi
body: Steve, Michael, Since both of you wanted to see the email Paul sent to Avi, I am forwarding it to the both of you. When you review Paul’s email, I trust you will see what I saw, specifically: 1. Paul contacting the buyer (Avi) and telling him that the parking industry is all but dead and that the only value in the technology is if you use it for something else is mind boggling. He is undermining the deal by saying that the industry for which it is made is dead. Why would a buyer go through with a purchase when a shareholder says the technology is for a dead industry, and its only value is maybe if used another way? It's like saying nobody uses widgets anymore but go ahead and buy it and MAYBE a guy I know can use it for something else. And oh by the way, we would have to send it overseas so they can see if they may want it. Talk about killing a deal! 2. This isn't the first time the UK person has been brought up. Remember, we discussed this before. I will not send the codes to someone overseas so that "he can see if it has any value.". What's to stop him from reverse engineering it and then telling us it is worthless while he goes forward without having to pay for it? That would be careless and would jeopardize all who the company owes a fiduciary duty to. Even if we could sue this guy, we would have to go to the UK to enforce a claim against him. That would be costly, and who knows how the UK laws would protect us. I wonder what Paul’s got going on the side with this Brit? Why does he keep insisting that we should give away our only asset?? For audit /validation?? Come on! He trying to cloak something with those words. The platform was up and running, both Orangecone AND Parkimon. I recall sending out an email to all the investors and requesting them to signup and use it and tell their friends. No one bothered except Mary Wong and a few others. 3. He questions me being a "team player". You gents saw the emails I sent to him the other day asking him to shake hands and to work together. He did not even bother to respond. You all know how hard I've worked to get a deal done, not just this one but all the deals in the past. He was in the middle of each deal failing; he was the obstruction.. 4. The email makes his intention very clear. Blow every deal and "wait me out" until he gets his way. Where have you ever seen a minority shareholder trying to do this? I have tried to protect ALL our interests. Does he not see that we all will benefit? He would rather us get nothing while he waits me out? How selfish is that? I tried to be fair and was even willing to talk to Avi about letting Paul keep his 20% while I gave up ALL of mine. But this guy is so intent on screwing me that he cannot see that he is screwing himself and all his friends. I guess this latest act proves your point that he needs to be out of it. I believe you 100% now. As I said on the phone, I don't want anything to do with inciting the other investors. What you do with the email is up to you. I am being transparent with you considering how you have shown a desire to do what is best for everybody. I hope you now see why I say I am just tired of all this. I can't keep coming up with solutions only to have Paul subvert them. Best Asad

subject: ~R..E: Parkimon -pauls mail to avi
body: Steve, Your weekend deadline to Paul is over. Given what’s at stake and what he needs to do together with past actions, I don’t think Paul is going to cooperate. He couldn’t sell the platform when it was live, let alone sell it when it’s sitting on the shelf. I have also given him enough opportunities to do what’s right by the investors. I’m assuming the ABC assignment documentation is also going to take a while and we don’t want to delay this any further or Avi might just go away. If Paul’s group is really interested, they can bid and file their offer with Armanino and take it up with them. What do you suggest we do? Kind regards Asad

subject: Re: Parkimon -pauls mail to avi
body: Paul after every contact you make with Avi he then calls Asad and Michael to ask what the heck is going on at our end and what are the new elements being introduced. Issue is he is now hearing from you Michael and Asad and its too much for him to digest. We all know that letters of credit or deposits is a normal step in such a sale process but nothing about Avi is normal and your jamming him on this point has been his biggest upset. Michael is trying to work out a simple deal with whats in front of us. We need to go back to Michael being single point of contact for Avi. All of us should put ourselves in Avi’s place - he must think we are all crazy at our end. Paul you are trying to get him to do a completely different deal with James at the same time - Michael and Asad are trying for the simplest deal possible. We had agreed we would push through with Avi 1st - then if no go we would then start up a conversation with James. You pushing James beyond a possible CEO role for Avi just pushes this opportunity sideways. In fact your only contact with James should be to do with him what you are doing with Avi - get James to put up 500K and we can then take the best offer? Clearly our side just plain out looks like a bunch on knuckleheads. If I was Avi I would have walked away from us a long time ago - The fact that he has not also tells me, Asad and Michael that Avi has the means and is actually interested. In this context why would we not just do what he wants? We had a simple deal on the table. Asad - Paul - you both have said you would give up your shares to do the deal. -This is what we should have done. in this context Avi then absorbs all payables and rebuilds our platform. Share Holders and note holders get extended We show 100% support and agreement from everyone involved. Done Now Paul will sell for $1 or assign his share on certain conditions, you both now want to be reimbursed via the payables which when we started was not part of the payables but has crept in over the course of the negotiation. Thats ok with me just keeps the payables number moving / growing which is another one of Avi’s key points. If you both could agree we could have had a deal by now. If this is not possible then we go the ABC route - Asad basically assigns the company over to Avi and all note/loan debt is extended - all other payables paid off in the terms of the assignment. Done We need to decide this weekend whats it going to be. I am pretty tired of all this and you all must be feeling the same. Paul I am asking you: - to just forget about James for now or have him out 500K in an account by Wednesday - not call or return Avi’s calls - work strictly through Michael - put your feelings for Avi and Asad aside - go along with the idea of assigning your 20 % to Avi and support doing the simplest deal Paul and Asad you must get onto the same page for this moment - pursue your personal issues after the fact. Asad - appreciate your jumping back in and being willing to interact with Avi and commit yourself to whatever is needed to do the deal. Paul - I am fully aware of your personal feelings about all this - I am not judging them - only asking you put them aside in the interest of your investors. Otherwise, Michael will do his best to do the ABC type deal which is not clean and simple and will be filled with the continued turmoil tand dysfunction hat will probably kill the deal with Avi all-together once and for all. Its just not right that this deal has become a ping pong ball for you guys to bat around publically to potential buyers and the investors. Our current position is the one of Wile E. Coyote - we are running fast but running in a cloud with no foundation and no-where to go but down. Don't you all want to move forward with your lives and get this mess behind us all ASAP. Life is to short and precious for the type of anger, frustration and dysfunction we are putting ourselves and investors through. We need to come together as a unit so we can look professional to Avi to instill some faith in him that this is a good deal and we are all in it with him to make it work. We also need to put on this same face for our investors although it may be too late as they have seen the Asad and Paul letters - so they have confidence in doing additional deals mostly with you Paul in the future. Please respond by email. I am driving to Yosemite now for the coming week with Marcy and I really don’t want to be on calls regarding this deal. I have already said I will go along with whatever it takes to do a deal - just want my attorney to write it up. Steve

Steve-

I agree with you that I should not be in the middle of the negotiations, nor am I standing in the way of making a deal, here. However, I have an obligation to protect the investors and the debt holders via my shares which secure the IP Assignment Agreement TO THE COMPANY and not to Asad, individually. I helped raise this money with the purpose of developing and expanding the IP which has created value for the company and I will not relinquish my shares it until I am assured that all debts are paid and that there is a documented and legal pathway for the C-Note holders to be made whole.

You, however, have recourse, with your lien on Orange Cone, but the other investors do not, so I have to take the role of highest and best for every one involved.

Again, I will relinquish all shares once the debts are paid, including yours, and C-Note holders are made whole. Feel free to call to discuss.

Best-

P-

body:
Asad-

Per your resignation letter dated June 12, 2020 you resigned as CEO and Director of Parkimon, Inc. You no longer have the authority to call a shareholder meeting, nor to reinstate yourself as CEO or Director to take action as a official of the Company.

I am standing by my C-Note holders and debt holders and will take action to support their best interests which includes holding my stock until both groups are satisfied. Steve Pinetti and/or Michael Stebbins will be providing you with instruction in the days ahead.

Paul

The purpose of this meeting will be to ratify proceeding with the Assignment for The Benefit of Creditors previously discussed.
