subject: Re: cancellation of Parkimon shareholders meeting
body: Call me - I believe I have a better solution that could involve Avi-- Steve

To all concerned:

I, as the majority shareholder, have the right to make decisions based on what I think is best for the company. I simply could call a shareholders meeting, conduct a vote, and majority rules. But I’m not going to do that.

Based on the feedback from the emails that I received from some of the investors, all of whom were prompted by Paul to send today, I am canceling the shareholders meeting (which by the way was noticed ONLY to shareholders as required by law). I thought the Assignment for the Benefit of Creditors transaction was the best deal for the investors since as C-note holders, you are all (with the exception of Paul and me) are creditors, all of whom would get paid first before the shareholders received anything. Although I think that is the best way to go, I will concede to your wishes and NOT ratify the ABC agreement, which I think will kill any deal with Avi.

That leaves only the UK deal that Paul and Steve seem eager to pursue. While I don’t agree with their assessment of the UK deal, I will not stand in the way. However, I have expressed my reservations about that deal, and simply don’t think it is in the best interest of the company and investors. Since Paul and Steve think that is the best route, I will go along as long as I get a written waiver from every single investor and shareholder releasing me from any liability and waiving any claims against me and indemnifying me from any damages that may result from pursuing that deal with James Cutler in the UK. I do not want to in any way be involved with that deal. I have expressed my concern about the dangers of turning over our IP /Source code to a party overseas without a signed purchase agreement. I have no desire to have to pursue a claim overseas. While I will not stand in the way of pursuing that deal, I insist on not being held responsible in any way for it.

Up to this point, I have made it clear that I only want what’s best for the company and investors and even reached out to Paul despite our differences. At this point, it appears that all you investors have aligned yourselves with Paul, who is a minority shareholder. I can only wish you all the best of luck and hope that there will be a good ending for you all. As the majority shareholder, I will vote my shares in support as long as I have absolutely no liability exposure whatsoever. If you are all going to drive this company, you should all shoulder the liability.
Kind regards
Asad Shah

subject: ~RE: cancellation of Parkimon shareholders meeting
body: I’ve tried to work things out with Avi twice and both times I’ve had the rug pulled out from under me. I have no interest in going back a 3rd time and looking like a fool. Going forward contact him directly – you don’t need me anymore.

subject: Re: cancellation of Parkimon shareholders meeting
body: Call me - I believe I have a better solution that could involve Avi-- Steve

subject: Parkimon / OC
body: Asad, I have taken a bit of time to reflect on your email dated November 24, 2020. First, to be perfectly honest, I am confused by your unwillingness to have a conversation with James Cutler, given his background and continued interest in the company and its products. To date all the investor and debt holder money has been spent for applications outside the U.S. Your background, history and vision for Parkimon has always been geared towards a global application of a parking solution as evidence by where the money has spent. I would ask you to set aside your feelings regarding this opportunity, its potential lack of funding, and simply promote your software to someone who may see this as an opportunity. I simply cannot accept that the past CEO and primary shareholder and now Director of Parkimon, would not explore every possibility for the benefit of all. The fact that you were willing to part with the company for a mere $300K and a promise from Avi to only look at the software, diminishes what you have represented in the past as to the value of Parkimon / OC. What harm is another round of discussions? To your point of wanting a release from everyone prior to giving access to a 3rd party to review the software, lets proceed. Please send me a draft of a version acceptable to you and we will have an attorney review it and then get it out for signature. Even if we decide to purchase the company from you for $126,591 as you have offered, we would still first need to ensure access to the platform to review the software for authenticity and consistency with the videos that demonstrate its capabilities. This situation is frustrating for us all, and we need to move forward in a positive fashion. I know you seem compelled to blame Paul for lack of funding, but money is never without limits for any company. If there is an opportunity to revive the platform, we will all work to find funding for that possibility, but it needs to be defined. Right now, that opportunity is with James Cutler unless you know of someone else either in the in the US or another country that you see has greater potential for our efforts. Asad, I will reach out to you to discuss this email. If you cannot find it within yourself to speak with James in depth about the software, and to release the software to a third party for review (with releases if necessary), then I will have no choice but to begin the foreclosure process at the start of the new year which I had stated that I just don’t want to do. Thank You Steve

subject: RE: Parkimon / OC /.
body:
I have no problem with a "discussion" with James. I made very clear what my objections were. In fact, I gave you and all the investors and Paul the perfect opportunity to pursue whatever deal you want. Buy me out for money already owed me for work I performed, and the company is yours to do what you will. I have told you countless number of times that I am tired of dealing with the lack of support from any of you. I tried to do what is best for the company (and in effect for you all), and all I got was roadblocks.

With regard to dealing overseas, DOT was an opportunity that we followed when the platform was up and running but which did not work out due to the fact that we did not have the funds to bid for and /or manage (for lack of human and monetary resources) such a large undertaking or for that matter any undertaking. You say: “money is never without limits for any company”. I agree, but then what in God’s name were you all expecting from a one-man-band?? Did you all seriously expect that this brown boy would code till dawn and then go out and sell the platform too with his babu bhat ding ding accent??

No one was willing to fund even the minimum amount to keep the platform alive. We had nothing to show, yet I found two buyers. Texpo was willing to fund the reactivation and so was Avi because I believed in the technology and convinced them. Speaking of Avi, you were in on the conversations with Mike Hogan wherein you agreed that we should proceed with the deal with Avi as that was the only way the platform could be up. In fact, it was you who said that Paul would "sit on the sidelines throwing stones" if he was not kept in check. Needless to say, I was shocked by your email to all the investors about how you felt Avi was not a good deal. That came out of left field. I felt I was doing the right thing because none of the investors were even willing to get the platform reactivated and Avi’s was the only deal on the table. Anyway, you agreed, and then you switched. In any event, that is neither here nor there. My position today has nothing to do with Paul, you, or anybody else. I am just tired of the constant roadblocks.

With regard to reactivating the IP, I have said all along that if an interested party wanted to pay for the activation, I would willingly do it. I even said whoever wanted could have their own engineers do it. James, if he is indeed interested in deploying it will need it reactivated before he can deploy it because that is the ONLY way “to review the software for authenticity and consistency with the videos that demonstrate its capabilities”. Anyway, if you think that my talking to him will give you all the courage to activate the platform then so be it. Set it up and I will be happy to talk to him.

You all want the code as is. I will be happy to hand it over once everyone has signed off on the waivers. By that I mean each and every one of the investors. Without those, I will not hand it over to anyone for whatever reasons. You can call it audit, you can call it review, assessment, evaluation or whatever. I reiterate. I have a fiduciary duty to protect the code and am not obliged, legally or otherwise to hand over the company’s code /IP to anyone. Period. At least as long as I am in the company OR get the waiver.

Finally, with regard to your lien, it is for Orangecone not Parkimon and all you have to do is make a formal demand and I will turn it over to you in satisfaction of your lien. You don’t need to bother with a foreclosure. Despite what you all may think, I am not trying to be difficult. I am just tired of all this.

Asad

subject: Parkimon / OC
body:
Asad, 
 I have taken a bit of time to reflect on your email dated November 24, 2020. 
First, to be perfectly honest, I am confused by your unwillingness to have a conversation with James 
Cutler, given his background and continued interest in the company and its products.  To date all the 
investor and debt holder money has been spent for applications outside the U.S.  Your background, 
history and vision for Parkimon has always been geared towards a global application of a parking 
solution as evidence by where the money has spent.  
I would ask you to set aside your feelings regarding this opportunity, its potential lack of funding, and 
simply promote your software to someone who may see this as an opportunity.  I simply cannot accept 
that the past CEO and primary shareholder and now Director of Parkimon, would not explore every 
possibility for the benefit of all.  The fact that you were willing to part with the company for a mere 
$300K and a promise from Avi to only look at the software, diminishes what you have represented in the 
past as to the value of Parkimon / OC.  What harm is another round of discussions? 
To your point of wanting a release from everyone prior to giving access to a 3rd party to review the 
software, lets proceed.  Please send me a draft of a version acceptable to you and we will have an 
attorney review it and then get it out for signature.  Even if we decide to purchase the company from 
you for $126,591 as you have offered, we would still first need to ensure access to the platform to 
review the software for authenticity and consistency with the videos that demonstrate its capabilities. 
This situation is frustrating for us all, and we need to move forward in a positive fashion.  I know you 
seem compelled to blame Paul for lack of funding, but money is never without limits for any 
company.  If there is an opportunity to revive the platform, we will all work to find funding for that 
possibility, but it needs to be defined.  Right now, that opportunity is with James Cutler unless you know 
of someone else either in the in the US or another country that you see has greater potential for our 
efforts. 
Asad, I will reach out to you to discuss this email.  If you cannot find it within yourself to speak with 
James in depth about the software, and to release the software to a third party for review (with releases 
if necessary), then I will have no choice but to begin the foreclosure process at the start of the new year 
which I had stated that I just don’t want to do.  
Thank You 
 
Steve

subject: RE: DOT RFP- Parkimon
body: Paul, Knowing you, I’m not surprised that since you and your girlfriend’s last month’s attempt to take over the company has failed, you are now resorting to spiteful emails. Given our history together, I lose all perspective and all forms of the tiny bit of intelligence I may have. Cerebral, inter-personal, emotional and everything in between fly’s out the window when I am conversing with you. It’s going to take supreme effort for me to remain professional, but I’ll try and behave. “Try” being the operative word. Allow me to respond to your email inline in red so everyone blind copied can differentiate and keep up. Let’s begin.

subject: DOT RFP- Parkimon
body:
Asad-

I received a copy of the Abu Dhabi RFP from the people at Transpogroup last week. Again, they contacted me. How they got my contact information I do not know. Hallelujah! It’s a miracle that EVERYONE bidding for the DOT RFP including Bala, Transpogroup, Civicsmart and all the rest are homing in on you. You don’t have eyes at the back of your head but trust me, you’re like a firefly --a beacon.
After having read the RFP, now, I can understand why you are so angry with “those assholes” at the DOT: They played you---plain and simple. They had no intention of granting you control of such a large scale project---even though they might have led you to believe otherwise. Regarding your comment about “making more money than God” if I had just found a way to raise 2.7M: Complete horseshit! 2.7M is a drop in the bucket relative to what is needed to have won this thing. They were not playing us. We were going to be GIVEN the contract -Remember? Not bid for it in an RFP!
Thanks to the brilliance of your sidekick Raj, we lost that opportunity (read on) and thanks to your brilliance we lost the Bin Dasmal Group, who were going to foot the local cost and infrastructure. So, your numbers do not apply and are what you say they are. Complete horseshit! Besides you haven’t even put them in the right context. Learn to comprehend what you read rather than just copy paste.

Let me remind you of the facts: YOU are the CEO. YOU are the sole board member, and YOU, alone, made the decision to travel to the UAE on what has turned out to be a gambit. And if/when this venture fails, which is likely, now, you will need take responsibility, as the company’s CEO. That’s what leaders do. But you won’t. Instead you’ll cast about and apportion blame anywhere and everywhere you can because you are simply incapable of taking responsibility for your decision(s). It’s part of your pathos which has been laid bare and, sadly, witnessed by so many over the last few months. My mistake---and I do take responsibility---was in having entrusted my investors’ money to an unsophisticated, narcistic engineer, without any real-world business skills---with no board and no advisors---with whom I might have been able to temper his delusional and highly risky proclivities had I insisted on bringing in someone like Strickland and/or Roth to begin with, and put board in place, to protect the investors from your poor judgement.

Before I respond to the above paragraph (written by Raj for you to copy from), Please ask him to let go off the dictionary and stop sending you emails from which to copy/ paste from (like he’s been doing for so long). [Attention everyone reading this, the yellow highlighted portions of this email are by Master Raj Kohli’s who has a proclivity for drafting emails for Paul to cut /paste from. I have several examples including an email from the author of these proclivities admitting to such proclivities]

Moving on. The sum of your above statements and of hamlet, lear, oedipus and all those guys and now you Paul, is "I wish I had known this some time ago.” If that flew over the cuckoo’s nest, it means: It’s easy to be wise and smart when you have hindsight. (you may not get this now, but hopefully you will by the end of this email]

You’re right! I don’t have any real-world business skills – this narcistic engineer can only show that I grew a former company from 1 to 68, had two successful exits and was raking in more money in a month than you will ever see in a lifetime. Granted I lost all but it was because our tech had become a monopoly. (Some would argue that becoming a monopoly is doing your job too well, but some would say otherwise.) Regardless, getting kicked out by a Central Bank for becoming a monopoly only validates Steve Roth’s point – DO NOT work with governments…

Paul, the only difference between my past achievements and lack of “ real-world business skills” in Parkimon is that I always had a team - not a back stabber, liar and a black mailer who together with his little sidekick kept me busy damage controlling his brilliant “plans”.

Now let’s talk about being “delusional”. Companies who have one tenth of what we have in tech like Passport Inc, Spothero, Parkwhiz, etc. have gone out and raised literally hundreds of millions of dollars and have thousands of employees. With Parkimon I had to become your bitch for the last 3 years. I assigned my IP that had cost millions of $$$ to Parkimon and poured three years of my life into this project, I didn’t have much choice. You had me backed up against the wall. I had to complete the technology any which way. - Even drip-feeding funds (like you did) was better than nothing at the time – or so I thought. You on the other hand have had 57 years of practice at being one and should know that no dog can hump all night and until dawn (with the few engineers left and at India time), and then expected to go down to Santa Cruz, knock on thousands of doors, get them on board Orangecone and then go to Bakersfield, Pasadena, New Jersey, Sacramento and Oakland, Abu Dhabi and Dubai to generate revenues ALL BY HIMSELF. If you think a one-man-band/dog can do that, then YOU are the one who is delusional.

HAD YOU INSISTED ON A BOARD? That’s a laugh! Can you stop lying for God’s sake? I have dozens of emails and text messages to you to get me a TEAM, a BOARD AND a CEO. Emails that go back two years! BTW, what the hell would a Board done without a team and infrastructure? Direct me to change your diapers every day?

Paul, I do not need to cast about and apportion blame; my apologies for calling you a moron so many times. Actually, I’m the moron to have believed in you and trusted you and take full responsibility for succumbing to your conspiracies, blackmail and threats.

Going forward:

a. I take full responsibility for trusting you and therefore the lack of oversight and control of the bank account which allowed you to indulge yourself at the cost of engineers and jeopardise the project.

b. I take full responsibility for blindly trusting you while you waited for me to get out of San Francisco and then embezzle funds for your merry travels to Los Angeles – again at the cost of the engineers.

c. I take full responsibility for believing in you every time you said, “tell the engineers to wait a few months for their salaries as this startup will not fail because of lack of funds” and then NOT paying them so that they would eventually quit. (All the while you paid yourself). We are a tech company and needed engineers. What did you think we were doing Polly? Baking crackers for you?

d. I’m an idiot and I take full responsibility for allowing my friendship with you, curb me from telling the investors (your friends) how you conspired with Raj Kohli to withhold funds that caused Upwork to shut us down many times and lose 64 engineers.

e. I take full responsibility for not hounding you every day but only a few times a month about building a team, a board and allocating just the 10K needed to hire Chris Maresca as the CEO. (who we were courting for the job)

And more recently:

f. I am a moron and take full responsibility for grasping at straws and not realising that you and your girlfriend were playing me and conspired to get me to resign and hand over total control to you and her.

g. For not seeing through your tactics of using Strickland and Steve Roth to give yourself legitimacy and wheel me in.

h. Although all your actions and inactions clearly prove malafide intent and I believe I have enough evidence (that you only provided), I do however, take responsibility for trusting you to do the right thing – yet again, just because you and your girlfriend promised to take the company out of the hole (and then reneged).

I can go on and on but will save the rest for the investor update.

I hold myself responsible for my own naivete’ - had I had more experience we would not be in the position we are, now. In short, we would have never gone overseas with the company capital to chase a pipe dream. It simply wouldn’t have happened.

Okay boy, let’s talk about company capital and it “going overseas”:

A. That “pipe dream” was pretty real until Master Raj Kohli, (CPA extraordinaire) gave me the numbers to propose to the DOT brass – numbers that were only 4.2 BILLION AED/Month off! In a recent conversation with a senior DOT executive, I was informed that the misquote of 4.2Billion had triggered a red flag. To cut a long story short, Parkimon was NOT to be trusted as our “intent” in giving a low quote (courtesy Raj) was so WE could pocket 4 Billion a month out of the 4.2 ( because we had inside knowledge of the efficiency of our enforcement module and the tons of citations we would be issuing and raking in.)

@rkohli461@yahoo.com . Thank you Raj Kohli for all your “help” you certainly deserve the 8% equity you were pining and willing to sue Parkimon for. (BTW where did you get your degree from? – Here’s a link to get you back on track https://youtu.be/PYL4nyE7zeo )

B. Now Paul, let’s analyse your great “concern” for the company capital. The total cost of the Abu Dhabi DOT pilot including travel, food and lodging was not more than $9750. (My friends hosted both Paul and me while we were in UAE) While you have personally indulged in 16X times that in salaries and other benefits that you gave yourself while you controlled the bank account.

C. Your “show” of concern for company capital is pathetic given the fact that EVERY person including yourself that you have brought to the table (other than Steve Roth and the investors), has cost the company thousands of dollars with ZERO results. Laxmi, Michael Calana, Jim Leahy, Lura Flyn, Raj Kohli, your sister are just a few of YOUR associates that had to be paid from Parkimon’s accounts for doing absolutely NOTHING. Every time I refused, YOU, together with Raj Kohli would resort to the time and tested formula of withholding funds and blackmailing me. I can show 13 such instances just in ONE year alone!

D. Regarding Abu Dhabi and DOT, you seem to have missed out on the development of Speedchalk, (our enforcement module) that was borne out of the DOT adventure. A module that Passport Inc. had to pay millions of dollars to acquire through Nupark. A module that completes our platform.

E. As the only fundraiser, I was dependent upon YOU. Your blackmail and conspiracies with Raj, has cost the company literally hundreds of thousands of dollars. I had assigned my payments IP and code to Parkimon and what could have been developed in less than $400K and with less than 15 engineers cost the company over a million and 64 full stack engineers!

F. Since you were not willing to provide the funds for building a team or even a business development hire, and proclaimed yourself the VP Biz Dev of Parkimon to everyone AND on Linkedin. What business did you develop Mr. VP Biz Dev???? BART, Sacramento, Palo Alto, Oakland, Las Vegas are just a few of the opportunities YOU could not close because other than bullshitting you have no skills whatsoever to follow-up OR close. You could not even close Parkingsense (who needed you) and Barnacle (your buddy) dare I say more.

G. My job was to build the tech and deliver that. I did that and much more! The next step was to build a team and find a CEO to take the company to the next level. Both required funding. YOUR JOB! Did YOU deliver? So do NOT give me your holy concern for company capital!

At this point none of the investors will risk anymore capital with you, and Pinetti and Jennifer Ross intend to exert their rights--unless you can find the capital to pay them off or find some compelling reason for them to forebear (read on). That said, they are not going to convert their debt to equity.

“exert their rights”?? – See my response to that later in this email (read on). So, Steve and Jennifer are not going to convert their debt to equity. Okay, fine, they don’t have to do anything. The way the universe works is that everyone has options and choices to do or not do something (not doing is also “an action” - in a way.) Your choice then manifests into a consequence (good or bad) that you alone are responsible for. If that is what they want to do, then fine. I sincerely hope that the company can reach a stage where it can pay off its debt to these fine people who were misled by you but still supported us.

So allow me to provide you with a potential solution, compliments of a CEO who owns more advanced degrees than you, AND, more importantly, has practical and applied business acumen with C-Suite experience having worked for two Fortune 100 companies, during which he ran his product lines into the BILLIONS with a “B.” This is what he said about your foray into Abu Dhabi after I sent him a copy of the RFP and what you might be able to do at this point---given the fact that you wrote a portion of the RFP and appear to have a window of opportunity to salvage something from this mis-guided adventure.

“This is not project for a start-up at your stage. In my opinion this is a $40 to 50M+ project over 4+ years for a big gov integrator, with huge multi-disciplinary expertise requiring 100+persons in my opinion. It asks also the contractor to take huge liabilities and responsibilities.

I hope you didn’t have to pay the CEO who owns more advanced degrees than me for all of this “intelligence and insight”? Cause, if you go through all the emails I and Steve Roth exchanged over and over and over with you and Sally – just a few weeks ago, you’ll find the same “insight”. (Steve didn’t even need a copy of the RFP to figure the above out)

If IBM comes to me and ask to help on some part, I would ask them to license a package from me for 0.5 to 1 or 2 million and buy it on the spot with a support contract. That is the only deal I would engage on the topic… they say yes or no, but I would not spend any time writing, presenting, defending and producing many iterative works on this. IBM can spend all the time they want to just prepare a response. This means many months of effort with uncertain outcome. But a start-up cannot…
-
Have you looked at the payment milestone”? how it is itemized? There is no way a start-up can survive in such heavy and lengthy project/process.

I do not know all the details about what happened before this RFP, but if I were you I think I would try the following idea:

1- gather testimonial (formal or informal) from the city officials, Dot, + MSFT to be ready to share with the prime bidder (more below)
2- ask Asad to produce a summary list of checkmarks showing how well you meet the RFP requirements
3- meet the prime bidder, share with them #1 and #2 above and ask the following:
- $200K in advance from them to support the bidding process with this amount being either refundable after bid is won (deducted from the price you will offer in the bid) or converted into an investment in Parkimon
- give them the right to sell/distribute a skinnier version of your solution right away to other cities
4- if they say no, tell them you are starving and cannot support them in this bidding process

--> for them, bidding alone will cost several $100K, and huge liability to accept as per this RFP requirements
--> winning probably becomes very low without you
--> cost and risk of implementation is very high if they win without you (will have to develop core solution from scratch)
--> you bring them other biz opportunities that can take off faster than this bid
--> the risk of losing their $ with you is small and will not kill them.. they can still sell your solution to other cities.”

So there it is: That’s the way out---the only way out to get any sort of return for the investors who put you there. There’s 15 months between the time this is going to be awarded and ultimately, implemented, which is plenty of time to get your mothballed platform up and running with your engineers. TranspoGroup is going to contact me tomorrow and let me know if someone has been selected or if, as you allege, it will continue to be postponed. (If it is, great---you can go to each of the likely top three bidders and explain WHY they need you, and leverage yourself, that way.) Your clearly have a chance to be on the winning team, so long as you can put your ego aside.

I’m all for your “potential solution”. However, I wasn’t paid for the last 18 months and had to get a job and as such don’t have the bandwidth to do what you can so easily do. (bullshit people). Also, everyone bidding for the DOT RFP have somehow miraculously found you. Interesting… So how about you do what you have proposed above (courtesy of the CEO who owns more advanced degrees than me), and I’ll help you with whatever is needed by them. After all, you have claimed (in writing to James) that YOU have a fiduciary duty to the investors. So, go for it – reach out to the DOT bidders and I will support you 100%.

You may be correct in the near term---this thing may be postponed---but ultimately someone will make the bid and endeavour to engineer what you and your engineers have already built and assume the risk---and all they need is time and money---which companies like Siemens and IBM have in spades. Hence. you have a window to make something happen and you need to make the most of it, otherwise you are going to have to contend with Pinetti and Jennifer which may get messy.

“Messy? Exert their rights?” Seems like you are threatening legal action. So, let me set the record straight.

a. All the loans that you got for the company were based on YOUR misrepresentation of the true facts to the lenders. (as I was not a party to those conversations and dealings.) You lied to your friends about the “imminent” Abu Dhabi DOT contract and are still stuck in Abu Dhabi -as is evident from your “potential solution” above.

b. All the loans were above the lawful usury limits which exposes the lender to criminal liability. Ignorance of the law is not a defence and loan sharking is a criminal offence that gets the lender 5 years minimum in the slammer. (Heck you didn’t even spare your own mother in this).

c. My total personal assets these days is the equity in my car which I used to drive for Uber until I got a job. It’s got over 168,000 miles. Definitely not worth $400K, I can assure you. Last I heard, lawsuits cost money and one second-hand vehicle divided by 38 investors is not going to get them much. I can assure you of that too.

d. Suing an insolvent company is stupid.

e. Suing me makes them liable to the rest of the investors. BTW, what a brilliant way to get your funds back. Throw me behind bars! Only you Paul could have thought of this one. I bow to your genius. A few do me, the rest do them, I do you and everyone does Raj for screwing up the DOT deal? Brilliant! We’ll call ourselves the Parkimon Chain Gang.

A word of advice boy. Don’t ever threaten me again. Just do what you have to. I’m sure we’ll ALL look good in orange or stripes. So, bring it on. In fact, go pump all your friends and let’s do a class action and everyone gets a piece of my car. You get dibs on the exhaust pipe as you’ll need that to pry your head out of your exhaust.

The bottom line is this: You are not going to be the parade---but you may have an opportunity to be part of it, the trombone player---if you will---and if you can get 500k, or 1m or 2m for your time and trouble and can use that to pay off the company’s debt and keep the other investors at bay, and restart the company with “your M&A guy,” you know, the guy I wanted to involve 6 months ago---so be it. If, however you have any better ideas please let me know what they are as Pinetti is awaiting your response.

You say that none of the investors are willing to invest in me? Fine. How about they invest in YOU and the “guy YOU wanted to involve 6 months ago” but didn’t because you were not willing to pay him. Mr. Steve Roth. Here is what I will propose to all investors next week. (Provided I can convince Mr. Roth to come back after you and Sally ghosted him because he was being pragmatic and talking sense about NOT going after DOT– unlike you two who were insisting that we do).
Raise just enough funds to take on Mr. Roth and let him do what he does best. Get Parkimon up to speed for VCs. As we get close to that and feel comfortable, the investors can convert their C-Notes and debt (as desired by VCs and suggested by him) to preferred shares and we go to the VCs. (NO VC is going to give new money to wash out old debt- YOU know it, I know it, anyone with half a brain knows it…oops, let me retract YOU from this last statement)
You don’t have to pay me anything. I will not take any salary. You can open your own Parkimon account and disburse through that. However, we will need to get the mothballed technology off the shelf and revive it, and as such will need to hire a few engineers. A total of approximately 25~30K a month (inclusive of Mr. Roth) should do it. That’s less than 1000 bucks per investor per month. (Here’s a bonus – thanks to your plans) – Since we lost all the engineers, it will take a few months before I can find new engineers and get them comfortable with the code where they can revive the tech- so their salary is not required for the next few months anyways – just Mr. Roth’s. That’s just $10K/Mth. The alternative is to dissolve the company, and everybody writes off their investment.

Moving forward, for those folks who are reading this and are wondering why Polly and Raj conspired to blackmail me by withholding funds for the project, please allow me to explain.

Master Raj Kohli, the learned CPA extraordinaire performed “Quality Control” on our proposal to DOT. He found 4 minor typos, offered 36 alternative word suggestions and fixed 46 or thereabout commas and periods that I had missed (out of 16641 words in the DOT proposal). For that he wanted 8% equity. After embarrassing me in front of the DOT senior brass in Abu Dhabi where our quote (provided by him) was only a mere 4 Billion with a B off, I did not think he deserved any equity. (He had also reneged on his promise to find more investors). Anyway, when I refused to give him equity, he then started tutoring AND threatening Paul.

Following are a few excerpts from his communication with Paul (which are self-explanatory):

“I will only deal with you, and you have promised me my six percent is in tact, so that works for me. As YOUR FRIEND, I beseech you to protect yourself and your kids by withholding further funds

I know this must be stressful, but keep in mind YOU control his cash flow, and you MUST seize this moment

If Asad does not grant me 3% immediately I will be left with no choice but to take legal action, and I will. This would probably have to be disclosed to our investors

If Asad refuses to fully vest me with 3% immediately, per our call this afternoon, you agree to give me 15% of your 20% fully vested interest in Parkimon effective immediately, which will ensure I have a fully vested 3% equity interest in Parkimon now as consideration for my (unpaid) work this past year.

Following is an excerpt from Paul’s response to him. (I have many more but just one is enough)

“I am going to drive things for the next 30-45 days and he is going to do things MY way, or he will get no more funds. I have a plan. Don’t send any emails to ANYONE, except me, and let me manage him”

This “plan” of withholding funds and threats continued every time Paul would want someone to get paid, including himself (even at the cost of the engineers)

Well Paul, I succumbed to your blackmail instances and became your bitch because I had to complete the technology somehow. What goes around comes around. Should be easy for you since you’ve had years of practice at being one.

So, this is how it’s going to go down: YOU have 4 options.

(i)
YOU do what you do best, sell your story and our tech to the potential bidders of DOT

(ii)
YOU raise the minimum to get Mr. Roth onboard and taking us to VCs

(iii)
You give your shares back and I find a buyer that puts in enough to pay off all the investors. Look at it this way: You’ve already pillaged Parkimon’s account for “raising capital”, plus the investors are YOUR friends and family, plus your shenanigans have cost the company millions and I’ll be justified in diluting your sorry ass down to less than half a percent anyways before I do anything. (you are welcome to sue – but that doesn’t get the investors their money back). Also, I gave you two chances to do right by your investors and you walked off with no thought of your friends and family. I’m giving you this 3rd chance to do right by them. Prove to them, that you care for anybody besides yourself.

(iv)
IF YOU don’t want to do any of the above, then we dissolve the company, and everyone can write off their investments. I’ll send over the above 4 points to all the investors and seek their guidance if I don’t hear from you by the end of this week.

Before I forget. About your email to Raj where you comfort him with: “ I would have done bodily harm to the guy---or paid someone else to do it...That's just the Sicilian in me...”

Don Paolo since you don’t have the crackers to take me on yourself and prefer to pay someone to do me bodily harm, let me save you some money. You don’t have to hire a hitman for this brown boy. Just find yourself a heroin addict, show him where I live and tell him you’ll get him a FIX after he does a good job. The best part. He’ll have forgotten your face after the FIX. Do give him something beforehand to gain his trust though, because I know you’ll renege later. Capisce Don Paolo? BTW, I always heard that with Italians, family comes first. Disrespecting and disparaging your own sisters and their kids to an outsider? No wonder your own family doesn’t think much of you. They know the real you -a storyteller and a looser. Paul, your mother named you well! You are small. Two timing your girlfriend, no compassion, no care, selfish to the core. You disgust me!

(in case you folks are wondering how I have access to his emails. He was brilliant enough to use the pgc@parkimon.com email address to sign up on Match.com. He only had 100MB allocated for his mailbox and given the hot stud that he is, was getting tons of “matches” from Match.com. Pretty soon his mailbox overloaded, and his emails started bouncing. As the “catch all and admin” of the parkimon domain, all bounced emails come to me.) [In case the concerned party needs evidence of him two timing her, please feel free to reach out. I have tons of cute messages from casanova with dates]

In conclusion Paul, I’ve copied this email to some of the “sophisticated” investors in our group and will wait for their response before I send the investor update to all. Although they are all your friends and can get duped by your stories- again when they question you, I’m not discounting their own brains to analyse your BS and see the truth for themselves. Also, if you all don’t believe me, I would like to invite everyone copied to reach out and ask for any document or emails or anything that demonstrates how irresponsible, stupid and delusional I alone have been.

There you go boy. I hope everyone has the remarkable hindsight you are gifted with. Anyway, I told you upfront – I lose all forms of intelligence when conversing with you and have given you enough ammo to go to your legal eagles and come get me. The exhaust pipe awaits!
God bless

-A

P-

subject: RE: RE: RE: two other options for parkimon
body: Finally. The voice of reason. Please see my response inline in red.

subject: Re: two other options for parkimon
body:
Asad,

Thank you for continuing to work for solutions.

While your Option #1 looks like a sale possibility on the surface, I believe your plan, as advised by Steven Roth, is for a VC or large investor play which does NOT result is an immediate liquidation and payback for the debt holders or angels, and will continue to carry risk of possible dilution to the investors down the road - please correct me if I am wrong. While I don’t think the investors will buy into this, perhaps it is worth discussing the particulars in more detail. To your first point, you are correct that in that route there won’t be any immediate liquidation and payback for the debt holders. However, the VC’s will bring a lot of value to the table including a team or provide enough funds to hire a stellar team that can actually sell and not play at being VP Biz Dev. As such it is worth exploring too, even in parallel.

None of the investors have shares and their C-Notes will convert to shares at the time of evaluation and as such dilution is not something that they need to worry about or will happen at the A-Series stage.

The acquisition is an opportunity that has come up recently. Please explain the following to the delusional character by your side:
A. For an M&A to happen, I don’t need any permission from any investor or even a minority shareholder. An M&A forces the sale of all the shares, including Paul’s. Only the board and a majority of the stockholders need to approve the merger. Guess who that is. Paul’s or the investors vote is NOT needed.

B. Paul seems to be floating in an alternate reality. Please sit him down in a corner and explain these simple facts to him again: An investor is not going to buy c-notes and pay of anyone’s debt FIRST. Why? SIMPLY BECAUSE if he does that, he is not owning the company but taking on debt. Why on earth would someone do that? An investor can only own a company if he buys shares. In case of a sale, the current investors in Parkimon should convert their c-notes to shares- so they can benefit from a sale when it happens. Don’t the note holders want to get paid back? – otherwise they’ll just have a convertible promissory note that just sits there with no chance of liquidity. It’s actually as simple as that, but since its coming from me, he thinks I have some hidden agenda for asking the investors to convert. A simple email or call to the honorable Madam Judy Esq. I retract that- Even a Google search, IF he could think of the right question to ask would have cleared things up in his convoluted mind. Now, if you can get this extremely simple phenomenon across to genius there, I’ll be forever in awe of you.
C. If I wanted to (i) I can very easily dilute him by issuing more shares and (ii) forming a newco and transferring ALL assets, IP and the debt to the new company. It would be an asset sale in which the newco assumes the debt too. This would leave Paul in the dust holding his 20% in a dissolved company, while I sell the newco to an Arab who takes the tech to the UAE. I might be opening the newco to a lawsuit from him, but do I care? NO. For one, it would be a test of his friendship with his investors (both ways). As they would be getting their money back and second, I would love for Paul to go and sue an Arab in an Arab country.

D. In case you missed out on Paul’s response to my two options. Here they are: “You have your "MIddle-Eastern Investor" pay off Pinetti, Ross, and Thomas, with interest, and everyone will "come along for the ride." See # B above, it’s not happening. Also, in case of an M&A, I don’t need everyone to come along for the ride. Besides, once the Arab sends a term sheet to WSGR and the investors see that is the ONLY way they are going to get their money back, they’ll come along for any ride. Trust me. Either way, I firmly believe that the technology should be sold which will happen in a foreclosure proceeding anyway. “Option 2: No point. The investors will own the IP when the lenders foreclose and the excess, if any, will be distributed to all, including WSGR, in a trustee sale.” It’s amazing how this man cannot think through even his own one thought. No wonder he is where he is, living off others. What are the lenders owning? foreclosing? and what’s excess? Is this a piece of real estate that they are foreclosing on? Who’s going to tell, evaluate and demonstrate to a potential buyer what the source code is all about and does? While a team of techs may be able to figure out the source code in a year or so, who’s going to figure out my encrypted algorithms that are in there? Do you think I’m going to be obligated to help Master Camera sell it to the Abu Dhabi bidders? Does he seriously believe that? Don’t you think I’ll want the cost of the IP I assigned to Parkimon back? IP that cost millions. Do you think someone will be willing to pay millions of dollars for not even understanding what they are buying? Do you think, I’ll let Polly’s buddy buy it for chump change without filing a legal claim and dragging it in courts till death do us part? Once the company is dissolved, I am not obligated to anyone or help anyone. Get that into his delusional head somehow. Startups fold and seed investors write off their debts. That’s how it is. Suing me is not going to get them anything anyways.

Your Option #2 is not appealing as YOU are integral to the technology. For the business plan to work, you must stay involved in a lead technical capacity. Can someone please explain that to him too. First it was Mary who was his best friend, then Steve, now its you. He thinks that by pissing me off, he can serve himself and the investors.

I would ask you to reconsider the proposal we sent you 8/30/2019. While perhaps it needs to be sured up, the basic tenets allow for a new management team with you remaining as CTO, your shares in tact, a salary for you and your engineers, and perhaps new funding and direction. Your efforts in Abu Dhabi have gained your technology worldwide recognition which can be parlayed in a new direction in the US, in addition to pursuing some subcontractor or strategic alliances with the Abu Dhabi DOT RFP participants. With this plan, I do believe that the debt holders may very well convert to preferred share holders, which will allow for larger investors to come into play. Through your very limited time spent with Edward, he has counseled us on some potential areas of opportunity which he feels may be greater sources of success than have currently been looked at. We have also connected with some potential seasoned CEO candidates whom could help us moving forward. Working together, perhaps we can find a solution.

Working together we can certainly find a solution. However, I have my doubts when it comes to Paul. I prefer not to communicate with him. Going forward, I am open to revisiting your 8/30/19 proposal – especially if you can bring in a seasoned CEO and if I am provided tangible assurance from 3rd parties of the tenets – Those will need to be discussed. Let me know next steps?
Best
Asad

Sally

Ms. Sally Thornber
(925) 708-3557
Lure Fine Real Estate
Broker/Owner
DRE# 01110608
sally@lurefinerealestate.com

subject: Fwd: R..E: Parkimon Re-organization Proposal
body:
Asad,

I am sorry things continue to deteriorate on the Parkimon front.

Just to clarify my position, I relied on the email from you to Paul and myself dated 8/31/2019 (below) from which you proposed an agreement which was my basis for all future negotiation. Indeed, you represented that you would step down as CEO which I still feel is in the best interest of all involved, yet unlikely to happen. The strategy was in fact recommended by Mary Wong and other investors whom several were willing to up their investment if there were new management to control the direction of the company. Never did I think this would be Paul or myself, but someone with experience working out of situations like yours such as Steven Roth.

In subsequent emails and upon our meeting in Pleasant Hill you reneged from your agreement dated 8/31, which is in your sole discretion, but significantly impacted my ultimate decision to invest as well.

Like Paul, I see an opportunity to capitalize on the Abu Dhabi RFP in some fashion, but again based on the tone of your responses you are unlikely to cooperate. You have an opportunity to partner of sell Parkimon technology to a company positioned to execute on the Abu Dhabi RFP. Based on the conversations I have been privy to, this may be your best opportunity to exit and make your investors whole or use the capital to restart the company.

Also, I did see the importance of having an independent valuation of Parkimon for the investors and was willing to pay for it, which I have done. It is simply good business sense and something the investors are awaiting. Unfortunately without a deep dive into the technology the report will be reflective of limited input, thereby minimizing its value.

Your tactics to discredit Paul only serve to hamper your ability to get support of the investors going forward - despite your continued efforts, they will not turn on him. Hate begets hate and it is doing nothing to move the company forward.

In the end it makes no difference, Parkimon will survive or fail and we will all continue on. I suspect that Steve Pinetti and Jennifer Ross will be taking legal action to exercise their rights unless a change of direction is offered imminently.

Peace,

Sally

subject: R..E: Parkimon Re-organization Proposal
body: Paul, Like your fearless leader with the orange hair, you seem to be living in a bubble - dissociated with reality, and it’s all about “YOU”. Allow me to explain “your” proposal from MY angle. (see if you can put yourself in my shoes and then forgive my unprofessionalism. -- I try hard to avoid when conversing with you.) 1. You got me to build a product. 2. I more than delivered, as you now have 3 products to sell. 3. However, you did not give me the tools (monetary and human resources) to sell the products but expected me to deliver revenues. – From where I sit, you did not deliver as per ..and I quote: “this company will not fail for lack of funds!!” 4. Since I did not have the resources, I could not sell. You have therefore concluded and now truly believe that I am inept and not capable of taking the company forward (in this you are correct) 5. As per your term #4, The board can at any time dilute me to less than 1% by issuing more stock. 6. “The BOD shall have overall voting power in lieu of majority stock voting control.” So I can be put out to pasture at the whim of the honorable Board any time? 7. As per your term #1, The BOD can sell the IP at whatever price and I won’t have any say in the matter. For instance, one fine day they can decide to justget every investor’s investment back ($1.6M) and leave me in the dust. 8. “As incentive, you will be paid a salary of $6,000 per month” So now you want me to continue slaving for you for peanuts while you neuter me some more and with no way to protect MY interests. What am I? your boy? 9. “a one-time reimbursement of $78,000 will be paid to you within ninety (90) days” I believe I mentioned to you several times that I have been taking loans to pay off my loans. I do not have 90 days, or even 30. If I get confirmed for a job, I will be able to go to a bank and get another loan- that I would like to avoid. So, 90 days is not an option. Paul, I can go on and on and on and conversing with you is getting tiresome. If I didn’t know you any better, I would have thought that you had planned this “takeover bid” all along. But chill, you are not capable of thinking that far ahead. Let me make this easy for you and let me give you a proposal that can actually get you what you want. Total control PLUS all the things you want to do as per your proposal: (The following terms are non-negotiable.) 1. I will not tell James to dissolve the company on Monday. Instead I will wait till Wednesday to hear your thoughts. 2. I will resign and hand over the company to you. 3. I will not come on the BOD (as I won’t have any say anyways, plus I don’t want any liability going forward with you running the show.) 4. You will wire $78K to Bela’s account within 48 hours of receiving my resignation. Not 7, 30, 60 or 90 days. 5. I will keep 49% of shares in the company with an anti-dilution clause for 70% of the 49% (to recoup the money I spent on my payments IP that has been assigned to Parkimon plus the opportunity cost and lost, the hours and time, the emotional and physical BS that I had to bear plus a whole lot more) 6. You can then hire me as an independent technical consultant on a monthly retainer of $20K (salary for engineers and moi) plus a “delivery” bonus in case of an acquisition, licensing or whatever to “incentivize” me. 7. As the consultant, I will help Strickland do what he does and help in the sales, fund raising, etc. Rest assured you will have my full support as I know Parkimon has excellent technology and can do very well under the rightmanagement, plus I want to recoup my time and efforts. 8. You will make WSGR’s dues as a priority and will pay half of their dues upon my resignation. There you have it, let me know if this works for you – as it should, and I’ll stay away from WSGR for another couple of days. Best A PS: BTW, I don’t know who drafted the c-note that you attached but the interest is above usury limits, meaning that the investors probably won’t be able to enforce such a high interest rate… PPS: Your clause -C: “20% of loan value in stock options iii) 2X return on investment upon acquisition or merger iv) in the event of continuing on, a initial valuation share discount of 30% plus interest accrued” Did you come up with this brilliant clause all by yourself or do you have an advisor? If the later, you need to get another advisor. If the former, then please stop thinking- It’s in your best interest. (With this clause, I doubt if any VC will touch you with a 100 foot pole!!)

subject: Parkimon Re-organization Proposal
body:
Asad,

Attached is a proposal for your review that will allow Parkimon to continue as a going concern while a new strategy and management expertise is put in place to maximum our success.

Please take some time to reflect on this as many hours have gone into finding a solution in this critical time that is upon us.

Best-

P-

subject: 2RE: two other options for parkimon
body:
Sally, Paul
I have to inform the investors of the situation and the current options on the table. I’d like to do this
Tuesday. Please let me know if what you wrote in your email below is still on the cards and if I should
add it as an option?
I hope you can understand that I need to wrap this up and move on.
Best
Asad

subject: Re: two other options for parkimon
body:
Asad,

Thank you for continuing to work for solutions.

While your Option #1 looks like a sale possibility on the surface, I believe your plan, as advised by Steven Roth, is for a VC or large investor play which does NOT result is an immediate liquidation and payback for the debt holders or angels, and will continue to carry risk of possible dilution to the investors down the road - please correct me if I am wrong. While I don’t think the investors will buy into this, perhaps it is worth discussing the particulars in more detail.

Your Option #2 is not appealing as YOU are integral to the technology. For the business plan to work, you must stay involved in a lead technical capacity.

I would ask you to reconsider the proposal we sent you 8/30/2019. While perhaps it needs to be sured up, the basic tenets allow for a new management team with you remaining as CTO, your shares in tact, a salary for you and your engineers, and perhaps new funding and direction. Your efforts in Abu Dhabi have gained your technology worldwide recognition which can be parlayed in a new direction in the US, in addition to pursuing some subcontractor or strategic alliances with the Abu Dhabi DOT RFP participants. With this plan, I do believe that the debt holders may very well convert to preferred share holders, which will allow for larger investors to come into play.

Through your very limited time spent with Edward, he has counseled us on some potential areas of opportunity which he feels may be greater sources of success than have currently been looked at. We have also connected with some potential seasoned CEO candidates whom could help us moving forward. Working together, perhaps we can find a solution.

Sally

sally@lurefinerealestate.com
